2_1_5.NKJV: All those who were descendants of Jacob were seventy persons (for Joseph was in Egypt already).
2_1_5.KJV: And all the souls that came out of the loins of Jacob were seventy souls: for Joseph was in Egypt already. loins: Heb. thigh
2_1_5.ESV: All the descendants of Jacob were seventy persons; Joseph was already in Egypt.
2_1_5.NASB: All the persons who came from the loins of Jacob were seventy in number, but Joseph was [already] in Egypt.
2_1_5.NLT: In all, Jacob had seventy descendants in Egypt, including Joseph, who was already there.
2_1_5.WEB: All the souls who came out of Jacob’s body were seventy souls, and Joseph was in Egypt already.
2_1_5.CSB17: The total number of Jacob’s descendants was seventy; Joseph was already in Egypt.
2_1_5.AMP: All the descendants of Jacob were seventy people; Joseph was [already] in Egypt.
2_1_5.MSG: Seventy persons in all generated by Jacob's seed. Joseph was already in Egypt.
2_1_5.NIV: The descendants of Jacob numbered seventy in all; Joseph was already in Egypt.
2_1_5.NRSVCE: The total number of people born to Jacob was seventy. Joseph was already in Egypt.

2_1_6.NKJV: And Joseph died, all his brothers, and all that generation.

2_1_6.WEB: Joseph died, as did all his brothers, and all that generation.
2_1_6.CSB17: Joseph and all his brothers and all that generation eventually died.

2_1_6.NIV: Now Joseph and all his brothers and all that generation died,
2_1_6.NRSVCE: Then Joseph died, and all his brothers, and that whole generation.

2_1_7.NKJV: But the children of Israel were fruitful and increased abundantly, multiplied and grew exceedingly mighty; and the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.KJV: And the children of Israel were fruitful, and increased abundantly, and multiplied, and waxed exceeding mighty; and the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.ESV: But the people of Israel were fruitful and increased greatly; they multiplied and grew exceedingly strong, so that the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.NASB: But the sons of Israel were fruitful and increased greatly, and multiplied, and became exceedingly mighty, so that the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.NLT: But their descendants, the Israelites, had many children and grandchildren. In fact, they multiplied so greatly that they became extremely powerful and filled the land.
2_1_7.WEB: The children of Israel were fruitful, and increased abundantly, and multiplied, and grew exceedingly mighty; and the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.CSB17: But the Israelites were fruitful, increased rapidly, multiplied, and became extremely numerous so that the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.AMP: but the Israelites were prolific and increased greatly; they multiplied and became extremely strong, so that the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.MSG: But the children of Israel kept on reproducing. They were very prolific—a population explosion in their own right—and the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.NIV: but the Israelites were fruitful and multiplied greatly and became exceedingly numerous, so that the land was filled with them.
2_1_7.NRSVCE: But the Israelites were fruitful and prolific; they multiplied and grew exceedingly strong, so that the land was filled with them.

2_1_8.NKJV: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
2_1_8.KJV: Now there arose up a new king over Egypt, which knew not Joseph.
2_1_8.ESV: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
2_1_8.NASB: Now a new king arose over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
2_1_8.NLT: Eventually, a new king came to power in Egypt who knew nothing about Joseph or what he had done.
2_1_8.WEB: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who didn’t know Joseph.
2_1_8.CSB17: A new king, who did not know about Joseph, came to power in Egypt.
2_1_8.AMP: Now a new king arose over Egypt, who did not know Joseph [nor the history of his accomplishments].
2_1_8.MSG: A new king came to power in Egypt who didn't know Joseph.
2_1_8.NIV: Then a new king, who did not know about Joseph, came to power in Egypt.
2_1_8.NRSVCE: Now a new king arose over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.

2_1_9.NKJV: And he said to his people, “Look, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we;
2_1_9.KJV: And he said unto his people, Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we:
2_1_9.ESV: And he said to his people, “Behold, the people of Israel are too many and too mighty for us.
2_1_9.NASB: He said to his people, 'Behold, the people of the sons of Israel are more and mightier than we.
2_1_9.NLT: He said to his people, “Look, the people of Israel now outnumber us and are stronger than we are.
2_1_9.WEB: He said to his people, “Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we.
2_1_9.CSB17: He said to his people, “Look, the Israelite people are more numerous and powerful than we are.
2_1_9.AMP: He said to his people, "Behold, the people of the sons of Israel are too many and too mighty for us [they greatly outnumber us].
2_1_9.MSG: He spoke to his people in alarm, "There are way too many of these Israelites for us to handle.
2_1_9.NIV: “Look,” he said to his people, “the Israelites have become much too numerous for us.
2_1_9.NRSVCE: He said to his people, “Look, the Israelite people are more numerous and more powerful than we.

2_1_10.NKJV: come, let us deal shrewdly with them, lest they multiply, and it happen, in the event of war, that they also join our enemies and fight against us, and so go up out of the land.”
2_1_10.KJV: Come on, let us deal wisely with them; lest they multiply, and it come to pass, that, when there falleth out any war, they join also unto our enemies, and fight against us, and so get them up out of the land.
2_1_10.ESV: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them, lest they multiply, and, if war breaks out, they join our enemies and fight against us and escape from the land.”
2_1_10.NASB: 'Come, let us deal wisely with them, or else they will multiply and in the event of war, they will also join themselves to those who hate us, and fight against us and depart from the land.'
2_1_10.NLT: We must make a plan to keep them from growing even more. If we don’t, and if war breaks out, they will join our enemies and fight against us. Then they will escape from the country. ”
2_1_10.WEB: Come, let us deal wisely with them, lest they multiply, and it happen that when any war breaks out, they also join themselves to our enemies, and fight against us, and escape out of the land.”
2_1_10.CSB17: Come, let’s deal shrewdly with them; otherwise they will multiply further, and when war breaks out, they will join our enemies, fight against us, and leave the country.”
2_1_10.AMP: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them, so that they will not multiply and in the event of war, join our enemies, and fight against us and escape from the land."
2_1_10.MSG: We've got to do something: Let's devise a plan to contain them, lest if there's a war they should join our enemies, or just walk off and leave us."
2_1_10.NIV: Come, we must deal shrewdly with them or they will become even more numerous and, if war breaks out, will join our enemies, fight against us and leave the country.”
2_1_10.NRSVCE: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them, or they will increase and, in the event of war, join our enemies and fight against us and escape from the land.”

2_1_11.NKJV: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to afflict them with their burdens. And they built for Pharaoh supply cities, Pithom and Raamses.
2_1_11.KJV: Therefore they did set over them taskmasters to afflict them with their burdens. And they built for Pharaoh treasure cities, Pithom and Raamses.
2_1_11.ESV: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to afflict them with heavy burdens. They built for Pharaoh store cities, Pithom and Raamses.
2_1_11.NASB: So they appointed taskmasters over them to afflict them with hard labor. And they built for Pharaoh storage cities, Pithom and Raamses.
2_1_11.NLT: So the Egyptians made the Israelites their slaves. They appointed brutal slave drivers over them, hoping to wear them down with crushing labor. They forced them to build the cities of Pithom and Rameses as supply centers for the king.
2_1_11.WEB: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to afflict them with their burdens. They built storage cities for Pharaoh: Pithom and Raamses.
2_1_11.CSB17: So the Egyptians assigned taskmasters over the Israelites to oppress them with forced labor. They built Pithom and Rameses as supply cities for Pharaoh.
2_1_11.AMP: So they set taskmasters over them to oppress them with hard labor. And the sons of Israel built Pithom and Raamses as storage cities for Pharaoh.
2_1_11.MSG: So they organized them into work-gangs and put them to hard labor under gang-foremen. They built the storage cities Pithom and Rameses for Pharaoh.
2_1_11.NIV: So they put slave masters over them to oppress them with forced labor, and they built Pithom and Rameses as store cities for Pharaoh.
2_1_11.NRSVCE: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to oppress them with forced labor. They built supply cities, Pithom and Rameses, for Pharaoh.

2_1_12.NKJV: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and grew. And they were in dread of the children of Israel.
2_1_12.KJV: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and grew. And they were grieved because of the children of Israel. But: Heb. And as they afflicted them, so they multiplied, etc
2_1_12.ESV: But the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and the more they spread abroad. And the Egyptians were in dread of the people of Israel.
2_1_12.NASB: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and the more they spread out, so that they were in dread of the sons of Israel.
2_1_12.NLT: But the more the Egyptians oppressed them, the more the Israelites multiplied and spread, and the more alarmed the Egyptians became.
2_1_12.WEB: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and the more they spread out. They were grieved because of the children of Israel.
2_1_12.CSB17: But the more they oppressed them, the more they multiplied and spread so that the Egyptians came to dread the Israelites.
2_1_12.AMP: But the more the Egyptians oppressed them, the more they multiplied and expanded, so that the Egyptians dreaded and were exasperated by the Israelites.
2_1_12.MSG: But the harder the Egyptians worked them the more children the Israelites had—children everywhere! The Egyptians got so they couldn't stand the Israelites
2_1_12.NIV: But the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and spread; so the Egyptians came to dread the Israelites
2_1_12.NRSVCE: But the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and spread, so that the Egyptians came to dread the Israelites.

2_1_13.NKJV: So the Egyptians made the children of Israel serve with rigor.
2_1_13.KJV: And the Egyptians made the children of Israel to serve with rigour:

2_1_13.NASB: The Egyptians compelled the sons of Israel to labor rigorously;
2_1_13.NLT: So the Egyptians worked the people of Israel without mercy.
2_1_13.WEB: The Egyptians ruthlessly made the children of Israel serve,
2_1_13.AMP: And the Egyptians made the Israelites serve rigorously [forcing them into severe slavery].
2_1_13.MSG: and treated them worse than ever, crushing them with slave labor.
2_1_13.NRSVCE: The Egyptians became ruthless in imposing tasks on the Israelites,

2_1_14.NKJV: And they made their lives bitter with hard bondage— in mortar, in brick, and in all manner of service in the field. All their service in which they made them serve was with rigor.
2_1_14.KJV: And they made their lives bitter with hard bondage, in morter, and in brick, and in all manner of service in the field: all their service, wherein they made them serve, was with rigour.
2_1_14.ESV: and made their lives bitter with hard service, in mortar and brick, and in all kinds of work in the field. In all their work they ruthlessly made them work as slaves.
2_1_14.NASB: and they made their lives bitter with hard labor in mortar and bricks and at all [kinds] of labor in the field, all their labors which they rigorously imposed on them.
2_1_14.NLT: They made their lives bitter, forcing them to mix mortar and make bricks and do all the work in the fields. They were ruthless in all their demands.
2_1_14.WEB: and they made their lives bitter with hard service, in mortar and in brick, and in all kinds of service in the field, all their service, in which they ruthlessly made them serve.
2_1_14.CSB17: and made their lives bitter with difficult labor in brick and mortar and in all kinds of fieldwork. They ruthlessly imposed all this work on them.
2_1_14.AMP: They made their lives bitter with hard labor in mortar, brick, and all kinds of field work. All their labor was harsh and severe.
2_1_14.MSG: They made them miserable with hard labor—making bricks and mortar and back-breaking work in the fields. They piled on the work, crushing them under the cruel workload.
2_1_14.NIV: They made their lives bitter with hard labor in brick and mortar and with all kinds of work in the fields; in all their hard labor the Egyptians used them ruthlessly.
2_1_14.NRSVCE: and made their lives bitter with hard service in mortar and brick and in every kind of field labor. They were ruthless in all the tasks that they imposed on them.

2_1_15.NIV: The king of Egypt said to the Hebrew midwives, whose names were Shiphrah and Puah,

2_1_16.NKJV: and he said, “When you do the duties of a midwife for the Hebrew women, and see them on the birthstools, if it is a son, then you shall kill him; but if it is a daughter, then she shall live.”
2_1_16.KJV: And he said, When ye do the office of a midwife to the Hebrew women, and see them upon the stools; if it be a son, then ye shall kill him: but if it be a daughter, then she shall live.
2_1_16.ESV: “When you serve as midwife to the Hebrew women and see them on the birthstool, if it is a son, you shall kill him, but if it is a daughter, she shall live.”
2_1_16.NASB: and he said, 'When you are helping the Hebrew women to give birth and see [them] upon the birthstool, if it is a son, then you shall put him to death; but if it is a daughter, then she shall live.'
2_1_16.NLT: “When you help the Hebrew women as they give birth, watch as they deliver. If the baby is a boy, kill him; if it is a girl, let her live.”
2_1_16.WEB: and he said, “When you perform the duty of a midwife to the Hebrew women, and see them on the birth stool; if it is a son, then you shall kill him; but if it is a daughter, then she shall live.”
2_1_16.CSB17: “When you help the Hebrew women give birth, observe them as they deliver. If the child is a son, kill him, but if it’s a daughter, she may live.”
2_1_16.AMP: "When you act as midwives to the Hebrew women and see them on the birthstool, if it is a son, you shall kill him; but if it is a daughter, she shall live."
2_1_16.MSG: He said, "When you deliver the Hebrew women, look at the sex of the baby. If it's a boy, kill him; if it's a girl, let her live."
2_1_16.NIV: “When you help the Hebrew women in childbirth and observe them on the delivery stool, if it is a boy, kill him; but if it is a girl, let her live.”
2_1_16.NRSVCE: “When you act as midwives to the Hebrew women, and see them on the birthstool, if it is a boy, kill him; but if it is a girl, she shall live.”

2_1_17.NKJV: But the midwives feared God, and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the male children alive.
2_1_17.KJV: But the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the men children alive.
2_1_17.ESV: But the midwives feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but let the male children live.
2_1_17.NASB: But the midwives feared God, and did not do as the king of Egypt had commanded them, but let the boys live.
2_1_17.NLT: But because the midwives feared God, they refused to obey the king’s orders. They allowed the boys to live, too.
2_1_17.WEB: But the midwives feared God, and didn’t do what the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the baby boys alive.
2_1_17.CSB17: The midwives, however, feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt had told them; they let the boys live.
2_1_17.AMP: But the midwives feared God [with profound reverence] and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded, but they let the boy babies live.
2_1_17.MSG: But the midwives had far too much respect for God and didn't do what the king of Egypt ordered; they let the boy babies live.
2_1_17.NIV: The midwives, however, feared God and did not do what the king of Egypt had told them to do; they let the boys live.
2_1_17.NRSVCE: But the midwives feared God; they did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but they let the boys live.

2_1_18.NKJV: So the king of Egypt called for the midwives and said to them, “Why have you done this thing, and saved the male children alive?”
2_1_18.KJV: And the king of Egypt called for the midwives, and said unto them, Why have ye done this thing, and have saved the men children alive?
2_1_18.ESV: So the king of Egypt called the midwives and said to them, “Why have you done this, and let the male children live?”
2_1_18.NASB: So the king of Egypt called for the midwives and said to them, 'Why have you done this thing, and let the boys live?'
2_1_18.NLT: So the king of Egypt called for the midwives. “Why have you done this?” he demanded. “Why have you allowed the boys to live?”
2_1_18.WEB: The king of Egypt called for the midwives, and said to them, “Why have you done this thing, and have saved the boys alive?”
2_1_18.CSB17: So the king of Egypt summoned the midwives and asked them, “Why have you done this and let the boys live?”
2_1_18.AMP: So the king of Egypt called for the midwives and said to them, "Why have you done this thing, and allowed the boy babies to live?"
2_1_18.MSG: The king of Egypt called in the midwives. "Why didn't you obey my orders? You've let those babies live!"
2_1_18.NIV: Then the king of Egypt summoned the midwives and asked them, “Why have you done this? Why have you let the boys live?”
2_1_18.NRSVCE: So the king of Egypt summoned the midwives and said to them, “Why have you done this, and allowed the boys to live?”

2_1_19.NKJV: And the midwives said to Pharaoh, “Because the Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women; for they are lively and give birth before the midwives come to them.”
2_1_19.KJV: And the midwives said unto Pharaoh, Because the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian women; for they are lively, and are delivered ere the midwives come in unto them.

2_1_19.NASB: The midwives said to Pharaoh, 'Because the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous and give birth before the midwife can get to them.'
2_1_19.NLT: “The Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women,” the midwives replied. “They are more vigorous and have their babies so quickly that we cannot get there in time.”
2_1_19.WEB: The midwives said to Pharaoh, “Because the Hebrew women aren’t like the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous, and give birth before the midwife comes to them.”
2_1_19.CSB17: The midwives said to Pharaoh, “The Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women, for they are vigorous and give birth before the midwife can get to them.”
2_1_19.AMP: The midwives answered Pharaoh, "Because the Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women; they are vigorous and give birth quickly and their babies are born before the midwife can get to them."
2_1_19.MSG: The midwives answered Pharaoh, "The Hebrew women aren't like the Egyptian women; they're vigorous. Before the midwife can get there, they've already had the baby."
2_1_19.NIV: The midwives answered Pharaoh, “Hebrew women are not like Egyptian women; they are vigorous and give birth before the midwives arrive.”
2_1_19.NRSVCE: The midwives said to Pharaoh, “Because the Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous and give birth before the midwife comes to them.”

2_1_20.NKJV: Therefore God dealt well with the midwives, and the people multiplied and grew very mighty.
2_1_20.KJV: Therefore God dealt well with the midwives: and the people multiplied, and waxed very mighty.
2_1_20.ESV: So God dealt well with the midwives. And the people multiplied and grew very strong.
2_1_20.NASB: So God was good to the midwives, and the people multiplied, and became very mighty.
2_1_20.NLT: So God was good to the midwives, and the Israelites continued to multiply, growing more and more powerful.
2_1_20.WEB: God dealt well with the midwives, and the people multiplied, and grew very mighty.

2_1_21.NLT: And because the midwives feared God, he gave them families of their own.

2_1_21.CSB17: Since the midwives feared God, he gave them families.

2_1_22.NKJV: So Pharaoh commanded all his people, saying, “Every son who is born you shall cast into the river, and every daughter you shall save alive.”
2_1_22.KJV: And Pharaoh charged all his people, saying, Every son that is born ye shall cast into the river, and every daughter ye shall save alive.

2_2_1.NKJV: And a man of the house of Levi went and took as wife a daughter of Levi.
2_2_1.KJV: And there went a man of the house of Levi, and took to wife a daughter of Levi.
2_2_1.ESV: Now a man from the house of Levi went and took as his wife a Levite woman.
2_2_1.NASB: Now a man from the house of Levi went and married a daughter of Levi.
2_2_1.NLT: About this time, a man and woman from the tribe of Levi got married.
2_2_1.WEB: A man of the house of Levi went and took a daughter of Levi as his wife.
2_2_1.CSB17: Now a man from the family of Levi married a Levite woman.
2_2_1.AMP: Now a man of the house of Levi [the priestly tribe] went and took as his wife a daughter of Levi.
2_2_1.MSG: A man from the family of Levi married a Levite woman.
2_2_1.NIV: The Birth of MosesNow a man of the house of Levi married a Levite woman,
2_2_1.NRSVCE: Now a man from the house of Levi went and married a Levite woman.

2_2_2.NKJV: So the woman conceived and bore a son. And when she saw that he was a beautiful child, she hid him three months.
2_2_2.KJV: And the woman conceived, and bare a son: and when she saw him that he was a goodly child, she hid him three months.
2_2_2.ESV: The woman conceived and bore a son, and when she saw that he was a fine child, she hid him three months.
2_2_2.NASB: The woman conceived and bore a son; and when she saw that he was beautiful, she hid him for three months.
2_2_2.NLT: The woman became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She saw that he was a special baby and kept him hidden for three months.
2_2_2.WEB: The woman conceived, and bore a son. When she saw that he was a fine child, she hid him three months.
2_2_2.CSB17: The woman became pregnant and gave birth to a son; when she saw that he was beautiful, she hid him for three months.
2_2_2.AMP: The woman conceived and gave birth to a son; and when she saw that he was [especially] beautiful and healthy, she hid him for three months [to protect him from the Egyptians].
2_2_2.MSG: The woman became pregnant and had a son. She saw there was something special about him and hid him. She hid him for three months.
2_2_2.NIV: and she became pregnant and gave birth to a son. When she saw that he was a fine child, she hid him for three months.
2_2_2.NRSVCE: The woman conceived and bore a son; and when she saw that he was a fine baby, she hid him three months.

2_2_3.NKJV: But when she could no longer hide him, she took an ark of bulrushes for him, daubed it with asphalt and pitch, put the child in it, and laid it in the reeds by the river’s bank.
2_2_3.KJV: And when she could not longer hide him, she took for him an ark of bulrushes, and daubed it with slime and with pitch, and put the child therein; and she laid it in the flags by the river's brink.
2_2_3.ESV: When she could hide him no longer, she took for him a basket made of bulrushes and daubed it with bitumen and pitch. She put the child in it and placed it among the reeds by the river bank.
2_2_3.NASB: But when she could hide him no longer, she got him a wicker basket and covered it over with tar and pitch. Then she put the child into it and set [it] among the reeds by the bank of the Nile.
2_2_3.NLT: But when she could no longer hide him, she got a basket made of papyrus reeds and waterproofed it with tar and pitch. She put the baby in the basket and laid it among the reeds along the bank of the Nile River.
2_2_3.WEB: When she could no longer hide him, she took a papyrus basket for him, and coated it with tar and with pitch. She put the child in it, and laid it in the reeds by the river’s bank.
2_2_3.CSB17: But when she could no longer hide him, she got a papyrus basket for him and coated it with asphalt and pitch. She placed the child in it and set it among the reeds by the bank of the Nile.
2_2_3.AMP: When she could no longer hide him, she got him a basket (chest) made of papyrus reeds and covered it with tar and pitch [making it waterproof]. Then she put the child in it and set it among the reeds by the bank of the Nile.
2_2_3.MSG: When she couldn't hide him any longer she got a little basket-boat made of papyrus, waterproofed it with tar and pitch, and placed the child in it. Then she set it afloat in the reeds at the edge of the Nile.
2_2_3.NIV: But when she could hide him no longer, she got a papyrus basket for him and coated it with tar and pitch. Then she placed the child in it and put it among the reeds along the bank of the Nile.
2_2_3.NRSVCE: When she could hide him no longer she got a papyrus basket for him, and plastered it with bitumen and pitch; she put the child in it and placed it among the reeds on the bank of the river.

2_2_4.NKJV: And his sister stood afar off, to know what would be done to him.
2_2_4.KJV: And his sister stood afar off, to wit what would be done to him.
2_2_4.ESV: And his sister stood at a distance to know what would be done to him.
2_2_4.NASB: His sister stood at a distance to find out what would happen to him.
2_2_4.NLT: The baby’s sister then stood at a distance, watching to see what would happen to him.
2_2_4.WEB: His sister stood far off, to see what would be done to him.
2_2_4.CSB17: Then his sister stood at a distance in order to see what would happen to him.
2_2_4.AMP: And his sister [Miriam] stood some distance away to find out what would happen to him.
2_2_4.MSG: The baby's older sister found herself a vantage point a little way off and watched to see what would happen to him.
2_2_4.NIV: His sister stood at a distance to see what would happen to him.
2_2_4.NRSVCE: His sister stood at a distance, to see what would happen to him.

2_2_5.NKJV: Then the daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe at the river. And her maidens walked along the riverside; and when she saw the ark among the reeds, she sent her maid to get it.
2_2_5.KJV: And the daughter of Pharaoh came down to wash herself at the river; and her maidens walked along by the river's side; and when she saw the ark among the flags, she sent her maid to fetch it.
2_2_5.ESV: Now the daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe at the river, while her young women walked beside the river. She saw the basket among the reeds and sent her servant woman, and she took it.
2_2_5.NASB: The daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe at the Nile, with her maidens walking alongside the Nile; and she saw the basket among the reeds and sent her maid, and she brought it [to her].
2_2_5.NLT: Soon Pharaoh’s daughter came down to bathe in the river, and her attendants walked along the riverbank. When the princess saw the basket among the reeds, she sent her maid to get it for her.
2_2_5.WEB: Pharaoh’s daughter came down to bathe at the river. Her maidens walked along by the riverside. She saw the basket among the reeds, and sent her servant to get it.
2_2_5.CSB17: Pharaoh’s daughter went down to bathe at the Nile while her servant girls walked along the riverbank. She saw the basket among the reeds, sent her slave girl, took it,
2_2_5.AMP: Now the daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe at the Nile, and [she, together with] her maidens walked along the river's bank; she saw the basket among the reeds and sent her maid [to get it], and she brought it to her.
2_2_5.MSG: Pharaoh's daughter came down to the Nile to bathe; her maidens strolled on the bank. She saw the basket-boat floating in the reeds and sent her maid to get it.
2_2_5.NIV: Then Pharaoh’s daughter went down to the Nile to bathe, and her attendants were walking along the river bank. She saw the basket among the reeds and sent her slave girl to get it.
2_2_5.NRSVCE: The daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe at the river, while her attendants walked beside the river. She saw the basket among the reeds and sent her maid to bring it.

2_2_6.NKJV: And when she opened it, she saw the child, and behold, the baby wept. So she had compassion on him, and said, “This is one of the Hebrews’ children.”
2_2_6.KJV: And when she had opened it, she saw the child: and, behold, the babe wept. And she had compassion on him, and said, This is one of the Hebrews' children.
2_2_6.ESV: When she opened it, she saw the child, and behold, the baby was crying. She took pity on him and said, “This is one of the Hebrews' children.”
2_2_6.NASB: When she opened [it], she saw the child, and behold, [the] boy was crying. And she had pity on him and said, 'This is one of the Hebrews' children.'
2_2_6.NLT: When the princess opened it, she saw the baby. The little boy was crying, and she felt sorry for him. “This must be one of the Hebrew children,” she said.
2_2_6.WEB: She opened it, and saw the child, and behold, the baby cried. She had compassion on him, and said, “This is one of the Hebrews’ children.”
2_2_6.CSB17: opened it, and saw him, the child — and there he was, a little boy, crying. She felt sorry for him and said, “This is one of the Hebrew boys.”
2_2_6.AMP: When she opened it, she saw the child, and behold, the baby was crying. And she took pity on him and said, "This is one of the Hebrews' children."
2_2_6.MSG: She opened it and saw the child—a baby crying! Her heart went out to him. She said, "This must be one of the Hebrew babies."
2_2_6.NIV: She opened it and saw the baby. He was crying, and she felt sorry for him. “This is one of the Hebrew babies,” she said.
2_2_6.NRSVCE: When she opened it, she saw the child. He was crying, and she took pity on him. “This must be one of the Hebrews’ children,” she said.

2_2_7.NKJV: Then his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, “Shall I go and call a nurse for you from the Hebrew women, that she may nurse the child for you?”
2_2_7.KJV: Then said his sister to Pharaoh's daughter, Shall I go and call to thee a nurse of the Hebrew women, that she may nurse the child for thee?
2_2_7.ESV: Then his sister said to Pharaoh's daughter, “Shall I go and call you a nurse from the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you?”
2_2_7.NASB: Then his sister said to Pharaoh's daughter, 'Shall I go and call a nurse for you from the Hebrew women that she may nurse the child for you?'
2_2_7.NLT: Then the baby’s sister approached the princess. “Should I go and find one of the Hebrew women to nurse the baby for you?” she asked.
2_2_7.WEB: Then his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, “Should I go and call a nurse for you from the Hebrew women, that she may nurse the child for you?”
2_2_7.CSB17: Then his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, “Should I go and call a Hebrew woman who is nursing to nurse the boy for you?”
2_2_7.AMP: Then his sister said to Pharaoh's daughter, "Shall I go and call a wet-nurse from the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you?"
2_2_7.MSG: Then his sister was before her: "Do you want me to go and get a nursing mother from the Hebrews so she can nurse the baby for you?"
2_2_7.NIV: Then his sister asked Pharaoh’s daughter, “Shall I go and get one of the Hebrew women to nurse the baby for you?”
2_2_7.NRSVCE: Then his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, “Shall I go and get you a nurse from the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you?”

2_2_8.NKJV: And Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Go.” So the maiden went and called the child’s mother.
2_2_8.KJV: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, Go. And the maid went and called the child's mother.
2_2_8.ESV: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, “Go.” So the girl went and called the child's mother.
2_2_8.NASB: Pharaoh's daughter said to her, 'Go [ahead].' So the girl went and called the child's mother.
2_2_8.NLT: “Yes, do!” the princess replied. So the girl went and called the baby’s mother.
2_2_8.WEB: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Go.” The maiden went and called the child’s mother.
2_2_8.CSB17: “Go,” Pharaoh’s daughter told her. So the girl went and called the boy’s mother.
2_2_8.AMP: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, "Go ahead." So the girl went and called the child's mother.
2_2_8.MSG: Pharaoh's daughter said, "Yes. Go." The girl went and called the child's mother.
2_2_8.NIV: “Yes, go,” she answered. And the girl went and got the baby’s mother.
2_2_8.NRSVCE: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Yes.” So the girl went and called the child’s mother.

2_2_9.NKJV: Then Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child away and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages.” So the woman took the child and nursed him.
2_2_9.KJV: And Pharaoh's daughter said unto her, Take this child away, and nurse it for me, and I will give thee thy wages. And the woman took the child, and nursed it.
2_2_9.ESV: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, “Take this child away and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages.” So the woman took the child and nursed him.
2_2_9.NASB: Then Pharaoh's daughter said to her, 'Take this child away and nurse him for me and I will give [you] your wages.' So the woman took the child and nursed him.
2_2_9.NLT: “Take this baby and nurse him for me,” the princess told the baby’s mother. “I will pay you for your help.” So the woman took her baby home and nursed him.
2_2_9.WEB: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child away, and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages.” The woman took the child, and nursed it.
2_2_9.CSB17: Then Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child and nurse him for me, and I will pay your wages.” So the woman took the boy and nursed him.
2_2_9.AMP: Then Pharaoh's daughter said to her, "Take this child away and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages." So the woman took the child and nursed him.
2_2_9.MSG: Pharaoh's daughter told her, "Take this baby and nurse him for me. I'll pay you." The woman took the child and nursed him.
2_2_9.NIV: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this baby and nurse him for me, and I will pay you.” So the woman took the baby and nursed him.
2_2_9.NRSVCE: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child and nurse it for me, and I will give you your wages.” So the woman took the child and nursed it.

2_2_10.NKJV: And the child grew, and she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. So she called his name Moses, saying, “Because I drew him out of the water.”
2_2_10.KJV: And the child grew, and she brought him unto Pharaoh's daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name Moses: and she said, Because I drew him out of the water. Moses: that is, Drawn out
2_2_10.ESV: When the child grew older, she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter, and he became her son. She named him Moses, “Because,” she said, “I drew him out of the water.”
2_2_10.NASB: The child grew, and she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter and he became her son. And she named him Moses, and said, 'Because I drew him out of the water.'
2_2_10.NLT: Later, when the boy was older, his mother brought him back to Pharaoh’s daughter, who adopted him as her own son. The princess named him Moses, for she explained, “I lifted him out of the water.”
2_2_10.WEB: The child grew, and she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. She named him Moses, and said, “Because I drew him out of the water.”
2_2_10.CSB17: When the child grew older, she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. She named him Moses, “Because,” she said, “I drew him out of the water.”
2_2_10.AMP: And the child grew, and she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter and he became her son. And she named him Moses, and said, "Because I drew him out of the water."
2_2_10.MSG: After the child was weaned, she presented him to Pharaoh's daughter who adopted him as her son. She named him Moses (Pulled-Out), saying, "I pulled him out of the water."
2_2_10.NIV: When the child grew older, she took him to Pharaoh’s daughter and he became her son. She named him Moses, saying, “I drew him out of the water.”
2_2_10.NRSVCE: When the child grew up, she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and she took him as her son. She named him Moses, “because,” she said, “I drew him out of the water.”

2_2_11.NKJV: Now it came to pass in those days, when Moses was grown, that he went out to his brethren and looked at their burdens. And he saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his brethren.
2_2_11.KJV: And it came to pass in those days, when Moses was grown, that he went out unto his brethren, and looked on their burdens: and he spied an Egyptian smiting an Hebrew, one of his brethren.
2_2_11.ESV: One day, when Moses had grown up, he went out to his people and looked on their burdens, and he saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his people.
2_2_11.NASB: Now it came about in those days, when Moses had grown up, that he went out to his brethren and looked on their hard labors; and he saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his brethren.
2_2_11.NLT: Many years later, when Moses had grown up, he went out to visit his own people, the Hebrews, and he saw how hard they were forced to work. During his visit, he saw an Egyptian beating one of his fellow Hebrews.
2_2_11.WEB: In those days, when Moses had grown up, he went out to his brothers, and looked at their burdens. He saw an Egyptian striking a Hebrew, one of his brothers.
2_2_11.CSB17: Years later, after Moses had grown up, he went out to his own people and observed their forced labor. He saw an Egyptian striking a Hebrew, one of his people.
2_2_11.AMP: One day, after Moses had grown [into adulthood], it happened that he went to his countrymen and looked [with compassion] at their hard labors; and he saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his countrymen.
2_2_11.MSG: Time passed. Moses grew up. One day he went and saw his brothers, saw all that hard labor. Then he saw an Egyptian hit a Hebrew—one of his relatives!
2_2_11.NIV: Moses Flees to MidianOne day, after Moses had grown up, he went out to where his own people were and watched them at their hard labor. He saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his own people.
2_2_11.NRSVCE: One day, after Moses had grown up, he went out to his people and saw their forced labor. He saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his kinsfolk.

2_2_12.NKJV: So he looked this way and that way, and when he saw no one, he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.KJV: And he looked this way and that way, and when he saw that there was no man, he slew the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.ESV: He looked this way and that, and seeing no one, he struck down the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.NASB: So he looked this way and that, and when he saw there was no one [around], he struck down the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.NLT: After looking in all directions to make sure no one was watching, Moses killed the Egyptian and hid the body in the sand.
2_2_12.WEB: He looked this way and that way, and when he saw that there was no one, he killed the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.CSB17: Looking all around and seeing no one, he struck the Egyptian dead and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.AMP: He turned to look around, and seeing no one, he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.MSG: He looked this way and then that; when he realized there was no one in sight, he killed the Egyptian and buried him in the sand.
2_2_12.NIV: Glancing this way and that and seeing no one, he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
2_2_12.NRSVCE: He looked this way and that, and seeing no one he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.

2_2_13.NKJV: And when he went out the second day, behold, two Hebrew men were fighting, and he said to the one who did the wrong, “Why are you striking your companion?”
2_2_13.KJV: And when he went out the second day, behold, two men of the Hebrews strove together: and he said to him that did the wrong, Wherefore smitest thou thy fellow?
2_2_13.ESV: When he went out the next day, behold, two Hebrews were struggling together. And he said to the man in the wrong, “Why do you strike your companion?”
2_2_13.NASB: He went out the next day, and behold, two Hebrews were fighting with each other; and he said to the offender, 'Why are you striking your companion?'
2_2_13.NLT: The next day, when Moses went out to visit his people again, he saw two Hebrew men fighting. “Why are you beating up your friend?” Moses said to the one who had started the fight.
2_2_13.WEB: He went out the second day, and behold, two men of the Hebrews were fighting with each other. He said to him who did the wrong, “Why do you strike your fellow?”
2_2_13.CSB17: The next day he went out and saw two Hebrews fighting. He asked the one in the wrong, “Why are you attacking your neighbor?”
2_2_13.AMP: He went out the next day and saw two Hebrew men fighting with each other; and he said to the aggressor, "Why are you striking your friend?"
2_2_13.MSG: The next day he went out there again. Two Hebrew men were fighting. He spoke to the man who started it: "Why are you hitting your neighbor?"
2_2_13.NIV: The next day he went out and saw two Hebrews fighting. He asked the one in the wrong, “Why are you hitting your fellow Hebrew?”
2_2_13.NRSVCE: When he went out the next day, he saw two Hebrews fighting; and he said to the one who was in the wrong, “Why do you strike your fellow Hebrew?”

2_2_14.NKJV: Then he said, “Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you intend to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?” So Moses feared and said, “Surely this thing is known!”
2_2_14.KJV: And he said, Who made thee a prince and a judge over us? intendest thou to kill me, as thou killedst the Egyptian? And Moses feared, and said, Surely this thing is known. a prince: Heb. a man, a prince
2_2_14.ESV: He answered, “Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid, and thought, “Surely the thing is known.”
2_2_14.NASB: But he said, 'Who made you a prince or a judge over us? Are you intending to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?' Then Moses was afraid and said, 'Surely the matter has become known.'
2_2_14.NLT: The man replied, “Who appointed you to be our prince and judge? Are you going to kill me as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?” Then Moses was afraid, thinking, “Everyone knows what I did.”
2_2_14.WEB: He said, “Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you plan to kill me, as you killed the Egyptian?” Moses was afraid, and said, “Surely this thing is known.”
2_2_14.CSB17: “Who made you a commander and judge over us?” the man replied. “Are you planning to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses became afraid and thought, “What I did is certainly known.”
2_2_14.AMP: But the man said, "Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you intend to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?" Then Moses was afraid and said, "Certainly this incident is known."
2_2_14.MSG: The man shot back: "Who do you think you are, telling us what to do? Are you going to kill me the way you killed that Egyptian?"Then Moses panicked: "Word's gotten out—people know about this."
2_2_14.NIV: The man said, “Who made you ruler and judge over us? Are you thinking of killing me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid and thought, “What I did must have become known.”
2_2_14.NRSVCE: He answered, “Who made you a ruler and judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid and thought, “Surely the thing is known.”

2_2_15.NKJV: When Pharaoh heard of this matter, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh and dwelt in the land of Midian; and he sat down by a well.
2_2_15.KJV: Now when Pharaoh heard this thing, he sought to slay Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and dwelt in the land of Midian: and he sat down by a well.
2_2_15.ESV: When Pharaoh heard of it, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from Pharaoh and stayed in the land of Midian. And he sat down by a well.
2_2_15.NASB: When Pharaoh heard of this matter, he tried to kill Moses. But Moses fled from the presence of Pharaoh and settled in the land of Midian, and he sat down by a well.
2_2_15.NLT: And sure enough, Pharaoh heard what had happened, and he tried to kill Moses. But Moses fled from Pharaoh and went to live in the land of Midian. When Moses arrived in Midian, he sat down beside a well.
2_2_15.WEB: Now when Pharaoh heard this thing, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and lived in the land of Midian, and he sat down by a well.
2_2_15.CSB17: When Pharaoh heard about this, he tried to kill Moses. But Moses fled from Pharaoh and went to live in the land of Midian, and sat down by a well.
2_2_15.AMP: When Pharaoh heard about this matter, he tried to kill Moses. Then Moses fled from Pharaoh's presence and took refuge in the land of Midian, where he sat down by a well.
2_2_15.MSG: Pharaoh heard about it and tried to kill Moses, but Moses got away to the land of Midian. He sat down by a well.
2_2_15.NIV: When Pharaoh heard of this, he tried to kill Moses, but Moses fled from Pharaoh and went to live in Midian, where he sat down by a well.
2_2_15.NRSVCE: When Pharaoh heard of it, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from Pharaoh. He settled in the land of Midian, and sat down by a well.

2_2_16.NKJV: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters. And they came and drew water, and they filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.
2_2_16.KJV: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters: and they came and drew water, and filled the troughs to water their father's flock. priest: or, prince
2_2_16.ESV: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters, and they came and drew water and filled the troughs to water their father's flock.
2_2_16.NASB: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters; and they came to draw water and filled the troughs to water their father's flock.
2_2_16.NLT: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters who came as usual to draw water and fill the water troughs for their father’s flocks.
2_2_16.WEB: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters. They came and drew water, and filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.
2_2_16.CSB17: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters. They came to draw water and filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.
2_2_16.AMP: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters; and they came and drew water [from the well where Moses was resting] and filled the troughs to water their father's flock.
2_2_16.MSG: The priest of Midian had seven daughters. They came and drew water, filling the troughs and watering their father's sheep.
2_2_16.NIV: Now a priest of Midian had seven daughters, and they came to draw water and fill the troughs to water their father’s flock.
2_2_16.NRSVCE: The priest of Midian had seven daughters. They came to draw water, and filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.

2_2_17.NKJV: Then the shepherds came and drove them away; but Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.
2_2_17.KJV: And the shepherds came and drove them away: but Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.
2_2_17.ESV: The shepherds came and drove them away, but Moses stood up and saved them, and watered their flock.
2_2_17.NASB: Then the shepherds came and drove them away, but Moses stood up and helped them and watered their flock.
2_2_17.NLT: But some other shepherds came and chased them away. So Moses jumped up and rescued the girls from the shepherds. Then he drew water for their flocks.
2_2_17.WEB: The shepherds came and drove them away; but Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.

2_4_10.NKJV: Then Moses said to the LORD, “O my Lord, I am not eloquent, neither before nor since You have spoken to Your servant; but I am slow of speech and slow of tongue.”

2_6_1.NKJV: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh. For with a strong hand he will let them go, and with a strong hand he will drive them out of his land.”

2_6_12.NKJV: And Moses spoke before the LORD, saying, “The children of Israel have not heeded me. How then shall Pharaoh heed me, for I am of uncircumcised lips?”
2_6_12.KJV: And Moses spake before the LORD, saying, Behold, the children of Israel have not hearkened unto me; how then shall Pharaoh hear me, who am of uncircumcised lips?
2_6_12.ESV: But Moses said to the LORD, “Behold, the people of Israel have not listened to me. How then shall Pharaoh listen to me, for I am of uncircumcised lips?”
2_6_12.NASB: But Moses spoke before the LORD, saying, 'Behold, the sons of Israel have not listened to me; how then will Pharaoh listen to me, for I am unskilled in speech?'
2_6_12.NLT: “But LORD!” Moses objected. “My own people won’t listen to me anymore. How can I expect Pharaoh to listen? I’m such a clumsy speaker! ”
2_6_12.WEB: Moses spoke before Yahweh, saying, “Behold, the children of Israel haven’t listened to me. How then shall Pharaoh listen to me, who am of uncircumcised lips?”
2_6_12.CSB17: But Moses said in the LORD’s presence: “If the Israelites will not listen to me, then how will Pharaoh listen to me, since I am such a poor speaker?”
2_6_12.AMP: But Moses said to the LORD, "Look, [my own people] the Israelites have not listened to me; so how then will Pharaoh listen to me, for I am unskilled and inept in speech?"
2_6_12.MSG: Moses answered God, "Look—the Israelites won't even listen to me. How do you expect Pharaoh to? And besides, I stutter."
2_6_12.NIV: But Moses said to the Lord, “If the Israelites will not listen to me, why would Pharaoh listen to me, since I speak with faltering lips?”
2_6_12.NRSVCE: But Moses spoke to the LORD, “The Israelites have not listened to me; how then shall Pharaoh listen to me, poor speaker that I am?”

2_6_13.NKJV: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, and gave them a command for the children of Israel and for Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.

2_10_7.NKJV: Then Pharaoh’s servants said to him, “How long shall this man be a snare to us? Let the men go, that they may serve the LORD their God. Do you not yet know that Egypt is destroyed?”

2_10_19.NLT: The LORD responded by shifting the wind, and the strong west wind blew the locusts into the Red Sea. Not a single locust remained in all the land of Egypt.

2_10_21.NKJV: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, darkness which may even be felt.”

2_10_21.NLT: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Lift your hand toward heaven, and the land of Egypt will be covered with a darkness so thick you can feel it.”
2_10_21.WEB: Yahweh said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward the sky, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, even darkness which may be felt.”
2_10_21.CSB17: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward heaven, and there will be darkness over the land of Egypt, a darkness that can be felt.”

2_10_22.NKJV: So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and there was thick darkness in all the land of Egypt three days.

2_10_22.CSB17: So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and there was thick darkness throughout the land of Egypt for three days.
2_10_22.AMP: So Moses stretched out his hand toward the sky, and for three days a thick darkness was all over the land of Egypt [no sun, no moon, no stars].

2_10_22.NIV: So Moses stretched out his hand toward the sky, and total darkness covered all Egypt for three days.

2_10_23.NKJV: They did not see one another; nor did anyone rise from his place for three days. But all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.
2_10_23.KJV: They saw not one another, neither rose any from his place for three days: but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.

2_10_23.NASB: They did not see one another, nor did anyone rise from his place for three days, but all the sons of Israel had light in their dwellings.
2_10_23.NLT: During all that time the people could not see each other, and no one moved. But there was light as usual where the people of Israel lived.
2_10_23.WEB: They didn’t see one another, and nobody rose from his place for three days; but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.
2_10_23.CSB17: One person could not see another, and for three days they did not move from where they were. Yet all the Israelites had light where they lived.
2_10_23.AMP: The Egyptians could not see one another, nor did anyone leave his place for three days, but all the Israelites had [supernatural] light in their dwellings.

2_10_23.NIV: No one could see anyone else or leave his place for three days. Yet all the Israelites had light in the places where they lived.
2_10_23.NRSVCE: People could not see one another, and for three days they could not move from where they were; but all the Israelites had light where they lived.

2_10_24.NKJV: Then Pharaoh called to Moses and said, “Go, serve the LORD; only let your flocks and your herds be kept back. Let your little ones also go with you.”

2_10_24.NASB: Then Pharaoh called to Moses, and said, 'Go, serve the LORD; only let your flocks and your herds be detained. Even your little ones may go with you.'
2_10_24.NLT: Finally, Pharaoh called for Moses. “Go and worship the LORD,” he said. “But leave your flocks and herds here. You may even take your little ones with you.”

2_10_25.NKJV: But Moses said, “You must also give us sacrifices and burnt offerings, that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God.

2_10_25.NLT: “No,” Moses said, “you must provide us with animals for sacrifices and burnt offerings to the LORD our God.

2_11_2.NKJV: Speak now in the hearing of the people, and let every man ask from his neighbor and every woman from her neighbor, articles of silver and articles of gold.”

2_11_3.NKJV: And the LORD gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh’s servants and in the sight of the people.

2_11_3.NASB: The LORD gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Furthermore, the man Moses [himself] was greatly esteemed in the land of Egypt, [both] in the sight of Pharaoh's servants and in the sight of the people.
2_11_3.NLT: (Now the LORD had caused the Egyptians to look favorably on the people of Israel. And Moses was considered a very great man in the land of Egypt, respected by Pharaoh’s officials and the Egyptian people alike.)

2_11_7.NKJV: But against none of the children of Israel shall a dog move its tongue, against man or beast, that you may know that the LORD does make a difference between the Egyptians and Israel.’

2_11_7.NLT: But among the Israelites it will be so peaceful that not even a dog will bark. Then you will know that the LORD makes a distinction between the Egyptians and the Israelites.
2_11_7.WEB: But against any of the children of Israel a dog won’t even bark or move its tongue, against man or animal; that you may know that Yahweh makes a distinction between the Egyptians and Israel.

2_11_9.NKJV: But the LORD said to Moses, “Pharaoh will not heed you, so that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.”

2_11_9.CSB17: The LORD said to Moses, “Pharaoh will not listen to you, so that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.”

2_12_3.NKJV: Speak to all the congregation of Israel, saying: ‘On the tenth of this month every man shall take for himself a lamb, according to the house of his father, a lamb for a household.

2_12_3.NASB: 'Speak to all the congregation of Israel, saying, 'On the tenth of this month they are each one to take a lamb for themselves, according to their fathers' households, a lamb for each household.
2_12_3.NLT: Announce to the whole community of Israel that on the tenth day of this month each family must choose a lamb or a young goat for a sacrifice, one animal for each household.

2_12_4.NKJV: And if the household is too small for the lamb, let him and his neighbor next to his house take it according to the number of the persons; according to each man’s need you shall make your count for the lamb.

2_12_4.NASB: 'Now if the household is too small for a lamb, then he and his neighbor nearest to his house are to take one according to the number of persons [in them]; according to what each man should eat, you are to divide the lamb.

2_12_12.KJV: For I will pass through the land of Egypt this night, and will smite all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgment: I am the LORD. gods: or, princes
