2_17_5.MSG: God said to Moses, "Go on out ahead of the people, taking with you some of the elders of Israel. Take the staff you used to strike the Nile. And go.
2_17_5.NIV: The Lord answered Moses, “Walk on ahead of the people. Take with you some of the elders of Israel and take in your hand the staff with which you struck the Nile, and go.
2_17_5.NRSVCE: The LORD said to Moses, “Go on ahead of the people, and take some of the elders of Israel with you; take in your hand the staff with which you struck the Nile, and go.

2_17_6.MSG: I'm going to be present before you there on the rock at Horeb. You are to strike the rock. Water will gush out of it and the people will drink."Moses did what he said, with the elders of Israel right there watching.

2_17_7.NKJV: So he called the name of the place Massah and Meribah, because of the contention of the children of Israel, and because they tempted the LORD, saying, “Is the LORD among us or not?”
2_17_7.KJV: And he called the name of the place Massah, and Meribah, because of the chiding of the children of Israel, and because they tempted the LORD, saying, Is the LORD among us, or not? Massah: that is, Temptation Meribah: that is, Chiding, or, Strife
2_17_7.ESV: And he called the name of the place Massah and Meribah, because of the quarreling of the people of Israel, and because they tested the LORD by saying, “Is the LORD among us or not?”
2_17_7.NASB: He named the place Massah and Meribah because of the quarrel of the sons of Israel, and because they tested the LORD, saying, 'Is the LORD among us, or not?'
2_17_7.NLT: Moses named the place Massah (which means “test”) and Meribah (which means “arguing”) because the people of Israel argued with Moses and tested the LORD by saying, “Is the LORD here with us or not?”
2_17_7.WEB: He called the name of the place Massah, and Meribah, because the children of Israel quarreled, and because they tested Yahweh, saying, “Is Yahweh among us, or not?”
2_17_7.CSB17: He named the place Massah and Meribah because the Israelites complained, and because they tested the LORD, saying, “Is the LORD among us or not?”
2_17_7.AMP: He named the place [where this miracle occurred] Massah (test) and Meribah (contention) because of the quarreling of the sons of Israel, and because they tested the [patience of the] LORD, saying, "Is the LORD among us, or not?"

2_17_12.NKJV: But Moses’ hands became heavy; so they took a stone and put it under him, and he sat on it. And Aaron and Hur supported his hands, one on one side, and the other on the other side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.

2_20_7.NKJV: “You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not hold him guiltless who takes His name in vain.
2_20_7.KJV: Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain; for the LORD will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.
2_20_7.ESV: “You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not hold him guiltless who takes his name in vain.
2_20_7.NASB: 'You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not leave him unpunished who takes His name in vain.
2_20_7.NLT: “You must not misuse the name of the LORD your God. The LORD will not let you go unpunished if you misuse his name.
2_20_7.WEB: “You shall not take the name of Yahweh your God in vain, for Yahweh will not hold him guiltless who takes his name in vain.
2_20_7.CSB17: Do not misuse the name of the LORD your God, because the LORD will not leave anyone unpunished who misuses his name.
2_20_7.AMP: "You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain [that is, irreverently, in false affirmations or in ways that impugn the character of God]; for the LORD will not hold guiltless nor leave unpunished the one who takes His name in vain [disregarding its reverence and its power].
2_20_7.MSG: No using the name of God, your God, in curses or silly banter; God won't put up with the irreverant use of his name.
2_20_7.NIV: “You shall not misuse the name of the Lord your God, for the Lord will not hold anyone guiltless who misuses his name.
2_20_7.NRSVCE: You shall not make wrongful use of the name of the LORD your God, for the LORD will not acquit anyone who misuses his name.

2_20_8.NKJV: “Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_8.KJV: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_8.ESV: “Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_8.NASB: 'Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_8.NLT: “Remember to observe the Sabbath day by keeping it holy.
2_20_8.WEB: “Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_8.CSB17: Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy:
2_20_8.AMP: "Remember the Sabbath (seventh) day to keep it holy (set apart, dedicated to God).
2_20_8.MSG: Observe the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_8.NIV: “Remember the Sabbath day by keeping it holy.
2_20_8.NRSVCE: Remember the sabbath day, and keep it holy.

2_20_9.NKJV: Six days you shall labor and do all your work,
2_20_9.KJV: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work:
2_20_9.ESV: Six days you shall labor, and do all your work,
2_20_9.NASB: 'Six days you shall labor and do all your work,
2_20_9.NLT: You have six days each week for your ordinary work,
2_20_9.WEB: You shall labor six days, and do all your work,
2_20_9.CSB17: You are to labor six days and do all your work,
2_20_9.AMP: Six days you shall labor and do all your work,
2_20_9.MSG: Work six days and do everything you need to do.
2_20_9.NIV: Six days you shall labor and do all your work,
2_20_9.NRSVCE: Six days you shall labor and do all your work.

2_20_10.NKJV: but the seventh day is the Sabbath of the LORD your God. In it you shall do no work: you, nor your son, nor your daughter, nor your male servant, nor your female servant, nor your cattle, nor your stranger who is within your gates.
2_20_10.KJV: But the seventh day is the sabbath of the LORD thy God: in it thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates:
2_20_10.ESV: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to the LORD your God. On it you shall not do any work, you, or your son, or your daughter, your male servant, or your female servant, or your livestock, or the sojourner who is within your gates.
2_20_10.NASB: but the seventh day is a sabbath of the LORD your God; [in it] you shall not do any work, you or your son or your daughter, your male or your female servant or your cattle or your sojourner who stays with you.
2_20_10.NLT: but the seventh day is a Sabbath day of rest dedicated to the LORD your God. On that day no one in your household may do any work. This includes you, your sons and daughters, your male and female servants, your livestock, and any foreigners living among you.
2_20_10.WEB: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to Yahweh your God. You shall not do any work in it, you, nor your son, nor your daughter, your male servant, nor your female servant, nor your livestock, nor your stranger who is within your gates;
2_20_10.CSB17: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to the LORD your God. You must not do any work — you, your son or daughter, your male or female servant, your livestock, or the resident alien who is within your city gates.
2_20_10.AMP: but the seventh day is a Sabbath [a day of rest dedicated] to the LORD your God; on that day you shall not do any work, you or your son, or your daughter, or your male servant, or your female servant, or your livestock or the temporary resident (foreigner) who stays within your [city] gates.
2_20_10.MSG: But the seventh day is a Sabbath to God, your God. Don't do any work—not you, nor your son, nor your daughter, nor your servant, nor your maid, nor your animals, not even the foreign guest visiting in your town.
2_20_10.NIV: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to the Lord your God. On it you shall not do any work, neither you, nor your son or daughter, nor your manservant or maidservant, nor your animals, nor the alien within your gates.
2_20_10.NRSVCE: But the seventh day is a sabbath to the LORD your God; you shall not do any work — you, your son or your daughter, your male or female slave, your livestock, or the alien resident in your towns.

2_20_11.NKJV: For in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day. Therefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and hallowed it.
2_20_11.KJV: For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and rested the seventh day: wherefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
2_20_11.ESV: For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested on the seventh day. Therefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and made it holy.
2_20_11.NASB: 'For in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, the sea and all that is in them, and rested on the seventh day; therefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day and made it holy.
2_20_11.NLT: For in six days the LORD made the heavens, the earth, the sea, and everything in them; but on the seventh day he rested. That is why the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and set it apart as holy.
2_20_11.WEB: for in six days Yahweh made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day; therefore Yahweh blessed the Sabbath day, and made it holy.
2_20_11.CSB17: For the LORD made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and everything in them in six days; then he rested on the seventh day. Therefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and declared it holy.
2_20_11.AMP: For in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, the sea and everything that is in them, and He rested (ceased) on the seventh day. That is why the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and made it holy [that is, set it apart for His purposes].
2_20_11.MSG: For in six days God made Heaven, Earth, and sea, and everything in them; he rested on the seventh day. Therefore God blessed the Sabbath day; he set it apart as a holy day.
2_20_11.NIV: For in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and all that is in them, but he rested on the seventh day. Therefore the Lord blessed the Sabbath day and made it holy.
2_20_11.NRSVCE: For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, but rested the seventh day; therefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day and consecrated it.

2_20_12.NKJV: “Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long upon the land which the LORD your God is giving you.
2_20_12.KJV: Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long upon the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee.
2_20_12.ESV: “Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.
2_20_12.NASB: 'Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be prolonged in the land which the LORD your God gives you.
2_20_12.NLT: “Honor your father and mother. Then you will live a long, full life in the land the LORD your God is giving you.
2_20_12.WEB: “Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land which Yahweh your God gives you.
2_20_12.CSB17: Honor your father and your mother so that you may have a long life in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.
2_20_12.AMP: "Honor (respect, obey, care for) your father and your mother, so that your days may be prolonged in the land the LORD your God gives you.
2_20_12.MSG: Honor your father and mother so that you'll live a long time in the land that God, your God, is giving you.
2_20_12.NIV: “Honor your father and your mother, so that you may live long in the land the Lord your God is giving you.
2_20_12.NRSVCE: Honor your father and your mother, so that your days may be long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

2_20_13.AMP: "You shall not commit murder (unjustified, deliberate homicide).

2_20_15.AMP: "You shall not steal [secretly, openly, fraudulently, or through carelessness].

2_20_16.NKJV: “You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
2_20_16.KJV: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
2_20_16.ESV: “You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
2_20_16.NASB: 'You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
2_20_16.NLT: “You must not testify falsely against your neighbor.
2_20_16.WEB: “You shall not give false testimony against your neighbor.
2_20_16.CSB17: Do not give false testimony against your neighbor.
2_20_16.AMP: "You shall not testify falsely [that is, lie, withhold, or manipulate the truth] against your neighbor (any person).
2_20_16.NIV: “You shall not give false testimony against your neighbor.
2_20_16.NRSVCE: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.

2_20_17.NKJV: “You shall not covet your neighbor’s house; you shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, nor his male servant, nor his female servant, nor his ox, nor his donkey, nor anything that is your neighbor’s.”
2_20_17.KJV: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's wife, nor his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour's.
2_20_17.ESV: “You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, or his male servant, or his female servant, or his ox, or his donkey, or anything that is your neighbor's.”
2_20_17.NASB: 'You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife or his male servant or his female servant or his ox or his donkey or anything that belongs to your neighbor.'
2_20_17.NLT: “You must not covet your neighbor’s house. You must not covet your neighbor’s wife, male or female servant, ox or donkey, or anything else that belongs to your neighbor.”
2_20_17.WEB: “You shall not covet your neighbor’s house. You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, nor his male servant, nor his female servant, nor his ox, nor his donkey, nor anything that is your neighbor’s.”
2_20_17.CSB17: Do not covet your neighbor’s house. Do not covet your neighbor’s wife, his male or female servant, his ox or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.
2_20_17.AMP: "You shall not covet [that is, selfishly desire and attempt to acquire] your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, or his male servant, or his female servant, or his ox, or his donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor."
2_20_17.MSG: No lusting after your neighbor's house—or wife or servant or maid or ox or donkey. Don't set your heart on anything that is your neighbor's.
2_20_17.NIV: “You shall not covet your neighbor’s house. You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, or his manservant or maidservant, his ox or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.”
2_20_17.NRSVCE: You shall not covet your neighbor’s house; you shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, or male or female slave, or ox, or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.

2_20_18.NKJV: Now all the people witnessed the thunderings, the lightning flashes, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled and stood afar off.
2_20_18.KJV: And all the people saw the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the noise of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking: and when the people saw it, they removed, and stood afar off.
2_20_18.ESV: Now when all the people saw the thunder and the flashes of lightning and the sound of the trumpet and the mountain smoking, the people were afraid and trembled, and they stood far off
2_20_18.NASB: All the people perceived the thunder and the lightning flashes and the sound of the trumpet and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw [it], they trembled and stood at a distance.
2_20_18.NLT: When the people heard the thunder and the loud blast of the ram’s horn, and when they saw the flashes of lightning and the smoke billowing from the mountain, they stood at a distance, trembling with fear.
2_20_18.WEB: All the people perceived the thunderings, the lightnings, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking. When the people saw it, they trembled, and stayed at a distance.
2_20_18.CSB17: All the people witnessed the thunder and lightning, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain surrounded by smoke. When the people saw it they trembled and stood at a distance.
2_20_18.AMP: Now all the people witnessed the thunder and the flashes of lightning and the sound of the trumpet and the smoking mountain; and as they looked, the people were afraid, and they trembled [and moved backward] and stood at a [safe] distance.
2_20_18.MSG: All the people, experiencing the thunder and lightning, the trumpet blast and the smoking mountain, were afraid—they pulled back and stood at a distance.
2_20_18.NIV: When the people saw the thunder and lightning and heard the trumpet and saw the mountain in smoke, they trembled with fear. They stayed at a distance
2_20_18.NRSVCE: When all the people witnessed the thunder and lightning, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking, they were afraid and trembled and stood at a distance,

2_20_19.NKJV: Then they said to Moses, “You speak with us, and we will hear; but let not God speak with us, lest we die.”
2_20_19.KJV: And they said unto Moses, Speak thou with us, and we will hear: but let not God speak with us, lest we die.
2_20_19.ESV: and said to Moses, “You speak to us, and we will listen; but do not let God speak to us, lest we die.”
2_20_19.NASB: Then they said to Moses, 'Speak to us yourself and we will listen; but let not God speak to us, or we will die.'
2_20_19.NLT: And they said to Moses, “You speak to us, and we will listen. But don’t let God speak directly to us, or we will die!”
2_20_19.WEB: They said to Moses, “Speak with us yourself, and we will listen; but don’t let God speak with us, lest we die.”
2_20_19.CSB17: “You speak to us, and we will listen,” they said to Moses, “but don’t let God speak to us, or we will die.”
2_20_19.AMP: Then they said to Moses, "You speak to us and we will listen, but do not let God speak to us or we will die."
2_20_19.MSG: They said to Moses, "You speak to us and we'll listen, but don't have God speak to us or we'll die."
2_20_19.NIV: and said to Moses, “Speak to us yourself and we will listen. But do not have God speak to us or we will die.”
2_20_19.NRSVCE: and said to Moses, “You speak to us, and we will listen; but do not let God speak to us, or we will die.”

2_20_20.NKJV: And Moses said to the people, “Do not fear; for God has come to test you, and that His fear may be before you, so that you may not sin.”
2_20_20.KJV: And Moses said unto the people, Fear not: for God is come to prove you, and that his fear may be before your faces, that ye sin not.
2_20_20.ESV: Moses said to the people, “Do not fear, for God has come to test you, that the fear of him may be before you, that you may not sin.”
2_20_20.NASB: Moses said to the people, 'Do not be afraid; for God has come in order to test you, and in order that the fear of Him may remain with you, so that you may not sin.'
2_20_20.NLT: “Don’t be afraid,” Moses answered them, “for God has come in this way to test you, and so that your fear of him will keep you from sinning!”
2_20_20.WEB: Moses said to the people, “Don’t be afraid, for God has come to test you, and that his fear may be before you, that you won’t sin.”
2_20_20.CSB17: Moses responded to the people, “Don’t be afraid, for God has come to test you, so that you will fear him and will not sin.”
2_20_20.AMP: Moses said to the people, "Do not be afraid; for God has come in order to test you, and in order that the fear of Him [that is, a profound reverence for Him] will remain with you, so that you do not sin."
2_20_20.MSG: Moses spoke to the people: "Don't be afraid. God has come to test you and instill a deep and reverent awe within you so that you won't sin."
2_20_20.NIV: Moses said to the people, “Do not be afraid. God has come to test you, so that the fear of God will be with you to keep you from sinning.”
2_20_20.NRSVCE: Moses said to the people, “Do not be afraid; for God has come only to test you and to put the fear of him upon you so that you do not sin.”

2_20_21.NKJV: So the people stood afar off, but Moses drew near the thick darkness where God was.
2_20_21.KJV: And the people stood afar off, and Moses drew near unto the thick darkness where God was.
2_20_21.ESV: The people stood far off, while Moses drew near to the thick darkness where God was.
2_20_21.NASB: So the people stood at a distance, while Moses approached the thick cloud where God [was].
2_20_21.NLT: As the people stood in the distance, Moses approached the dark cloud where God was.
2_20_21.WEB: The people stayed at a distance, and Moses came near to the thick darkness where God was.
2_20_21.CSB17: And the people remained standing at a distance as Moses approached the total darkness where God was.
2_20_21.AMP: So the people stood at a [safe] distance, but Moses approached the thick cloud where God was.
2_20_21.MSG: The people kept their distance while Moses approached the thick cloud where God was.
2_20_21.NIV: The people remained at a distance, while Moses approached the thick darkness where God was.
2_20_21.NRSVCE: Then the people stood at a distance, while Moses drew near to the thick darkness where God was.

2_20_22.NKJV: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Thus you shall say to the children of Israel: ‘You have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
2_20_22.KJV: And the LORD said unto Moses, Thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel, Ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
2_20_22.ESV: And the LORD said to Moses, “Thus you shall say to the people of Israel: ‘You have seen for yourselves that I have talked with you from heaven.
2_20_22.NASB: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Thus you shall say to the sons of Israel, 'You yourselves have seen that I have spoken to you from heaven.
2_20_22.NLT: And the LORD said to Moses, “Say this to the people of Israel: You saw for yourselves that I spoke to you from heaven.
2_20_22.WEB: Yahweh said to Moses, “This is what you shall tell the children of Israel: ‘You yourselves have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
2_20_22.CSB17: Then the LORD told Moses, “This is what you are to say to the Israelites: You have seen that I have spoken to you from heaven.
2_20_22.AMP: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Thus you shall say to the Israelites, 'You have seen for yourselves that I have spoken to you from heaven.
2_20_22.MSG: God said to Moses, "Give this Message to the People of Israel: 'You've experienced firsthand how I spoke with you from Heaven.
2_20_22.NIV: Idols and AltarsThen the Lord said to Moses, “Tell the Israelites this: ‘You have seen for yourselves that I have spoken to you from heaven:
2_20_22.NRSVCE: The LORD said to Moses: Thus you shall say to the Israelites: “You have seen for yourselves that I spoke with you from heaven.

2_20_23.NKJV: You shall not make anything to be with Me—gods of silver or gods of gold you shall not make for yourselves.
2_20_23.KJV: Ye shall not make with me gods of silver, neither shall ye make unto you gods of gold.
2_20_23.ESV: You shall not make gods of silver to be with me, nor shall you make for yourselves gods of gold.
2_20_23.NASB: 'You shall not make [other gods] besides Me; gods of silver or gods of gold, you shall not make for yourselves.
2_20_23.NLT: Remember, you must not make any idols of silver or gold to rival me.
2_20_23.WEB: You shall most certainly not make alongside of me gods of silver, or gods of gold for yourselves.
2_20_23.CSB17: Do not make gods of silver to rival me; do not make gods of gold for yourselves.
2_20_23.AMP: You shall not make other gods [to worship] besides Me; gods of silver or gods of gold, you shall not make [these lifeless idols] for yourselves.
2_20_23.MSG: Don't make gods of silver and gods of gold and then set them alongside me.
2_20_23.NIV: Do not make any gods to be alongside me; do not make for yourselves gods of silver or gods of gold.
2_20_23.NRSVCE: You shall not make gods of silver alongside me, nor shall you make for yourselves gods of gold.

2_20_24.NKJV: An altar of earth you shall make for Me, and you shall sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I record My name I will come to you, and I will bless you.
2_20_24.KJV: An altar of earth thou shalt make unto me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt offerings, and thy peace offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen: in all places where I record my name I will come unto thee, and I will bless thee.
2_20_24.ESV: An altar of earth you shall make for me and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I cause my name to be remembered I will come to you and bless you.
2_20_24.NASB: 'You shall make an altar of earth for Me, and you shall sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen; in every place where I cause My name to be remembered, I will come to you and bless you.
2_20_24.NLT: “Build for me an altar made of earth, and offer your sacrifices to me — your burnt offerings and peace offerings, your sheep and goats, and your cattle. Build my altar wherever I cause my name to be remembered, and I will come to you and bless you.
2_20_24.WEB: You shall make an altar of earth for me, and shall sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your cattle. In every place where I record my name I will come to you and I will bless you.
2_20_24.CSB17: “Make an earthen altar for me, and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and fellowship offerings, your flocks and herds. I will come to you and bless you in every place where I cause my name to be remembered.
2_20_24.AMP: You shall make an altar of earth for Me, and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I cause My Name to be recorded and remembered [through revelation of My divine nature] I will come to you and bless you.
2_20_24.MSG: Make me an earthen Altar. Sacrifice your Whole-Burnt-Offerings, your Peace-Offerings, your sheep, and your cattle on it. Every place where I cause my name to be honored in your worship, I'll be there myself and bless you.
2_20_24.NIV: “‘Make an altar of earth for me and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and fellowship offerings, your sheep and goats and your cattle. Wherever I cause my name to be honored, I will come to you and bless you.
2_20_24.NRSVCE: You need make for me only an altar of earth and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your offerings of well-being, your sheep and your oxen; in every place where I cause my name to be remembered I will come to you and bless you.

2_20_25.NKJV: And if you make Me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of hewn stone; for if you use your tool on it, you have profaned it.
2_20_25.KJV: And if thou wilt make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone: for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast polluted it. build: Heb. build them with hewing
2_20_25.ESV: If you make me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of hewn stones, for if you wield your tool on it you profane it.
2_20_25.NASB: 'If you make an altar of stone for Me, you shall not build it of cut stones, for if you wield your tool on it, you will profane it.
2_20_25.NLT: If you use stones to build my altar, use only natural, uncut stones. Do not shape the stones with a tool, for that would make the altar unfit for holy use.
2_20_25.WEB: If you make me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of cut stones; for if you lift up your tool on it, you have polluted it.
2_20_25.CSB17: If you make a stone altar for me, do not build it out of cut stones. If you use your chisel on it, you will defile it.
2_20_25.AMP: If you make an altar of stone for Me, you shall not build it of cut stones, for if you use a chisel on it, you will profane it.
2_20_25.MSG: If you use stones to make my Altar, don't use dressed stones. If you use a chisel on the stones you'll profane the Altar.
2_20_25.NIV: If you make an altar of stones for me, do not build it with dressed stones, for you will defile it if you use a tool on it.
2_20_25.NRSVCE: But if you make for me an altar of stone, do not build it of hewn stones; for if you use a chisel upon it you profane it.

2_20_26.NKJV: Nor shall you go up by steps to My altar, that your nakedness may not be exposed on it.’
2_20_26.KJV: Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar, that thy nakedness be not discovered thereon.
2_20_26.ESV: And you shall not go up by steps to my altar, that your nakedness be not exposed on it.’
2_20_26.NASB: 'And you shall not go up by steps to My altar, so that your nakedness will not be exposed on it.'
2_20_26.NLT: And do not approach my altar by going up steps. If you do, someone might look up under your clothing and see your nakedness.
2_20_26.WEB: You shall not go up by steps to my altar, that your nakedness may not be exposed to it.’
2_20_26.CSB17: Do not go up to my altar on steps, so that your nakedness is not exposed on it.
2_20_26.AMP: Nor shall you go up to My altar on steps, so that your nakedness will not be exposed on it.'
2_20_26.MSG: Don't use steps to climb to my Altar because that will expose your nakedness.'
2_20_26.NIV: And do not go up to my altar on steps, lest your nakedness be exposed on it.’
2_20_26.NRSVCE: You shall not go up by steps to my altar, so that your nakedness may not be exposed on it.”

2_21_1.NKJV: “Now these are the judgments which you shall set before them:
2_21_1.KJV: Now these are the judgments which thou shalt set before them.
2_21_1.ESV: “Now these are the rules that you shall set before them.
2_21_1.NASB: 'Now these are the ordinances which you are to set before them:
2_21_1.NLT: “These are the regulations you must present to Israel.
2_21_1.WEB: “Now these are the ordinances which you shall set before them.
2_21_1.CSB17: “These are the ordinances that you are to set before them:
2_21_1.AMP: "Now these are the ordinances (laws) which you shall set before the Israelites:
2_21_1.MSG: "These are the laws that you are to place before them:
2_21_1.NIV: “These are the laws you are to set before them:
2_21_1.NRSVCE: These are the ordinances that you shall set before them:

2_21_2.NKJV: If you buy a Hebrew servant, he shall serve six years; and in the seventh he shall go out free and pay nothing.
2_21_2.KJV: If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve: and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
2_21_2.ESV: When you buy a Hebrew slave, he shall serve six years, and in the seventh he shall go out free, for nothing.
2_21_2.NASB: 'If you buy a Hebrew slave, he shall serve for six years; but on the seventh he shall go out as a free man without payment.
2_21_2.NLT: “If you buy a Hebrew slave, he may serve for no more than six years. Set him free in the seventh year, and he will owe you nothing for his freedom.
2_21_2.WEB: “If you buy a Hebrew servant, he shall serve six years and in the seventh he shall go out free without paying anything.
2_21_2.CSB17: “When you buy a Hebrew slave, he is to serve for six years; then in the seventh he is to leave as a free man without paying anything.
2_21_2.AMP: "If you purchase a Hebrew servant [because of his debt or poverty], he shall serve six years, and in the seventh [year] he shall leave as a free man, paying nothing.
2_21_2.MSG: "When you buy a Hebrew slave, he will serve six years. The seventh year he goes free, for nothing.
2_21_2.NIV: Hebrew Servants“If you buy a Hebrew servant, he is to serve you for six years. But in the seventh year, he shall go free, without paying anything.
2_21_2.NRSVCE: When you buy a male Hebrew slave, he shall serve six years, but in the seventh he shall go out a free person, without debt.

2_21_3.NKJV: If he comes in by himself, he shall go out by himself; if he comes in married, then his wife shall go out with him.
2_21_3.KJV: If he came in by himself, he shall go out by himself: if he were married , then his wife shall go out with him. by himself: Heb. with his body
2_21_3.ESV: If he comes in single, he shall go out single; if he comes in married, then his wife shall go out with him.
2_21_3.NASB: 'If he comes alone, he shall go out alone; if he is the husband of a wife, then his wife shall go out with him.
2_21_3.NLT: If he was single when he became your slave, he shall leave single. But if he was married before he became a slave, then his wife must be freed with him.
2_21_3.WEB: If he comes in by himself, he shall go out by himself. If he is married, then his wife shall go out with him.
2_21_3.CSB17: If he arrives alone, he is to leave alone; if he arrives with a wife, his wife is to leave with him.
2_21_3.AMP: If he came [to you] alone, he shall leave alone; if he came married, then his wife shall leave with him.
2_21_3.MSG: If he came in single he leaves single. If he came in married he leaves with his wife.
2_21_3.NIV: If he comes alone, he is to go free alone; but if he has a wife when he comes, she is to go with him.
2_21_3.NRSVCE: If he comes in single, he shall go out single; if he comes in married, then his wife shall go out with him.

2_21_4.NKJV: If his master has given him a wife, and she has borne him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
2_21_4.KJV: If his master have given him a wife, and she have born him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master's, and he shall go out by himself.
2_21_4.ESV: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master's, and he shall go out alone.
2_21_4.NASB: 'If his master gives him a wife, and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall belong to her master, and he shall go out alone.
2_21_4.NLT: “If his master gave him a wife while he was a slave and they had sons or daughters, then only the man will be free in the seventh year, but his wife and children will still belong to his master.
2_21_4.WEB: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
2_21_4.CSB17: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children belong to her master, and the man must leave alone.
2_21_4.AMP: If his master gives him a wife, and she gives birth to sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall belong to her master, and he shall leave [your service] alone.
2_21_4.MSG: If the master gives him a wife and she gave him sons and daughters, the wife and children stay with the master and he leaves by himself.
2_21_4.NIV: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the woman and her children shall belong to her master, and only the man shall go free.
2_21_4.NRSVCE: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master’s and he shall go out alone.

2_21_5.NKJV: But if the servant plainly says, ‘I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free,’
2_21_5.KJV: And if the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free: shall: Heb. saying shall say
2_21_5.ESV: But if the slave plainly says, ‘I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free,’
2_21_5.NASB: 'But if the slave plainly says, 'I love my master, my wife and my children; I will not go out as a free man,'
2_21_5.NLT: But the slave may declare, ‘I love my master, my wife, and my children. I don’t want to go free.’
2_21_5.WEB: But if the servant shall plainly say, ‘I love my master, my wife, and my children. I will not go out free;’
2_21_5.CSB17: “But if the slave declares, ‘I love my master, my wife, and my children; I do not want to leave as a free man,’
2_21_5.AMP: But if the servant plainly says, 'I love my master, my wife and my children; I will not leave as a free man,'
2_21_5.MSG: But suppose the slave should say, 'I love my master and my wife and children—I don't want my freedom,'
2_21_5.NIV: “But if the servant declares, ‘I love my master and my wife and children and do not want to go free,’
2_21_5.NRSVCE: But if the slave declares, “I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out a free person,”

2_21_6.NKJV: then his master shall bring him to the judges. He shall also bring him to the door, or to the doorpost, and his master shall pierce his ear with an awl; and he shall serve him forever.
2_21_6.KJV: Then his master shall bring him unto the judges; he shall also bring him to the door, or unto the door post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an aul; and he shall serve him for ever.
2_21_6.ESV: then his master shall bring him to God, and he shall bring him to the door or the doorpost. And his master shall bore his ear through with an awl, and he shall be his slave forever.
2_21_6.NASB: then his master shall bring him to God, then he shall bring him to the door or the doorpost. And his master shall pierce his ear with an awl; and he shall serve him permanently.
2_21_6.NLT: If he does this, his master must present him before God. Then his master must take him to the door or doorpost and publicly pierce his ear with an awl. After that, the slave will serve his master for life.
2_21_6.WEB: then his master shall bring him to God, and shall bring him to the door or to the doorpost, and his master shall bore his ear through with an awl, and he shall serve him for ever.
2_21_6.CSB17: his master is to bring him to the judges and then bring him to the door or doorpost. His master will pierce his ear with an awl, and he will serve his master for life.
2_21_6.AMP: then his master shall bring him to God [that is, to the judges who act in God's name], then he shall bring him to the door or doorpost. And his master shall pierce his ear with an awl (strong needle); and he shall serve him for life.
2_21_6.MSG: then his master is to bring him before God and to a door or doorpost and pierce his ear with an awl, a sign that he is a slave for life.
2_21_6.NIV: then his master must take him before the judges. He shall take him to the door or the doorpost and pierce his ear with an awl. Then he will be his servant for life.
2_21_6.NRSVCE: then his master shall bring him before God. He shall be brought to the door or the doorpost; and his master shall pierce his ear with an awl; and he shall serve him for life.

2_21_7.NKJV: “And if a man sells his daughter to be a female slave, she shall not go out as the male slaves do.
2_21_7.KJV: And if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do.
2_21_7.ESV: “When a man sells his daughter as a slave, she shall not go out as the male slaves do.
2_21_7.NASB: 'If a man sells his daughter as a female slave, she is not to go free as the male slaves do.
2_21_7.NLT: “When a man sells his daughter as a slave, she will not be freed at the end of six years as the men are.
2_21_7.WEB: “If a man sells his daughter to be a female servant, she shall not go out as the male servants do.
2_21_7.CSB17: “When a man sells his daughter as a concubine, she is not to leave as the male slaves do.
2_21_7.AMP: "If a man sells his daughter to be a female servant, she shall not go free [after six years] as male servants do.
2_21_7.MSG: "When a man sells his daughter to be a handmaid, she doesn't go free after six years like the men.
2_21_7.NIV: “If a man sells his daughter as a servant, she is not to go free as menservants do.
2_21_7.NRSVCE: When a man sells his daughter as a slave, she shall not go out as the male slaves do.

2_21_8.NKJV: If she does not please her master, who has betrothed her to himself, then he shall let her be redeemed. He shall have no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has dealt deceitfully with her.
2_21_8.KJV: If she please not her master, who hath betrothed her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed: to sell her unto a strange nation he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her. please: Heb. be evil in the eyes of, etc
2_21_8.ESV: If she does not please her master, who has designated her for himself, then he shall let her be redeemed. He shall have no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has broken faith with her.
2_21_8.NASB: 'If she is displeasing in the eyes of her master who designated her for himself, then he shall let her be redeemed. He does not have authority to sell her to a foreign people because of his unfairness to her.
2_21_8.NLT: If she does not satisfy her owner, he must allow her to be bought back again. But he is not allowed to sell her to foreigners, since he is the one who broke the contract with her.
2_21_8.WEB: If she doesn’t please her master, who has married her to himself, then he shall let her be redeemed. He shall have no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has dealt deceitfully with her.
2_21_8.CSB17: If she is displeasing to her master, who chose her for himself, then he must let her be redeemed. He has no right to sell her to foreigners because he has acted treacherously toward her.
2_21_8.AMP: If she does not please her master who has chosen her for himself [as a wife], he shall let her be redeemed [by her family]. He does not have the authority to sell her to a foreign people, because he has been unfair to her.
2_21_8.MSG: If she doesn't please her master, her family must buy her back; her master doesn't have the right to sell her to foreigners since he broke his word to her.
2_21_8.NIV: If she does not please the master who has selected her for himself, he must let her be redeemed. He has no right to sell her to foreigners, because he has broken faith with her.
2_21_8.NRSVCE: If she does not please her master, who designated her for himself, then he shall let her be redeemed; he shall have no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has dealt unfairly with her.

2_21_9.NKJV: And if he has betrothed her to his son, he shall deal with her according to the custom of daughters.
2_21_9.KJV: And if he have betrothed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.
2_21_9.ESV: If he designates her for his son, he shall deal with her as with a daughter.
2_21_9.NASB: 'If he designates her for his son, he shall deal with her according to the custom of daughters.
2_21_9.NLT: But if the slave’s owner arranges for her to marry his son, he may no longer treat her as a slave but as a daughter.
2_21_9.WEB: If he marries her to his son, he shall deal with her as a daughter.
2_21_9.CSB17: Or if he chooses her for his son, he must deal with her according to the customary treatment of daughters.
2_21_9.AMP: If her master chooses her [as a wife] for his son, he shall act toward her as if she were legally his daughter.
2_21_9.MSG: If he turns her over to his son, he has to treat her like a daughter.
2_21_9.NIV: If he selects her for his son, he must grant her the rights of a daughter.
2_21_9.NRSVCE: If he designates her for his son, he shall deal with her as with a daughter.

2_21_10.NKJV: If he takes another wife, he shall not diminish her food, her clothing, and her marriage rights.
2_21_10.KJV: If he take him another wife; her food, her raiment, and her duty of marriage, shall he not diminish.
2_21_10.ESV: If he takes another wife to himself, he shall not diminish her food, her clothing, or her marital rights.
2_21_10.NASB: 'If he takes to himself another woman, he may not reduce her food, her clothing, or her conjugal rights.
2_21_10.NLT: “If a man who has married a slave wife takes another wife for himself, he must not neglect the rights of the first wife to food, clothing, and sexual intimacy.
2_21_10.WEB: If he takes another wife to himself, he shall not diminish her food, her clothing, and her marital rights.
2_21_10.CSB17: If he takes an additional wife, he must not reduce the food, clothing, or marital rights of the first wife.
2_21_10.AMP: If her master marries another wife, he may not reduce her food, her clothing, or her privilege as a wife.
2_21_10.MSG: If he marries another woman, she retains all her full rights to meals, clothing, and marital relations.
2_21_10.NIV: If he marries another woman, he must not deprive the first one of her food, clothing and marital rights.
2_21_10.NRSVCE: If he takes another wife to himself, he shall not diminish the food, clothing, or marital rights of the first wife.

2_21_11.NKJV: And if he does not do these three for her, then she shall go out free, without paying money.
2_21_11.KJV: And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out free without money.
2_21_11.ESV: And if he does not do these three things for her, she shall go out for nothing, without payment of money.
2_21_11.NASB: 'If he will not do these three [things] for her, then she shall go out for nothing, without [payment of] money.
2_21_11.NLT: If he fails in any of these three obligations, she may leave as a free woman without making any payment.
2_21_11.WEB: If he doesn’t do these three things for her, she may go free without paying any money.
2_21_11.CSB17: And if he does not do these three things for her, she may leave free of charge, without any payment.
2_21_11.AMP: If he does not do these three things for her, then shall she leave free, without payment of money.
2_21_11.MSG: If he won't do any of these three things for her, she goes free, for nothing.
2_21_11.NIV: If he does not provide her with these three things, she is to go free, without any payment of money.
2_21_11.NRSVCE: And if he does not do these three things for her, she shall go out without debt, without payment of money.

2_21_12.NKJV: “He who strikes a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death.
2_21_12.KJV: He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall be surely put to death.
2_21_12.ESV: “Whoever strikes a man so that he dies shall be put to death.
2_21_12.NASB: 'He who strikes a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death.
2_21_12.NLT: “Anyone who assaults and kills another person must be put to death.
2_21_12.WEB: “One who strikes a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death,
2_21_12.CSB17: “Whoever strikes a person so that he dies must be put to death.
2_21_12.AMP: "Whoever strikes a man so that he dies must be put to death.
2_21_12.MSG: "If someone hits another and death results, the penalty is death.
2_21_12.NIV: Personal Injuries“Anyone who strikes a man and kills him shall surely be put to death.
2_21_12.NRSVCE: Whoever strikes a person mortally shall be put to death.

2_21_13.NKJV: However, if he did not lie in wait, but God delivered him into his hand, then I will appoint for you a place where he may flee.
2_21_13.KJV: And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver him into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.
2_21_13.ESV: But if he did not lie in wait for him, but God let him fall into his hand, then I will appoint for you a place to which he may flee.
2_21_13.NASB: 'But if he did not lie in wait [for him], but God let [him] fall into his hand, then I will appoint you a place to which he may flee.
2_21_13.NLT: But if it was simply an accident permitted by God, I will appoint a place of refuge where the slayer can run for safety.
2_21_13.WEB: but not if it is unintentional, but God allows it to happen: then I will appoint you a place where he shall flee.
2_21_13.CSB17: But if he did not intend any harm, and yet God allowed it to happen, I will appoint a place for you where he may flee.
2_21_13.AMP: However, if he did not lie in wait [for him], but God allowed him to fall into his hand, then I will establish for you a place to which he may escape [for protection until duly tried].
2_21_13.MSG: But if there was no intent to kill—if it was an accident, an 'act of God'—I'll set aside a place to which the killer can flee for refuge.
2_21_13.NIV: However, if he does not do it intentionally, but God lets it happen, he is to flee to a place I will designate.
2_21_13.NRSVCE: If it was not premeditated, but came about by an act of God, then I will appoint for you a place to which the killer may flee.

2_21_14.NKJV: “But if a man acts with premeditation against his neighbor, to kill him by treachery, you shall take him from My altar, that he may die.
2_21_14.KJV: But if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.
2_21_14.ESV: But if a man willfully attacks another to kill him by cunning, you shall take him from my altar, that he may die.
2_21_14.NASB: 'If, however, a man acts presumptuously toward his neighbor, so as to kill him craftily, you are to take him [even] from My altar, that he may die.
2_21_14.NLT: However, if someone deliberately kills another person, then the slayer must be dragged even from my altar and be put to death.
2_21_14.WEB: If a man schemes and comes presumptuously on his neighbor to kill him, you shall take him from my altar, that he may die.
2_21_14.CSB17: If a person schemes and willfully acts against his neighbor to murder him, you must take him from my altar to be put to death.
2_21_14.AMP: But if a man acts intentionally against another and kills him by [design through] treachery, you are to take him from My altar [to which he may have fled for protection], so that he may be put to death.
2_21_14.MSG: But if the murder was premeditated, cunningly plotted, then drag the killer away, even if it's from my Altar, to be put to death.
2_21_14.NIV: But if a man schemes and kills another man deliberately, take him away from my altar and put him to death.
2_21_14.NRSVCE: But if someone willfully attacks and kills another by treachery, you shall take the killer from my altar for execution.

2_21_15.NKJV: “And he who strikes his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
2_21_15.KJV: And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death.
2_21_15.ESV: “Whoever strikes his father or his mother shall be put to death.
2_21_15.NASB: 'He who strikes his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
2_21_15.NLT: “Anyone who strikes father or mother must be put to death.
2_21_15.WEB: “Anyone who attacks his father or his mother shall be surely put to death.
2_21_15.CSB17: “Whoever strikes his father or his mother must be put to death.
2_21_15.AMP: "Whoever strikes his father or his mother must be put to death.
2_21_15.MSG: "If someone hits father or mother, the penalty is death.
2_21_15.NIV: “Anyone who attacks his father or his mother must be put to death.
2_21_15.NRSVCE: Whoever strikes father or mother shall be put to death.

2_21_16.NKJV: “He who kidnaps a man and sells him, or if he is found in his hand, shall surely be put to death.
2_21_16.KJV: And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
2_21_16.ESV: “Whoever steals a man and sells him, and anyone found in possession of him, shall be put to death.
2_21_16.NASB: 'He who kidnaps a man, whether he sells him or he is found in his possession, shall surely be put to death.
2_21_16.NLT: “Kidnappers must be put to death, whether they are caught in possession of their victims or have already sold them as slaves.
2_21_16.WEB: “Anyone who kidnaps someone and sells him, or if he is found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
2_21_16.CSB17: “Whoever kidnaps a person must be put to death, whether he sells him or the person is found in his possession.
2_21_16.AMP: "Whoever kidnaps a man, whether he sells him or is found with him in his possession, must be put to death.
2_21_16.MSG: "If someone kidnaps a person, the penalty is death, regardless of whether the person has been sold or is still held in possession.
2_21_16.NIV: “Anyone who kidnaps another and either sells him or still has him when he is caught must be put to death.
2_21_16.NRSVCE: Whoever kidnaps a person, whether that person has been sold or is still held in possession, shall be put to death.

2_21_17.NKJV: “And he who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
2_21_17.KJV: And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall surely be put to death. curseth: or, revileth
2_21_17.ESV: “Whoever curses his father or his mother shall be put to death.
2_21_17.NASB: 'He who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
2_21_17.NLT: “Anyone who dishonors father or mother must be put to death.
2_21_17.WEB: “Anyone who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
2_21_17.CSB17: “Whoever curses his father or his mother must be put to death.
2_21_17.AMP: "Whoever curses his father or his mother or treats them contemptuously must be put to death.
2_21_17.MSG: "If someone curses father or mother, the penalty is death.
2_21_17.NIV: “Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.
2_21_17.NRSVCE: Whoever curses father or mother shall be put to death.

2_21_18.NKJV: “If men contend with each other, and one strikes the other with a stone or with his fist, and he does not die but is confined to his bed,
2_21_18.KJV: And if men strive together, and one smite another with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keepeth his bed: another: or, his neighbour
2_21_18.ESV: “When men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or with his fist and the man does not die but takes to his bed,
2_21_18.NASB: 'If men have a quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or with [his] fist, and he does not die but remains in bed,
2_21_18.NLT: “Now suppose two men quarrel, and one hits the other with a stone or fist, and the injured person does not die but is confined to bed.
2_21_18.WEB: “If men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone, or with his fist, and he doesn’t die, but is confined to bed;
2_21_18.CSB17: “When men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or his fist, and the injured man does not die but is confined to bed,
2_21_18.AMP: "If men quarrel and one strikes another with a stone or with his fist, and he does not die but is confined to bed,
2_21_18.MSG: "If a quarrel breaks out and one hits the other with a rock or a fist and the injured one doesn't die but is confined to bed
2_21_18.NIV: “If men quarrel and one hits the other with a stone or with his fist and he does not die but is confined to bed,
2_21_18.NRSVCE: When individuals quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or fist so that the injured party, though not dead, is confined to bed,

2_21_19.NKJV: if he rises again and walks about outside with his staff, then he who struck him shall be acquitted. He shall only pay for the loss of his time, and shall provide for him to be thoroughly healed.
2_21_19.KJV: If he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit: only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed. the loss: Heb. his ceasing
2_21_19.ESV: then if the man rises again and walks outdoors with his staff, he who struck him shall be clear; only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall have him thoroughly healed.
2_21_19.NASB: if he gets up and walks around outside on his staff, then he who struck him shall go unpunished; he shall only pay for his loss of time, and shall take care of him until he is completely healed.
2_21_19.NLT: If he is later able to walk outside again, even with a crutch, the assailant will not be punished but must compensate his victim for lost wages and provide for his full recovery.
2_21_19.WEB: if he rises again and walks around with his staff, then he who struck him shall be cleared: only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall provide for his healing until he is thoroughly healed.
2_21_19.CSB17: if he can later get up and walk around outside leaning on his staff, then the one who struck him will be exempt from punishment. Nevertheless, he must pay for his lost work time and provide for his complete recovery.
2_21_19.AMP: if he gets up and walks around leaning on his cane, then the one who struck him shall be left [physically] unpunished; he must only pay for his loss of time [at work], and the costs [of treatment and recuperation] until he is thoroughly healed.
2_21_19.MSG: and then later gets better and can get about on a crutch, the one who hit him is in the clear, except to pay for the loss of time and make sure of complete recovery.
2_21_19.NIV: the one who struck the blow will not be held responsible if the other gets up and walks around outside with his staff; however, he must pay the injured man for the loss of his time and see that he is completely healed.
2_21_19.NRSVCE: but recovers and walks around outside with the help of a staff, then the assailant shall be free of liability, except to pay for the loss of time, and to arrange for full recovery.

2_21_20.NKJV: “And if a man beats his male or female servant with a rod, so that he dies under his hand, he shall surely be punished.
2_21_20.KJV: And if a man smite his servant, or his maid, with a rod, and he die under his hand; he shall be surely punished. punished: Heb. avenged
2_21_20.ESV: “When a man strikes his slave, male or female, with a rod and the slave dies under his hand, he shall be avenged.
2_21_20.NASB: 'If a man strikes his male or female slave with a rod and he dies at his hand, he shall be punished.
2_21_20.NLT: “If a man beats his male or female slave with a club and the slave dies as a result, the owner must be punished.
2_21_20.WEB: “If a man strikes his servant or his maid with a rod, and he dies under his hand, he shall surely be punished.
2_21_20.CSB17: “When a man strikes his male or female slave with a rod, and the slave dies under his abuse, the owner must be punished.
2_21_20.AMP: "If a man strikes his male or his female servant with a staff and the servant dies at his hand, he must be punished.
2_21_20.MSG: "If a slave owner hits a slave, male or female, with a stick and the slave dies on the spot, the slave must be avenged.
2_21_20.NIV: “If a man beats his male or female slave with a rod and the slave dies as a direct result, he must be punished,
2_21_20.NRSVCE: When a slaveowner strikes a male or female slave with a rod and the slave dies immediately, the owner shall be punished.

2_21_21.NKJV: Notwithstanding, if he remains alive a day or two, he shall not be punished; for he is his property.
2_21_21.KJV: Notwithstanding, if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished: for he is his money.
2_21_21.ESV: But if the slave survives a day or two, he is not to be avenged, for the slave is his money.
2_21_21.NASB: 'If, however, he survives a day or two, no vengeance shall be taken; for he is his property.
2_21_21.NLT: But if the slave recovers within a day or two, then the owner shall not be punished, since the slave is his property.
2_21_21.WEB: Notwithstanding, if he gets up after a day or two, he shall not be punished, for he is his property.
2_21_21.CSB17: However, if the slave can stand up after a day or two, the owner should not be punished because he is his owner’s property.
2_21_21.AMP: If, however, the servant survives for a day or two, the offender shall not be punished, for the [injured] servant is his own property.
2_21_21.MSG: But if the slave survives a day or two, he's not to be avenged—the slave is the owner's property.
2_21_21.NIV: but he is not to be punished if the slave gets up after a day or two, since the slave is his property.
2_21_21.NRSVCE: But if the slave survives a day or two, there is no punishment; for the slave is the owner’s property.

2_21_22.NKJV: “If men fight, and hurt a woman with child, so that she gives birth prematurely, yet no harm follows, he shall surely be punished accordingly as the woman’s husband imposes on him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
2_21_22.KJV: If men strive, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart from her, and yet no mischief follow: he shall be surely punished, according as the woman's husband will lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
2_21_22.ESV: “When men strive together and hit a pregnant woman, so that her children come out, but there is no harm, the one who hit her shall surely be fined, as the woman's husband shall impose on him, and he shall pay as the judges determine.
2_21_22.NASB: 'If men struggle with each other and strike a woman with child so that she gives birth prematurely, yet there is no injury, he shall surely be fined as the woman's husband may demand of him, and he shall pay as the judges [decide].
2_21_22.NLT: “Now suppose two men are fighting, and in the process they accidentally strike a pregnant woman so she gives birth prematurely. If no further injury results, the man who struck the woman must pay the amount of compensation the woman’s husband demands and the judges approve.
2_21_22.WEB: “If men fight and hurt a pregnant woman so that she gives birth prematurely, and yet no harm follows, he shall be surely fined as much as the woman’s husband demands and the judges allow.
2_21_22.CSB17: “When men get in a fight and hit a pregnant woman so that her children are born prematurely but there is no injury, the one who hit her must be fined as the woman’s husband demands from him, and he must pay according to judicial assessment.
2_21_22.AMP: "If men fight with each other and injure a pregnant woman so that she gives birth prematurely [and the baby lives], yet there is no further injury, the one who hurt her must be punished with a fine [paid] to the woman's husband, as much as the judges decide.
2_21_22.MSG: "When there's a fight and in the fight a pregnant woman is hit so that she miscarries but is not otherwise hurt, the one responsible has to pay whatever the husband demands in compensation.
2_21_22.NIV: “If men who are fighting hit a pregnant woman and she gives birth prematurely but there is no serious injury, the offender must be fined whatever the woman’s husband demands and the court allows.
2_21_22.NRSVCE: When people who are fighting injure a pregnant woman so that there is a miscarriage, and yet no further harm follows, the one responsible shall be fined what the woman’s husband demands, paying as much as the judges determine.

2_21_23.NKJV: But if any harm follows, then you shall give life for life,
2_21_23.KJV: And if any mischief follow, then thou shalt give life for life,
2_21_23.ESV: But if there is harm, then you shall pay life for life,
2_21_23.NASB: 'But if there is [any further] injury, then you shall appoint [as a penalty] life for life,
2_21_23.NLT: But if there is further injury, the punishment must match the injury: a life for a life,
2_21_23.WEB: But if any harm follows, then you must take life for life,
2_21_23.CSB17: If there is an injury, then you must give life for life,
2_21_23.AMP: But if there is any further injury, then you shall require [as a penalty] life for life,
2_21_23.MSG: But if there is further damage, then you must give life for life
2_21_23.NIV: But if there is serious injury, you are to take life for life,
2_21_23.NRSVCE: If any harm follows, then you shall give life for life,

2_21_24.NKJV: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.KJV: Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.ESV: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.NASB: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.NLT: an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth, a hand for a hand, a foot for a foot,
2_21_24.WEB: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.CSB17: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.AMP: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.MSG: —eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.NIV: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
2_21_24.NRSVCE: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

2_21_25.NKJV: burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
2_21_25.KJV: Burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
2_21_25.ESV: burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
2_21_25.NASB: burn for burn, wound for wound, bruise for bruise.
2_21_25.NLT: a burn for a burn, a wound for a wound, a bruise for a bruise.
2_21_25.WEB: burning for burning, wound for wound, and bruise for bruise.
2_21_25.CSB17: burn for burn, bruise for bruise, wound for wound.
2_21_25.AMP: burn for burn, wound for wound, bruise for bruise.
2_21_25.MSG: burn for burn, wound for wound, bruise for bruise.
2_21_25.NIV: burn for burn, wound for wound, bruise for bruise.
2_21_25.NRSVCE: burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.

2_21_26.NKJV: “If a man strikes the eye of his male or female servant, and destroys it, he shall let him go free for the sake of his eye.
2_21_26.KJV: And if a man smite the eye of his servant, or the eye of his maid, that it perish; he shall let him go free for his eye's sake.
2_21_26.ESV: “When a man strikes the eye of his slave, male or female, and destroys it, he shall let the slave go free because of his eye.
2_21_26.NASB: 'If a man strikes the eye of his male or female slave, and destroys it, he shall let him go free on account of his eye.
2_21_26.NLT: “If a man hits his male or female slave in the eye and the eye is blinded, he must let the slave go free to compensate for the eye.
2_21_26.WEB: “If a man strikes his servant’s eye, or his maid’s eye, and destroys it, he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.
2_21_26.CSB17: “When a man strikes the eye of his male or female slave and destroys it, he must let the slave go free in compensation for his eye.
2_21_26.AMP: "If a man hits the eye of his male servant or female servant and it is destroyed, he must let the servant go free because of [the loss of] the eye.
2_21_26.MSG: "If a slave owner hits the eye of a slave or handmaid and ruins it, the owner must let the slave go free because of the eye.
2_21_26.NIV: “If a man hits a manservant or maidservant in the eye and destroys it, he must let the servant go free to compensate for the eye.
2_21_26.NRSVCE: When a slaveowner strikes the eye of a male or female slave, destroying it, the owner shall let the slave go, a free person, to compensate for the eye.

2_21_27.NKJV: And if he knocks out the tooth of his male or female servant, he shall let him go free for the sake of his tooth.
2_21_27.KJV: And if he smite out his manservant's tooth, or his maidservant's tooth; he shall let him go free for his tooth's sake.
2_21_27.ESV: If he knocks out the tooth of his slave, male or female, he shall let the slave go free because of his tooth.
2_21_27.NASB: 'And if he knocks out a tooth of his male or female slave, he shall let him go free on account of his tooth.
2_21_27.NLT: And if a man knocks out the tooth of his male or female slave, he must let the slave go free to compensate for the tooth.
2_21_27.WEB: If he strikes out his male servant’s tooth, or his female servant’s tooth, he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.
2_21_27.CSB17: If he knocks out the tooth of his male or female slave, he must let the slave go free in compensation for his tooth.
2_21_27.AMP: And if he knocks out the tooth of his male servant or female servant, he must let the servant go free because of [the loss of] the tooth.
2_21_27.MSG: If the owner knocks out the tooth of the male or female slave, the slave must be released and go free because of the tooth.
2_21_27.NIV: And if he knocks out the tooth of a manservant or maidservant, he must let the servant go free to compensate for the tooth.
2_21_27.NRSVCE: If the owner knocks out a tooth of a male or female slave, the slave shall be let go, a free person, to compensate for the tooth.

2_21_28.NKJV: “If an ox gores a man or a woman to death, then the ox shall surely be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be acquitted.
2_21_28.KJV: If an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die: then the ox shall be surely stoned, and his flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.
2_21_28.ESV: “When an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox shall be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten, but the owner of the ox shall not be liable.
2_21_28.NASB: 'If an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox shall surely be stoned and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall go unpunished.
2_21_28.NLT: “If an ox gores a man or woman to death, the ox must be stoned, and its flesh may not be eaten. In such a case, however, the owner will not be held liable.
2_21_28.WEB: “If a bull gores a man or a woman to death, the bull shall surely be stoned, and its meat shall not be eaten; but the owner of the bull shall not be held responsible.
2_21_28.CSB17: “When an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox must be stoned, and its meat may not be eaten, but the ox’s owner is innocent.
2_21_28.AMP: "If an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox must be stoned and its meat shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be cleared [of responsibility].
2_21_28.MSG: "If an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox must be stoned. The meat cannot be eaten but the owner of the ox is in the clear.
2_21_28.NIV: “If a bull gores a man or a woman to death, the bull must be stoned to death, and its meat must not be eaten. But the owner of the bull will not be held responsible.
2_21_28.NRSVCE: When an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox shall be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall not be liable.

2_21_29.NKJV: But if the ox tended to thrust with its horn in times past, and it has been made known to his owner, and he has not kept it confined, so that it has killed a man or a woman, the ox shall be stoned and its owner also shall be put to death.
2_21_29.KJV: But if the ox were wont to push with his horn in time past , and it hath been testified to his owner, and he hath not kept him in, but that he hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and his owner also shall be put to death.
2_21_29.ESV: But if the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past, and its owner has been warned but has not kept it in, and it kills a man or a woman, the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.
2_21_29.NASB: 'If, however, an ox was previously in the habit of goring and its owner has been warned, yet he does not confine it and it kills a man or a woman, the ox shall be stoned and its owner also shall be put to death.
2_21_29.NLT: But suppose the ox had a reputation for goring, and the owner had been informed but failed to keep it under control. If the ox then kills someone, it must be stoned, and the owner must also be put to death.
2_21_29.WEB: But if the bull had a habit of goring in the past, and it has been testified to its owner, and he has not kept it in, but it has killed a man or a woman, the bull shall be stoned, and its owner shall also be put to death.
2_21_29.CSB17: However, if the ox was in the habit of goring, and its owner has been warned yet does not restrain it, and it kills a man or a woman, the ox must be stoned, and its owner must also be put to death.
2_21_29.AMP: But if the ox has tried to gore on a previous occasion, and its owner has been warned, but has not kept it confined and it kills a man or a woman, the ox shall be stoned and its owner shall be put to death as well.
2_21_29.MSG: But if the ox has a history of goring and the owner knew it and did nothing to guard against it, then if the ox kills a man or a woman, the ox is to be stoned and the owner given the death penalty.
2_21_29.NIV: If, however, the bull has had the habit of goring and the owner has been warned but has not kept it penned up and it kills a man or woman, the bull must be stoned and the owner also must be put to death.
2_21_29.NRSVCE: If the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past, and its owner has been warned but has not restrained it, and it kills a man or a woman, the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.

2_21_30.NKJV: If there is imposed on him a sum of money, then he shall pay to redeem his life, whatever is imposed on him.
2_21_30.KJV: If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
2_21_30.ESV: If a ransom is imposed on him, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatever is imposed on him.
2_21_30.NASB: 'If a ransom is demanded of him, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatever is demanded of him.
2_21_30.NLT: However, the dead person’s relatives may accept payment to compensate for the loss of life. The owner of the ox may redeem his life by paying whatever is demanded.
2_21_30.WEB: If a ransom is laid on him, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatever is laid on him.
2_21_30.CSB17: If instead a ransom is demanded of him, he can pay a redemption price for his life in the full amount demanded from him.
2_21_30.AMP: If a ransom is demanded of him [in return for his life], then he shall give whatever is demanded for the redemption of his life.
2_21_30.MSG: If a ransom is agreed upon instead of death, he must pay it in full as a redemption for his life.
2_21_30.NIV: However, if payment is demanded of him, he may redeem his life by paying whatever is demanded.
2_21_30.NRSVCE: If a ransom is imposed on the owner, then the owner shall pay whatever is imposed for the redemption of the victim’s life.

2_21_31.NKJV: Whether it has gored a son or gored a daughter, according to this judgment it shall be done to him.
2_21_31.KJV: Whether he have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.
2_21_31.ESV: If it gores a man's son or daughter, he shall be dealt with according to this same rule.
2_21_31.NASB: 'Whether it gores a son or a daughter, it shall be done to him according to the same rule.
2_21_31.NLT: “The same regulation applies if the ox gores a boy or a girl.
2_21_31.WEB: Whether it has gored a son or has gored a daughter, according to this judgment it shall be done to him.
2_21_31.CSB17: If it gores a son or a daughter, he is to be dealt with according to this same law.
2_21_31.AMP: If the ox has gored another's son or daughter, he shall be dealt with according to this same rule.
2_21_31.MSG: If a son or daughter is gored, the same judgment holds.
2_21_31.NIV: This law also applies if the bull gores a son or daughter.
2_21_31.NRSVCE: If it gores a boy or a girl, the owner shall be dealt with according to this same rule.

2_21_32.NKJV: If the ox gores a male or female servant, he shall give to their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
2_21_32.KJV: If the ox shall push a manservant or a maidservant; he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
2_21_32.ESV: If the ox gores a slave, male or female, the owner shall give to their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
2_21_32.NASB: 'If the ox gores a male or female slave, the owner shall give his [or her] master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
2_21_32.NLT: But if the ox gores a slave, either male or female, the animal’s owner must pay the slave’s owner thirty silver coins, and the ox must be stoned.
2_21_32.WEB: If the bull gores a male servant or a female servant, thirty shekels of silver shall be given to their master, and the ox shall be stoned.
2_21_32.CSB17: If the ox gores a male or female slave, he must give thirty shekels of silver to the slave’s master, and the ox must be stoned.
2_21_32.AMP: If the ox gores a male or a female servant, the owner shall give to the servant's master thirty shekels of silver [the purchase price for a slave], and the ox shall be stoned.
2_21_32.MSG: If it is a slave or a handmaid the ox gores, thirty shekels of silver is to be paid to the owner and the ox stoned.
2_21_32.NIV: If the bull gores a male or female slave, the owner must pay thirty shekels of silver to the master of the slave, and the bull must be stoned.
2_21_32.NRSVCE: If the ox gores a male or female slave, the owner shall pay to the slaveowner thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.

2_21_33.NKJV: “And if a man opens a pit, or if a man digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls in it,
2_21_33.KJV: And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit, and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein;
2_21_33.ESV: “When a man opens a pit, or when a man digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
2_21_33.NASB: 'If a man opens a pit, or digs a pit and does not cover it over, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
2_21_33.NLT: “Suppose someone digs or uncovers a pit and fails to cover it, and then an ox or a donkey falls into it.
2_21_33.WEB: “If a man opens a pit, or if a man digs a pit and doesn’t cover it, and a bull or a donkey falls into it,
2_21_33.CSB17: “When a man uncovers a pit or digs a pit, and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
2_21_33.AMP: "If a man leaves a pit open, or digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
2_21_33.MSG: "If someone uncovers a cistern or digs a pit and leaves it open and an ox or donkey falls into it,
2_21_33.NIV: “If a man uncovers a pit or digs one and fails to cover it and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
2_21_33.NRSVCE: If someone leaves a pit open, or digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,

2_21_34.NKJV: the owner of the pit shall make it good; he shall give money to their owner, but the dead animal shall be his.
2_21_34.KJV: The owner of the pit shall make it good, and give money unto the owner of them; and the dead beast shall be his.
2_21_34.ESV: the owner of the pit shall make restoration. He shall give money to its owner, and the dead beast shall be his.
2_21_34.NASB: the owner of the pit shall make restitution; he shall give money to its owner, and the dead [animal] shall become his.
2_21_34.NLT: The owner of the pit must pay full compensation to the owner of the animal, but then he gets to keep the dead animal.
2_21_34.WEB: the owner of the pit shall make it good. He shall give money to its owner, and the dead animal shall be his.
2_21_34.CSB17: the owner of the pit must give compensation; he must pay to its owner, but the dead animal will become his.
2_21_34.AMP: the owner of the pit shall make restitution; he shall give money to the animal's owner, but the dead [animal] shall be his.
2_21_34.MSG: the owner of the pit must pay whatever the animal is worth to its owner but can keep the dead animal.
2_21_34.NIV: the owner of the pit must pay for the loss; he must pay its owner, and the dead animal will be his.
2_21_34.NRSVCE: the owner of the pit shall make restitution, giving money to its owner, but keeping the dead animal.

2_21_35.NKJV: “If one man’s ox hurts another’s, so that it dies, then they shall sell the live ox and divide the money from it; and the dead ox they shall also divide.
2_21_35.KJV: And if one man's ox hurt another's, that he die; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money of it; and the dead ox also they shall divide.
2_21_35.ESV: “When one man's ox butts another's, so that it dies, then they shall sell the live ox and share its price, and the dead beast also they shall share.
2_21_35.NASB: 'If one man's ox hurts another's so that it dies, then they shall sell the live ox and divide its price equally; and also they shall divide the dead [ox].
