2_23_20.NASB: 'Behold, I am going to send an angel before you to guard you along the way and to bring you into the place which I have prepared.
2_23_20.NLT: “See, I am sending an angel before you to protect you on your journey and lead you safely to the place I have prepared for you.
2_23_20.WEB: “Behold, I send an angel before you, to keep you by the way, and to bring you into the place which I have prepared.
2_23_20.CSB17: “I am going to send an angel before you to protect you on the way and bring you to the place I have prepared.
2_23_20.AMP: "Behold, I am going to send an Angel before you to keep and guard you on the way and to bring you to the place I have prepared.
2_23_20.MSG: "Now get yourselves ready. I'm sending my Angel ahead of you to guard you in your travels, to lead you to the place that I've prepared.
2_23_20.NIV: God’s Angel to Prepare the Way“See, I am sending an angel ahead of you to guard you along the way and to bring you to the place I have prepared.
2_23_20.NRSVCE: I am going to send an angel in front of you, to guard you on the way and to bring you to the place that I have prepared.

2_23_21.NKJV: Beware of Him and obey His voice; do not provoke Him, for He will not pardon your transgressions; for My name is in Him.
2_23_21.KJV: Beware of him, and obey his voice, provoke him not; for he will not pardon your transgressions: for my name is in him.
2_23_21.ESV: Pay careful attention to him and obey his voice; do not rebel against him, for he will not pardon your transgression, for my name is in him.
2_23_21.NASB: 'Be on your guard before him and obey his voice; do not be rebellious toward him, for he will not pardon your transgression, since My name is in him.
2_23_21.NLT: Pay close attention to him, and obey his instructions. Do not rebel against him, for he is my representative, and he will not forgive your rebellion.
2_23_21.WEB: Pay attention to him, and listen to his voice. Don’t provoke him, for he will not pardon your disobedience, for my name is in him.
2_23_21.CSB17: Be attentive to him and listen to him. Do not defy him, because he will not forgive your acts of rebellion, for my name is in him.
2_23_21.AMP: Be on your guard before Him, listen to and obey His voice; do not be rebellious toward Him or provoke Him, for He will not pardon your transgression, since My Name (authority) is in Him.
2_23_21.MSG: Pay close attention to him. Obey him. Don't go against him. He won't put up with your rebellions because he's acting on my authority.
2_23_21.NIV: Pay attention to him and listen to what he says. Do not rebel against him; he will not forgive your rebellion, since my Name is in him.
2_23_21.NRSVCE: Be attentive to him and listen to his voice; do not rebel against him, for he will not pardon your transgression; for my name is in him.

2_23_22.NKJV: But if you indeed obey His voice and do all that I speak, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.
2_23_22.KJV: But if thou shalt indeed obey his voice, and do all that I speak; then I will be an enemy unto thine enemies, and an adversary unto thine adversaries. an adversary: or, I will afflict them that afflict thee
2_23_22.ESV: “But if you carefully obey his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.
2_23_22.NASB: 'But if you truly obey his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.
2_23_22.NLT: But if you are careful to obey him, following all my instructions, then I will be an enemy to your enemies, and I will oppose those who oppose you.
2_23_22.WEB: But if you indeed listen to his voice, and do all that I speak, then I will be an enemy to your enemies, and an adversary to your adversaries.
2_23_22.CSB17: But if you will carefully obey him and do everything I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and a foe to your foes.
2_23_22.AMP: But if you will indeed listen to and truly obey His voice and do everything that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.
2_23_22.MSG: But if you obey him and do everything I tell you, I'll be an enemy to your enemies, I'll fight those who fight you.
2_23_22.NIV: If you listen carefully to what he says and do all that I say, I will be an enemy to your enemies and will oppose those who oppose you.
2_23_22.NRSVCE: But if you listen attentively to his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and a foe to your foes.

2_23_23.NKJV: For My Angel will go before you and bring you in to the Amorites and the Hittites and the Perizzites and the Canaanites and the Hivites and the Jebusites; and I will cut them off.
2_23_23.KJV: For mine Angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the Amorites, and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites: and I will cut them off.
2_23_23.ESV: “When my angel goes before you and brings you to the Amorites and the Hittites and the Perizzites and the Canaanites, the Hivites and the Jebusites, and I blot them out,
2_23_23.NASB: 'For My angel will go before you and bring you in to [the land of] the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Canaanites, the Hivites and the Jebusites; and I will completely destroy them.
2_23_23.NLT: For my angel will go before you and bring you into the land of the Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hivites, and Jebusites, so you may live there. And I will destroy them completely.
2_23_23.WEB: For my angel shall go before you, and bring you in to the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Canaanite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite; and I will cut them off.
2_23_23.CSB17: For my angel will go before you and bring you to the land of the Amorites, Hethites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hivites, and Jebusites, and I will wipe them out.
2_23_23.AMP: When My Angel goes before you and brings you to [the land of] the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Canaanite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, I will reject them and completely destroy them.

2_23_24.NKJV: You shall not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do according to their works; but you shall utterly overthrow them and completely break down their sacred pillars.
2_23_24.KJV: Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their works: but thou shalt utterly overthrow them, and quite break down their images.

2_23_24.NASB: 'You shall not worship their gods, nor serve them, nor do according to their deeds; but you shall utterly overthrow them and break their [sacred] pillars in pieces.
2_23_24.NLT: You must not worship the gods of these nations or serve them in any way or imitate their evil practices. Instead, you must utterly destroy them and smash their sacred pillars.
2_23_24.WEB: You shall not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor follow their practices, but you shall utterly overthrow them and demolish their pillars.
2_23_24.CSB17: Do not bow in worship to their gods, and do not serve them. Do not imitate their practices. Instead, demolish them and smash their sacred pillars to pieces.
2_23_24.AMP: You shall not bow down to worship their gods, nor serve them, nor do [anything] in accordance with their practices. You shall completely overthrow them and break down their [sacred] pillars and images [of pagan worship].
2_23_24.MSG: So don't worship or serve their gods; don't do anything they do because I'm going to wipe them right off the face of the Earth and smash their sacred phallic pillars to bits.
2_23_24.NIV: Do not bow down before their gods or worship them or follow their practices. You must demolish them and break their sacred stones to pieces.
2_23_24.NRSVCE: you shall not bow down to their gods, or worship them, or follow their practices, but you shall utterly demolish them and break their pillars in pieces.

2_23_25.NKJV: “So you shall serve the LORD your God, and He will bless your bread and your water. And I will take sickness away from the midst of you.
2_23_25.KJV: And ye shall serve the LORD your God, and he shall bless thy bread, and thy water; and I will take sickness away from the midst of thee.
2_23_25.ESV: You shall serve the LORD your God, and he will bless your bread and your water, and I will take sickness away from among you.
2_23_25.NASB: 'But you shall serve the LORD your God, and He will bless your bread and your water; and I will remove sickness from your midst.
2_23_25.NLT: “You must serve only the LORD your God. If you do, I will bless you with food and water, and I will protect you from illness.

2_23_25.CSB17: Serve the LORD your God, and he will bless your bread and your water. I will remove illnesses from you.
2_23_25.AMP: You shall serve [only] the LORD your God, and He shall bless your bread and water. I will also remove sickness from among you.
2_23_25.MSG: "But you—you serve your God and he'll bless your food and your water. I'll get rid of the sickness among you;
2_23_25.NIV: Worship the Lord your God, and his blessing will be on your food and water. I will take away sickness from among you,
2_23_25.NRSVCE: You shall worship the LORD your God, and I will bless your bread and your water; and I will take sickness away from among you.

2_23_26.NKJV: No one shall suffer miscarriage or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
2_23_26.KJV: There shall nothing cast their young, nor be barren, in thy land: the number of thy days I will fulfil.
2_23_26.ESV: None shall miscarry or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
2_23_26.NASB: 'There shall be no one miscarrying or barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
2_23_26.NLT: There will be no miscarriages or infertility in your land, and I will give you long, full lives.
2_23_26.WEB: No one will miscarry or be barren in your land. I will fulfill the number of your days.
2_23_26.CSB17: No woman will miscarry or be childless in your land. I will give you the full number of your days.
2_23_26.AMP: No one shall suffer miscarriage or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
2_23_26.MSG: there won't be any miscarriages nor barren women in your land. I'll make sure you live full and complete lives.
2_23_26.NIV: and none will miscarry or be barren in your land. I will give you a full life span.
2_23_26.NRSVCE: No one shall miscarry or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.

2_23_27.NKJV: “I will send My fear before you, I will cause confusion among all the people to whom you come, and will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.
2_23_27.KJV: I will send my fear before thee, and will destroy all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee. backs: Heb. neck
2_23_27.ESV: I will send my terror before you and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.
2_23_27.NASB: 'I will send My terror ahead of you, and throw into confusion all the people among whom you come, and I will make all your enemies turn [their] backs to you.
2_23_27.NLT: “I will send my terror ahead of you and create panic among all the people whose lands you invade. I will make all your enemies turn and run.
2_23_27.WEB: I will send my terror before you, and will confuse all the people to whom you come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.
2_23_27.CSB17: “I will cause the people ahead of you to feel terror and will throw into confusion all the nations you come to. I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you in retreat.
2_23_27.AMP: I will send My terror ahead of you, and I will throw into confusion all the people among whom you come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you [in flight].
2_23_27.MSG: "I'll send my Terror on ahead of you and throw those peoples you're approaching into a panic. All you'll see of your enemies is the backs of their necks.
2_23_27.NIV: “I will send my terror ahead of you and throw into confusion every nation you encounter. I will make all your enemies turn their backs and run.
2_23_27.NRSVCE: I will send my terror in front of you, and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.

2_23_28.NKJV: And I will send hornets before you, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite from before you.
2_23_28.KJV: And I will send hornets before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.

2_23_28.NASB: 'I will send hornets ahead of you so that they will drive out the Hivites, the Canaanites, and the Hittites before you.
2_23_28.NLT: I will send terror ahead of you to drive out the Hivites, Canaanites, and Hittites.
2_23_28.WEB: I will send the hornet before you, which will drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before you.
2_23_28.CSB17: I will send hornets in front of you, and they will drive the Hivites, Canaanites, and Hethites away from you.
2_23_28.AMP: I will send hornets ahead of you which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite before you.

2_23_28.NIV: I will send the hornet ahead of you to drive the Hivites, Canaanites and Hittites out of your way.

2_23_29.NKJV: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate and the beasts of the field become too numerous for you.
2_23_29.KJV: I will not drive them out from before thee in one year; lest the land become desolate, and the beast of the field multiply against thee.
2_23_29.ESV: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate and the wild beasts multiply against you.
2_23_29.NASB: 'I will not drive them out before you in a single year, that the land may not become desolate and the beasts of the field become too numerous for you.
2_23_29.NLT: But I will not drive them out in a single year, because the land would become desolate and the wild animals would multiply and threaten you.
2_23_29.WEB: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate, and the animals of the field multiply against you.
2_23_29.CSB17: I will not drive them out ahead of you in a single year; otherwise, the land would become desolate, and wild animals would multiply against you.
2_23_29.AMP: I will not drive them out before you in a single year, so that the land does not become desolate [due to lack of attention] and the [wild] animals of the field do not become too numerous for you.
2_23_29.MSG: I won't get rid of them all at once lest the land grow up in weeds and the wild animals take over.
2_23_29.NIV: But I will not drive them out in a single year, because the land would become desolate and the wild animals too numerous for you.
2_23_29.NRSVCE: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, or the land would become desolate and the wild animals would multiply against you.

2_23_30.NKJV: Little by little I will drive them out from before you, until you have increased, and you inherit the land.

2_23_30.NRSVCE: Little by little I will drive them out from before you, until you have increased and possess the land.

2_24_3.NKJV: So Moses came and told the people all the words of the LORD and all the judgments. And all the people answered with one voice and said, “All the words which the LORD has said we will do.”

2_24_3.ESV: Moses came and told the people all the words of the LORD and all the rules. And all the people answered with one voice and said, “All the words that the LORD has spoken we will do.”
2_24_3.NASB: Then Moses came and recounted to the people all the words of the LORD and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice and said, 'All the words which the LORD has spoken we will do!'
2_24_3.NLT: Then Moses went down to the people and repeated all the instructions and regulations the LORD had given him. All the people answered with one voice, “We will do everything the LORD has commanded.”

2_24_3.NIV: When Moses went and told the people all the Lord’s words and laws, they responded with one voice, “Everything the Lord has said we will do.”
2_24_3.NRSVCE: Moses came and told the people all the words of the LORD and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice, and said, “All the words that the LORD has spoken we will do.”

2_25_9.NRSVCE: In accordance with all that I show you concerning the pattern of the tabernacle and of all its furniture, so you shall make it.

2_25_10.KJV: And they shall make an ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

2_25_10.NASB: 'They shall construct an ark of acacia wood two and a half cubits long, and one and a half cubits wide, and one and a half cubits high.
2_25_10.NLT: “Have the people make an Ark of acacia wood — a sacred chest 45 inches long, 27 inches wide, and 27 inches high.

2_25_10.AMP: "They shall make an ark of acacia wood two and a half cubits long, one and a half cubits wide, and one and a half cubits high.

2_25_10.NRSVCE: They shall make an ark of acacia wood; it shall be two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, and a cubit and a half high.

2_25_11.NKJV: And you shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and out you shall overlay it, and shall make on it a molding of gold all around.

2_25_11.ESV: You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and outside shall you overlay it, and you shall make on it a molding of gold around it.
2_25_11.NASB: 'You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and out you shall overlay it, and you shall make a gold molding around it.
2_25_11.NLT: Overlay it inside and outside with pure gold, and run a molding of gold all around it.
2_25_11.WEB: You shall overlay it with pure gold. You shall overlay it inside and outside, and you shall make a gold molding around it.
2_25_11.CSB17: Overlay it with pure gold; overlay it both inside and out. Also make a gold molding all around it.

2_25_11.MSG: . Cover it with a veneer of pure gold inside and out and make a molding of gold all around it.

2_25_11.NRSVCE: You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and outside you shall overlay it, and you shall make a molding of gold upon it all around.

2_25_12.NKJV: You shall cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in its four corners; two rings shall be on one side, and two rings on the other side.
2_25_12.KJV: And thou shalt cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in the four corners thereof; and two rings shall be in the one side of it, and two rings in the other side of it.

2_25_12.NASB: 'You shall cast four gold rings for it and fasten them on its four feet, and two rings shall be on one side of it and two rings on the other side of it.
2_25_12.NLT: Cast four gold rings and attach them to its four feet, two rings on each side.
2_25_12.WEB: You shall cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in its four feet. Two rings shall be on the one side of it, and two rings on the other side of it.
2_25_12.CSB17: Cast four gold rings for it and place them on its four feet, two rings on one side and two rings on the other side.
2_25_12.AMP: You shall cast four gold rings for it and attach them to the four feet, two rings on either side.
2_25_12.MSG: Cast four gold rings and attach them to its four feet, two rings on one side and two rings on the other.
2_25_12.NIV: Cast four gold rings for it and fasten them to its four feet, with two rings on one side and two rings on the other.
2_25_12.NRSVCE: You shall cast four rings of gold for it and put them on its four feet, two rings on the one side of it, and two rings on the other side.

2_25_13.NKJV: And you shall make poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.

2_25_13.NASB: 'You shall make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.

2_25_13.AMP: You shall make [carrying] poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold,
2_25_13.MSG: Make poles from acacia wood and cover them with a veneer of gold
2_25_13.NIV: Then make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
2_25_13.NRSVCE: You shall make poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.

2_25_14.NKJV: You shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, that the ark may be carried by them.
2_25_14.KJV: And thou shalt put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, that the ark may be borne with them.
2_25_14.ESV: And you shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry the ark by them.
2_25_14.NASB: 'You shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, to carry the ark with them.
2_25_14.NLT: Insert the poles into the rings at the sides of the Ark to carry it.

2_25_14.AMP: and put the poles through the rings on the sides of the ark, by which to carry it.

2_25_14.NIV: Insert the poles into the rings on the sides of the chest to carry it.
2_25_14.NRSVCE: And you shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, by which to carry the ark.

2_25_15.NKJV: The poles shall be in the rings of the ark; they shall not be taken from it.
2_25_15.KJV: The staves shall be in the rings of the ark: they shall not be taken from it.

2_25_15.NASB: 'The poles shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be removed from it.

2_25_15.CSB17: The poles are to remain in the rings of the ark; they must not be removed from it.

2_25_15.NIV: The poles are to remain in the rings of this ark; they are not to be removed.
2_25_15.NRSVCE: The poles shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be taken from it.

2_25_16.NKJV: And you shall put into the ark the Testimony which I will give you.

2_25_16.ESV: And you shall put into the ark the testimony that I shall give you.
2_25_16.NASB: 'You shall put into the ark the testimony which I shall give you.
2_25_16.NLT: When the Ark is finished, place inside it the stone tablets inscribed with the terms of the covenant, which I will give to you.
2_25_16.WEB: You shall put the testimony which I shall give you into the ark.
2_25_16.CSB17: Put the tablets of the testimony that I will give you into the ark.
2_25_16.AMP: You shall put into the ark the Testimony (Ten Commandments) which I will give you.

2_25_16.NIV: Then put in the ark the Testimony, which I will give you.
2_25_16.NRSVCE: You shall put into the ark the covenant that I shall give you.

2_25_17.NKJV: “You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold; two and a half cubits shall be its length and a cubit and a half its width.
2_25_17.KJV: And thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof.
2_25_17.ESV: “You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold. Two cubits and a half shall be its length, and a cubit and a half its breadth.
2_25_17.NASB: 'You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.
2_25_17.NLT: “Then make the Ark’s cover — the place of atonement — from pure gold. It must be 45 inches long and 27 inches wide.
2_25_17.WEB: You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold. Two and a half cubits shall be its length, and a cubit and a half its width.
2_25_17.CSB17: Make a mercy seat of pure gold, forty-five inches long and twenty-seven inches wide.
2_25_17.AMP: "You shall make a mercy seat (cover) of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.
2_25_17.MSG: "Now make a lid of pure gold for the Chest, an Atonement-Cover, three and three-quarters feet long and two and one-quarter feet wide.
2_25_17.NIV: “Make an atonement cover of pure gold—two and a half cubits long and a cubit and a half wide.
2_25_17.NRSVCE: Then you shall make a mercy seat of pure gold; two cubits and a half shall be its length, and a cubit and a half its width.

2_25_18.NKJV: And you shall make two cherubim of gold; of hammered work you shall make them at the two ends of the mercy seat.

2_25_18.CSB17: Make two cherubim of gold; make them of hammered work at the two ends of the mercy seat.
2_25_18.AMP: You shall make two cherubim (winged angelic figures) of [solid] hammered gold at the two ends of the mercy seat.
2_25_18.MSG: "Sculpt two winged angels out of hammered gold for either end of the Atonement-Cover,
2_25_18.NIV: And make two cherubim out of hammered gold at the ends of the cover.
2_25_18.NRSVCE: You shall make two cherubim of gold; you shall make them of hammered work, at the two ends of the mercy seat.

2_25_19.NKJV: Make one cherub at one end, and the other cherub at the other end; you shall make the cherubim at the two ends of it of one piece with the mercy seat.
2_25_19.KJV: And make one cherub on the one end, and the other cherub on the other end: even of the mercy seat shall ye make the cherubims on the two ends thereof. of: or, of the matter of the mercy seat
2_25_19.ESV: Make one cherub on the one end, and one cherub on the other end. Of one piece with the mercy seat shall you make the cherubim on its two ends.
2_25_19.NASB: 'Make one cherub at one end and one cherub at the other end; you shall make the cherubim [of one piece] with the mercy seat at its two ends.
2_25_19.NLT: Mold the cherubim on each end of the atonement cover, making it all of one piece of gold.
2_25_19.WEB: Make one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other end. You shall make the cherubim on its two ends of one piece with the mercy seat.
2_25_19.CSB17: Make one cherub at one end and one cherub at the other end. At its two ends, make the cherubim of one piece with the mercy seat.
2_25_19.AMP: Make one cherub at each end, making the cherubim of one piece with the mercy seat at its two ends.
2_25_19.MSG: one angel at one end, one angel at the other. Make them of one piece with the Atonement-Cover.
2_25_19.NIV: Make one cherub on one end and the second cherub on the other; make the cherubim of one piece with the cover, at the two ends.
2_25_19.NRSVCE: Make one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other; of one piece with the mercy seat you shall make the cherubim at its two ends.

2_25_20.NKJV: And the cherubim shall stretch out their wings above, covering the mercy seat with their wings, and they shall face one another; the faces of the cherubim shall be toward the mercy seat.
2_25_20.KJV: And the cherubims shall stretch forth their wings on high, covering the mercy seat with their wings, and their faces shall look one to another; toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubims be.
2_25_20.ESV: The cherubim shall spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings, their faces one to another; toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubim be.
2_25_20.NASB: 'The cherubim shall have [their] wings spread upward, covering the mercy seat with their wings and facing one another; the faces of the cherubim are to be [turned] toward the mercy seat.
2_25_20.NLT: The cherubim will face each other and look down on the atonement cover. With their wings spread above it, they will protect it.

2_25_20.MSG: Make the angels with their wings spread, hovering over the Atonement-Cover, facing one another but looking down on it.
2_25_20.NIV: The cherubim are to have their wings spread upward, overshadowing the cover with them. The cherubim are to face each other, looking toward the cover.
2_25_20.NRSVCE: The cherubim shall spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings. They shall face one to another; the faces of the cherubim shall be turned toward the mercy seat.

2_25_21.NKJV: You shall put the mercy seat on top of the ark, and in the ark you shall put the Testimony that I will give you.
2_25_21.KJV: And thou shalt put the mercy seat above upon the ark; and in the ark thou shalt put the testimony that I shall give thee.

2_25_21.NASB: 'You shall put the mercy seat on top of the ark, and in the ark you shall put the testimony which I will give to you.
2_25_21.NLT: Place inside the Ark the stone tablets inscribed with the terms of the covenant, which I will give to you. Then put the atonement cover on top of the Ark.

2_25_21.AMP: You shall put the mercy seat on the top of the ark, and in the ark you shall put the Testimony which I will give you.

2_25_21.NIV: Place the cover on top of the ark and put in the ark the Testimony, which I will give you.
2_25_21.NRSVCE: You shall put the mercy seat on the top of the ark; and in the ark you shall put the covenant that I shall give you.

2_25_22.NKJV: And there I will meet with you, and I will speak with you from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim which are on the ark of the Testimony, about everything which I will give you in commandment to the children of Israel.
2_25_22.KJV: And there I will meet with thee, and I will commune with thee from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubims which are upon the ark of the testimony, of all things which I will give thee in commandment unto the children of Israel.
2_25_22.ESV: There I will meet with you, and from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim that are on the ark of the testimony, I will speak with you about all that I will give you in commandment for the people of Israel.
2_25_22.NASB: 'There I will meet with you; and from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim which are upon the ark of the testimony, I will speak to you about all that I will give you in commandment for the sons of Israel.
2_25_22.NLT: I will meet with you there and talk to you from above the atonement cover between the gold cherubim that hover over the Ark of the Covenant. From there I will give you my commands for the people of Israel.
2_25_22.WEB: There I will meet with you, and I will tell you from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim which are on the ark of the testimony, all that I command you for the children of Israel.
2_25_22.CSB17: I will meet with you there above the mercy seat, between the two cherubim that are over the ark of the testimony; I will speak with you from there about all that I command you regarding the Israelites.
2_25_22.AMP: There I will meet with you; from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim which are on the ark of the Testimony, I will speak [intimately] with you regarding every commandment that I will give you for the Israelites.
2_25_22.MSG: I will meet you there at set times and speak with you from above the Atonement-Cover and from between the angel-figures that are on it, speaking the commands that I have for the Israelites.
2_25_22.NIV: There, above the cover between the two cherubim that are over the ark of the Testimony, I will meet with you and give you all my commands for the Israelites.
2_25_22.NRSVCE: There I will meet with you, and from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim that are on the ark of the covenant, I will deliver to you all my commands for the Israelites.

2_25_23.NKJV: “You shall also make a table of acacia wood; two cubits shall be its length, a cubit its width, and a cubit and a half its height.

2_25_24.NKJV: And you shall overlay it with pure gold, and make a molding of gold all around.

2_25_28.NRSVCE: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold, and the table shall be carried with these.

2_25_29.NKJV: You shall make its dishes, its pans, its pitchers, and its bowls for pouring. You shall make them of pure gold.
2_25_29.KJV: And thou shalt make the dishes thereof, and spoons thereof, and covers thereof, and bowls thereof, to cover withal: of pure gold shalt thou make them. to: or, to pour out withal
2_25_29.ESV: And you shall make its plates and dishes for incense, and its flagons and bowls with which to pour drink offerings; you shall make them of pure gold.
2_25_29.NASB: 'You shall make its dishes and its pans and its jars and its bowls with which to pour drink offerings; you shall make them of pure gold.
2_25_29.NLT: Make special containers of pure gold for the table — bowls, ladles, pitchers, and jars — to be used in pouring out liquid offerings.
2_25_29.WEB: You shall make its dishes, its spoons, its ladles, and its bowls to pour out offerings with. You shall make them of pure gold.
2_25_29.CSB17: You are also to make its plates and cups, as well as its pitchers and bowls for pouring drink offerings. Make them out of pure gold.
2_25_29.AMP: You shall make its plates [for the showbread] and its cups [for incense] and its pitchers and bowls for sacrificial drink offerings; you shall make them of pure gold.
2_25_29.MSG: "Make plates, bowls, jars, and jugs for pouring out offerings. Make them of pure gold.
2_25_29.NIV: And make its plates and dishes of pure gold, as well as its pitchers and bowls for the pouring out of offerings.
2_25_29.NRSVCE: You shall make its plates and dishes for incense, and its flagons and bowls with which to pour drink offerings; you shall make them of pure gold.

2_25_30.NKJV: And you shall set the showbread on the table before Me always.
2_25_30.KJV: And thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me alway.
2_25_30.ESV: And you shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before me regularly.
2_25_30.NASB: 'You shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before Me at all times.
2_25_30.NLT: Place the Bread of the Presence on the table to remain before me at all times.
2_25_30.WEB: You shall set bread of the presence on the table before me always.
2_25_30.CSB17: Put the Bread of the Presence on the table before me at all times.
2_25_30.AMP: You shall set the bread of the Presence (showbread) on the table before Me at all times.
2_25_30.MSG: "Always keep fresh Bread of the Presence on the Table before me.
2_25_30.NIV: Put the bread of the Presence on this table to be before me at all times.
2_25_30.NRSVCE: And you shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before me always.

2_25_31.NKJV: “You shall also make a lampstand of pure gold; the lampstand shall be of hammered work. Its shaft, its branches, its bowls, its ornamental knobs, and flowers shall be of one piece.
2_25_31.KJV: And thou shalt make a candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work shall the candlestick be made: his shaft, and his branches, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, shall be of the same.
2_25_31.ESV: “You shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The lampstand shall be made of hammered work: its base, its stem, its cups, its calyxes, and its flowers shall be of one piece with it.
2_25_31.NASB: 'Then you shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The lampstand [and] its base and its shaft are to be made of hammered work; its cups, its bulbs and its flowers shall be [of one piece] with it.
2_25_31.NLT: “Make a lampstand of pure, hammered gold. Make the entire lampstand and its decorations of one piece — the base, center stem, lamp cups, buds, and petals.
2_25_31.WEB: “You shall make a lamp stand of pure gold. Of hammered work shall the lamp stand be made, even its base, its shaft, its cups, its buds, and its flowers, shall be of one piece with it.
2_25_31.CSB17: “You are to make a lampstand out of pure, hammered gold. It is to be made of one piece: its base and shaft, its ornamental cups, and its buds and petals.
2_25_31.AMP: "You shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The lampstand and its base and its shaft shall be made of hammered work; its cups, its calyxes and its flowers shall be all of one piece with it.
2_25_31.MSG: "Make a Lampstand of pure hammered gold. Make its stem and branches, cups, calyxes, and petals all of one piece.
2_25_31.NIV: The Lampstand“Make a lampstand of pure gold and hammer it out, base and shaft; its flowerlike cups, buds and blossoms shall be of one piece with it.
2_25_31.NRSVCE: You shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The base and the shaft of the lampstand shall be made of hammered work; its cups, its calyxes, and its petals shall be of one piece with it;

2_25_32.NKJV: And six branches shall come out of its sides: three branches of the lampstand out of one side, and three branches of the lampstand out of the other side.
2_25_32.KJV: And six branches shall come out of the sides of it; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side:
2_25_32.ESV: And there shall be six branches going out of its sides, three branches of the lampstand out of one side of it and three branches of the lampstand out of the other side of it;
2_25_32.NASB: 'Six branches shall go out from its sides; three branches of the lampstand from its one side and three branches of the lampstand from its other side.
2_25_32.NLT: Make it with six branches going out from the center stem, three on each side.
2_25_32.WEB: There shall be six branches going out of its sides: three branches of the lamp stand out of its one side, and three branches of the lamp stand out of its other side;
2_25_32.CSB17: Six branches are to extend from its sides, three branches of the lampstand from one side and three branches of the lampstand from the other side.
2_25_32.AMP: Six branches shall come out of its sides; three branches of the lampstand out of the one side and three branches of the lampstand out of its other side [the shaft being the seventh branch].
2_25_32.MSG: Give it six branches, three from one side and three from the other;
2_25_32.NIV: Six branches are to extend from the sides of the lampstand—three on one side and three on the other.
2_25_32.NRSVCE: and there shall be six branches going out of its sides, three branches of the lampstand out of one side of it and three branches of the lampstand out of the other side of it;

2_25_33.NKJV: Three bowls shall be made like almond blossoms on one branch, with an ornamental knob and a flower, and three bowls made like almond blossoms on the other branch, with an ornamental knob and a flower—and so for the six branches that come out of the lampstand.
2_25_33.KJV: Three bowls made like unto almonds, with a knop and a flower in one branch; and three bowls made like almonds in the other branch, with a knop and a flower: so in the six branches that come out of the candlestick.
2_25_33.ESV: three cups made like almond blossoms, each with calyx and flower, on one branch, and three cups made like almond blossoms, each with calyx and flower, on the other branch — so for the six branches going out of the lampstand.
2_25_33.NASB: 'Three cups [shall be] shaped like almond [blossoms] in the one branch, a bulb and a flower, and three cups shaped like almond [blossoms] in the other branch, a bulb and a flower-- so for six branches going out from the lampstand;
2_25_33.NLT: Each of the six branches will have three lamp cups shaped like almond blossoms, complete with buds and petals.
2_25_33.WEB: three cups made like almond blossoms in one branch, a bud and a flower; and three cups made like almond blossoms in the other branch, a bud and a flower, so for the six branches going out of the lamp stand;
2_25_33.CSB17: There are to be three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with a bud and petals, on one branch, and three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with a bud and petals, on the next branch. It is to be this way for the six branches that extend from the lampstand.
2_25_33.AMP: Three cups shall be made like almond blossoms, each with a calyx and a flower on one branch, and three cups made like almond blossoms on the other branch with a calyx and a flower—so for the six branches coming out of the lampstand;
2_25_33.MSG: put three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on one branch, three on the next, and so on—the same for all six branches.
2_25_33.NIV: Three cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms are to be on one branch, three on the next branch, and the same for all six branches extending from the lampstand.
2_25_33.NRSVCE: three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on one branch, and three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on the other branch — so for the six branches going out of the lampstand.

2_25_34.NKJV: On the lampstand itself four bowls shall be made like almond blossoms, each with its ornamental knob and flower.
2_25_34.KJV: And in the candlestick shall be four bowls made like unto almonds, with their knops and their flowers.
2_25_34.ESV: And on the lampstand itself there shall be four cups made like almond blossoms, with their calyxes and flowers,
2_25_34.NASB: and in the lampstand four cups shaped like almond [blossoms], its bulbs and its flowers.
2_25_34.NLT: Craft the center stem of the lampstand with four lamp cups shaped like almond blossoms, complete with buds and petals.
2_25_34.WEB: and in the lamp stand four cups made like almond blossoms, its buds and its flowers;
2_25_34.CSB17: There are to be four cups shaped like almond blossoms on the lampstand shaft along with its buds and petals.
2_25_34.AMP: and in the [center shaft of the] lampstand [you shall make] four cups shaped like almond blossoms, with their calyxes and their flowers.
2_25_34.MSG: On the main stem of the Lampstand, make four cups shaped like almonds, with calyx and petals,
2_25_34.NIV: And on the lampstand there are to be four cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms.
2_25_34.NRSVCE: On the lampstand itself there shall be four cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with its calyxes and petals.

2_25_35.NKJV: And there shall be a knob under the first two branches of the same, a knob under the second two branches of the same, and a knob under the third two branches of the same, according to the six branches that extend from the lampstand.
2_25_35.KJV: And there shall be a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches that proceed out of the candlestick.
2_25_35.ESV: and a calyx of one piece with it under each pair of the six branches going out from the lampstand.
2_25_35.NASB: 'A bulb shall be under the [first] pair of branches [coming] out of it, and a bulb under the [second] pair of branches [coming] out of it, and a bulb under the [third] pair of branches [coming] out of it, for the six branches coming out of the lampstand.
2_25_35.NLT: There will also be an almond bud beneath each pair of branches where the six branches extend from the center stem.
2_25_35.WEB: and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, for the six branches going out of the lamp stand.
2_25_35.CSB17: For the six branches that extend from the lampstand, a bud must be under the first pair of branches from it, a bud under the second pair of branches from it, and a bud under the third pair of branches from it.
2_25_35.AMP: A calyx shall be under the first pair of branches coming out of it, and a calyx under the second pair of branches coming out of it, and a calyx under the third pair of branches coming out of it, for the six branches coming out of the lampstand.
2_25_35.MSG: a calyx extending from under each pair of the six branches,
2_25_35.NIV: One bud shall be under the first pair of branches extending from the lampstand, a second bud under the second pair, and a third bud under the third pair—six branches in all.
2_25_35.NRSVCE: There shall be a calyx of one piece with it under the first pair of branches, a calyx of one piece with it under the next pair of branches, and a calyx of one piece with it under the last pair of branches — so for the six branches that go out of the lampstand.

2_25_36.NKJV: Their knobs and their branches shall be of one piece; all of it shall be one hammered piece of pure gold.
2_25_36.KJV: Their knops and their branches shall be of the same: all it shall be one beaten work of pure gold.
2_25_36.ESV: Their calyxes and their branches shall be of one piece with it, the whole of it a single piece of hammered work of pure gold.
2_25_36.NASB: 'Their bulbs and their branches [shall be of one piece] with it; all of it shall be one piece of hammered work of pure gold.
2_25_36.NLT: The almond buds and branches must all be of one piece with the center stem, and they must be hammered from pure gold.
2_25_36.WEB: Their buds and their branches shall be of one piece with it, all of it one beaten work of pure gold.
2_25_36.CSB17: Their buds and branches are to be of one piece. All of it is to be a single hammered piece of pure gold.
2_25_36.AMP: Their calyxes and their branches shall be of one piece with it; all of it shall be one piece of hammered work of pure gold.
2_25_36.MSG: the entire Lampstand fashioned from one piece of hammered pure gold.
2_25_36.NIV: The buds and branches shall all be of one piece with the lampstand, hammered out of pure gold.
2_25_36.NRSVCE: Their calyxes and their branches shall be of one piece with it, the whole of it one hammered piece of pure gold.

2_25_37.NKJV: You shall make seven lamps for it, and they shall arrange its lamps so that they give light in front of it.
2_25_37.KJV: And thou shalt make the seven lamps thereof: and they shall light the lamps thereof, that they may give light over against it. shall light: or, shall cause to ascend it: Heb. the face of it
2_25_37.ESV: You shall make seven lamps for it. And the lamps shall be set up so as to give light on the space in front of it.
2_25_37.NASB: 'Then you shall make its lamps seven [in number]; and they shall mount its lamps so as to shed light on the space in front of it.
2_25_37.NLT: Then make the seven lamps for the lampstand, and set them so they reflect their light forward.
2_25_37.WEB: You shall make its lamps seven, and they shall light its lamps to give light to the space in front of it.
2_25_37.CSB17: “Make its seven lamps, and set them up so that they illuminate the area in front of it.
2_25_37.AMP: Then you shall make the lamps [of the lampstand] seven in number [with one lamp at the top of the shaft]. The priests shall set up its seven lamps so that they will light the space in front of it.
2_25_37.MSG: "Make seven of these lamps for the Table. Arrange the lamps so they throw their light out in front.
2_25_37.NIV: “Then make its seven lamps and set them up on it so that they light the space in front of it.
2_25_37.NRSVCE: You shall make the seven lamps for it; and the lamps shall be set up so as to give light on the space in front of it.

2_25_38.NKJV: And its wick-trimmers and their trays shall be of pure gold.
2_25_38.KJV: And the tongs thereof, and the snuffdishes thereof, shall be of pure gold.
2_25_38.ESV: Its tongs and their trays shall be of pure gold.
2_25_38.NASB: 'Its snuffers and their trays [shall be] of pure gold.
2_25_38.NLT: The lamp snuffers and trays must also be made of pure gold.
2_25_38.WEB: Its snuffers and its snuff dishes shall be of pure gold.
2_25_38.CSB17: Its snuffers and firepans must be of pure gold.
2_25_38.AMP: Its snuffers and their trays shall be of pure gold.
2_25_38.MSG: Make the candle snuffers and trays out of pure gold.
2_25_38.NIV: Its wick trimmers and trays are to be of pure gold.
2_25_38.NRSVCE: Its snuffers and trays shall be of pure gold.

2_25_39.NKJV: It shall be made of a talent of pure gold, with all these utensils.
2_25_39.KJV: Of a talent of pure gold shall he make it, with all these vessels.
2_25_39.ESV: It shall be made, with all these utensils, out of a talent of pure gold.
2_25_39.NASB: 'It shall be made from a talent of pure gold, with all these utensils.
2_25_39.NLT: You will need 75 pounds of pure gold for the lampstand and its accessories.
2_25_39.WEB: It shall be made of a talent of pure gold, with all these accessories.
2_25_39.CSB17: The lampstand with all these utensils is to be made from seventy-five pounds of pure gold.
2_25_39.AMP: It shall be made from a talent (50-80 lbs.) of pure gold, including all these utensils.
2_25_39.MSG: "Use a seventy-five-pound brick of pure gold to make the Lampstand and its accessories.
2_25_39.NIV: A talent of pure gold is to be used for the lampstand and all these accessories.
2_25_39.NRSVCE: It, and all these utensils, shall be made from a talent of pure gold.

2_25_40.NKJV: And see to it that you make them according to the pattern which was shown you on the mountain.
2_25_40.KJV: And look that thou make them after their pattern, which was shewed thee in the mount. which: Heb. which thou wast caused to see
2_25_40.ESV: And see that you make them after the pattern for them, which is being shown you on the mountain.
2_25_40.NASB: 'See that you make [them] after the pattern for them, which was shown to you on the mountain.
2_25_40.NLT: “Be sure that you make everything according to the pattern I have shown you here on the mountain.
2_25_40.WEB: See that you make them after their pattern, which has been shown to you on the mountain.
2_25_40.CSB17: Be careful to make them according to the pattern you have been shown on the mountain.
2_25_40.AMP: See that you make them [exactly] after their pattern which was shown to you on the mountain.
2_25_40.MSG: Study the design you were given on the mountain and make everything accordingly.
2_25_40.NIV: See that you make them according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.
2_25_40.NRSVCE: And see that you make them according to the pattern for them, which is being shown you on the mountain.

2_26_1.NKJV: “Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine woven linen and blue, purple, and scarlet thread; with artistic designs of cherubim you shall weave them.
2_26_1.KJV: Moreover thou shalt make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet : with cherubims of cunning work shalt thou make them. of cunning: Heb. the work of a cunning workman, or, embroiderer
2_26_1.ESV: “Moreover, you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns; you shall make them with cherubim skillfully worked into them.
2_26_1.NASB: 'Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet [material]; you shall make them with cherubim, the work of a skillful workman.
2_26_1.NLT: “Make the Tabernacle from ten curtains of finely woven linen. Decorate the curtains with blue, purple, and scarlet thread and with skillfully embroidered cherubim.
2_26_1.WEB: “Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, with cherubim. The work of the skillful workman you shall make them.
2_26_1.CSB17: “You are to construct the tabernacle itself with ten curtains. You must make them of finely spun linen, and blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, with a design of cherubim worked into them.
2_26_1.AMP: "Moreover, you shall make the tabernacle (sacred tent of worship) with ten [interior] curtains of fine twisted linen, and blue, purple, and scarlet fabric; you shall make them with [embroidered] cherubim, the handwork of a skillful craftsman.
2_26_1.MSG: "Make The Dwelling itself from ten panels of tapestry woven from fine twisted linen, blue and purple and scarlet material, with an angel-cherubim design. A skilled craftsman should do it.
2_26_1.NIV: The Tabernacle“Make the tabernacle with ten curtains of finely twisted linen and blue, purple and scarlet yarn, with cherubim worked into them by a skilled craftsman.
2_26_1.NRSVCE: Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen, and blue, purple, and crimson yarns; you shall make them with cherubim skillfully worked into them.

2_26_2.NKJV: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits. And every one of the curtains shall have the same measurements.
2_26_2.KJV: The length of one curtain shall be eight and twenty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and every one of the curtains shall have one measure.
2_26_2.ESV: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains shall be the same size.
2_26_2.NASB: 'The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains shall have the same measurements.
2_26_2.NLT: These ten curtains must all be exactly the same size — 42 feet long and 6 feet wide.
2_26_2.WEB: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits: all the curtains shall have one measure.
2_26_2.CSB17: Each curtain should be forty-two feet long and six feet wide; all the curtains are to have the same measurements.
2_26_2.AMP: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all of the curtains shall measure the same.
2_26_2.MSG: The panels of tapestry are each to be forty-six feet long and six feet wide.
2_26_2.NIV: All the curtains are to be the same size—twenty-eight cubits long and four cubits wide.
2_26_2.NRSVCE: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains shall be of the same size.

2_26_3.NKJV: Five curtains shall be coupled to one another, and the other five curtains shall be coupled to one another.
2_26_3.KJV: The five curtains shall be coupled together one to another; and other five curtains shall be coupled one to another.
2_26_3.ESV: Five curtains shall be coupled to one another, and the other five curtains shall be coupled to one another.
2_26_3.NASB: 'Five curtains shall be joined to one another, and [the other] five curtains [shall be] joined to one another.
2_26_3.NLT: Join five of these curtains together to make one long curtain, then join the other five into a second long curtain.
2_26_3.WEB: Five curtains shall be coupled together to one another; and the other five curtains shall be coupled to one another.
2_26_3.CSB17: Five of the curtains should be joined together, and the other five curtains joined together.
2_26_3.AMP: The five curtains shall be joined to one another, and the other five curtains shall be joined to one another.
2_26_3.MSG: Join five of the panels together, and then the other five together.
2_26_3.NIV: Join five of the curtains together, and do the same with the other five.
2_26_3.NRSVCE: Five curtains shall be joined to one another; and the other five curtains shall be joined to one another.

2_26_4.NKJV: And you shall make loops of blue yarn on the edge of the curtain on the selvedge of one set, and likewise you shall do on the outer edge of the other curtain of the second set.
2_26_4.KJV: And thou shalt make loops of blue upon the edge of the one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling; and likewise shalt thou make in the uttermost edge of another curtain, in the coupling of the second.
2_26_4.ESV: And you shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set. Likewise you shall make loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.
2_26_4.NASB: 'You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the [first] set, and likewise you shall make [them] on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the second set.
2_26_4.NLT: Put loops of blue yarn along the edge of the last curtain in each set.
2_26_4.WEB: You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the one curtain from the edge in the coupling; and likewise you shall make in the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the second coupling.
2_26_4.CSB17: Make loops of blue yarn on the edge of the last curtain in the first set, and do the same on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.
2_26_4.AMP: You shall make loops of blue on the outer edge of the last curtain in the first set, and likewise in the second set.
2_26_4.MSG: Make loops of blue along the edge of the outside panel of the first set and the same on the outside panel of the second set.
2_26_4.NIV: Make loops of blue material along the edge of the end curtain in one set, and do the same with the end curtain in the other set.
2_26_4.NRSVCE: You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set; and likewise you shall make loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.

2_26_5.NKJV: Fifty loops you shall make in the one curtain, and fifty loops you shall make on the edge of the curtain that is on the end of the second set, that the loops may be clasped to one another.
2_26_5.KJV: Fifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain, and fifty loops shalt thou make in the edge of the curtain that is in the coupling of the second; that the loops may take hold one of another.
2_26_5.ESV: Fifty loops you shall make on the one curtain, and fifty loops you shall make on the edge of the curtain that is in the second set; the loops shall be opposite one another.
2_26_5.NASB: 'You shall make fifty loops in the one curtain, and you shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is in the second set; the loops shall be opposite each other.
2_26_5.NLT: The fifty loops along the edge of one curtain are to match the fifty loops along the edge of the other curtain.
2_26_5.WEB: You shall make fifty loops in the one curtain, and you shall make fifty loops in the edge of the curtain that is in the second coupling. The loops shall be opposite to one another.
2_26_5.CSB17: Make fifty loops on the one curtain and make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain in the second set, so that the loops line up together.
2_26_5.AMP: You shall make fifty loops on the one curtain, and fifty loops on the edge of the last curtain that is in the second set. The loops on one curtain correspond to the loops on the other.
2_26_5.MSG: Make fifty loops on each panel.
2_26_5.NIV: Make fifty loops on one curtain and fifty loops on the end curtain of the other set, with the loops opposite each other.
2_26_5.NRSVCE: You shall make fifty loops on the one curtain, and you shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is in the second set; the loops shall be opposite one another.

2_26_6.NKJV: And you shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains together with the clasps, so that it may be one tabernacle.
2_26_6.KJV: And thou shalt make fifty taches of gold, and couple the curtains together with the taches: and it shall be one tabernacle.
2_26_6.ESV: And you shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains one to the other with the clasps, so that the tabernacle may be a single whole.
2_26_6.NASB: 'You shall make fifty clasps of gold, and join the curtains to one another with the clasps so that the tabernacle will be a unit.
2_26_6.NLT: Then make fifty gold clasps and fasten the long curtains together with the clasps. In this way, the Tabernacle will be made of one continuous piece.
2_26_6.WEB: You shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains to one another with the clasps: and the tabernacle shall be a unit.
2_26_6.CSB17: Also make fifty gold clasps and join the curtains together with the clasps, so that the tabernacle may be a single unit.
2_26_6.AMP: You shall make fifty gold hooks, and fasten the curtains together with the hooks; and the tabernacle shall be one unit.
2_26_6.MSG: Then make fifty gold clasps and join the tapestries together so that The Dwelling is one whole.
2_26_6.NIV: Then make fifty gold clasps and use them to fasten the curtains together so that the tabernacle is a unit.
2_26_6.NRSVCE: You shall make fifty clasps of gold, and join the curtains to one another with the clasps, so that the tabernacle may be one whole.

2_26_7.NKJV: “You shall also make curtains of goats’ hair, to be a tent over the tabernacle. You shall make eleven curtains.
2_26_7.KJV: And thou shalt make curtains of goats' hair to be a covering upon the tabernacle: eleven curtains shalt thou make.
2_26_7.ESV: “You shall also make curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle; eleven curtains shall you make.
2_26_7.NASB: 'Then you shall make curtains of goats' [hair] for a tent over the tabernacle; you shall make eleven curtains in all.
2_26_7.NLT: “Make eleven curtains of goat-hair cloth to serve as a tent covering for the Tabernacle.
2_26_7.WEB: “You shall make curtains of goats’ hair for a covering over the tabernacle. You shall make them eleven curtains.
2_26_7.CSB17: “You are to make curtains of goat hair for a tent over the tabernacle; make eleven of these curtains.
2_26_7.AMP: "Then you shall make [exterior] curtains of goats' hair as a tent over the tabernacle. You shall make eleven curtains in all.
2_26_7.MSG: "Next make tapestries of goat hair for a tent that will cover The Dwelling. Make eleven panels of these tapestries.
2_26_7.NIV: “Make curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle—eleven altogether.
2_26_7.NRSVCE: You shall also make curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; you shall make eleven curtains.

2_26_8.NKJV: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; and the eleven curtains shall all have the same measurements.
2_26_8.KJV: The length of one curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and the eleven curtains shall be all of one measure.
2_26_8.ESV: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth of each curtain four cubits. The eleven curtains shall be the same size.
2_26_8.NASB: 'The length of each curtain [shall be] thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; the eleven curtains shall have the same measurements.
2_26_8.NLT: These eleven curtains must all be exactly the same size — 45 feet long and 6 feet wide.
2_26_8.WEB: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits: the eleven curtains shall have one measure.
2_26_8.CSB17: Each curtain should be forty-five feet long and six feet wide. All eleven curtains are to have the same measurements.
2_26_8.AMP: Each curtain shall be thirty cubits long and four cubits wide. The eleven curtains shall all measure the same.
2_26_8.MSG: The length of each panel will be forty-five feet long and six feet wide.
2_26_8.NIV: All eleven curtains are to be the same size—thirty cubits long and four cubits wide.
2_26_8.NRSVCE: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; the eleven curtains shall be of the same size.

2_26_9.NKJV: And you shall couple five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves, and you shall double over the sixth curtain at the forefront of the tent.
2_26_9.KJV: And thou shalt couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shalt double the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tabernacle.
2_26_9.ESV: You shall couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and the sixth curtain you shall double over at the front of the tent.
2_26_9.NASB: 'You shall join five curtains by themselves and the [other] six curtains by themselves, and you shall double over the sixth curtain at the front of the tent.
2_26_9.NLT: Join five of these curtains together to make one long curtain, and join the other six into a second long curtain. Allow 3 feet of material from the second set of curtains to hang over the front of the sacred tent.
2_26_9.WEB: You shall couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shall double over the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tent.
2_26_9.CSB17: Join five of the curtains by themselves, and the other six curtains by themselves. Then fold the sixth curtain double at the front of the tent.
2_26_9.AMP: You shall join five curtains by themselves and the other six curtains by themselves, and you shall double over the sixth curtain at the front of the tent [to make a closed door].
2_26_9.MSG: Join five of the panels together, and then the other six. Fold the sixth panel double at the front of the tent.
2_26_9.NIV: Join five of the curtains together into one set and the other six into another set. Fold the sixth curtain double at the front of the tent.
2_26_9.NRSVCE: You shall join five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and the sixth curtain you shall double over at the front of the tent.

2_26_10.NKJV: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain of the second set.
2_26_10.KJV: And thou shalt make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outmost in the coupling, and fifty loops in the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.
2_26_10.ESV: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the second set.
2_26_10.NASB: 'You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the [first] set, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain [that is outermost in] the second set.
2_26_10.NLT: Make fifty loops for one edge of each large curtain.
2_26_10.WEB: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outermost in the coupling, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which is outermost in the second coupling.
2_26_10.CSB17: Make fifty loops on the edge of one curtain, the outermost in the first set, and make fifty loops on the edge of the corresponding curtain of the second set.
2_26_10.AMP: Make fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set, and fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.
2_26_10.MSG: Now make fifty loops along the edge of the end panel and fifty loops along the edge of the joining panel.
2_26_10.NIV: Make fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in one set and also along the edge of the end curtain in the other set.
2_26_10.NRSVCE: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the second set.

2_26_11.NKJV: And you shall make fifty bronze clasps, put the clasps into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.
2_26_11.KJV: And thou shalt make fifty taches of brass, and put the taches into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one. tent: or, covering
2_26_11.ESV: “You shall make fifty clasps of bronze, and put the clasps into the loops, and couple the tent together that it may be a single whole.
2_26_11.NASB: 'You shall make fifty clasps of bronze, and you shall put the clasps into the loops and join the tent together so that it will be a unit.
2_26_11.NLT: Then make fifty bronze clasps, and fasten the loops of the long curtains with the clasps. In this way, the tent covering will be made of one continuous piece.
2_26_11.WEB: You shall make fifty clasps of brass, and put the clasps into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.
2_26_11.CSB17: Make fifty bronze clasps; put the clasps through the loops and join the tent together so that it is a single unit.
2_26_11.AMP: "You shall make fifty bronze hooks and put the hooks into the loops and join the tent together so that it may be one unit.
2_26_11.MSG: Make fifty clasps of bronze and connect the clasps with the loops, bringing the tent together.
2_26_11.NIV: Then make fifty bronze clasps and put them in the loops to fasten the tent together as a unit.
2_26_11.NRSVCE: You shall make fifty clasps of bronze, and put the clasps into the loops, and join the tent together, so that it may be one whole.

2_26_12.NKJV: The remnant that remains of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.KJV: And the remnant that remaineth of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remaineth, shall hang over the backside of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.ESV: And the part that remains of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.NASB: 'The overlapping part that is left over in the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that is left over, shall lap over the back of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.NLT: The remaining 3 feet of this tent covering will be left to hang over the back of the Tabernacle.
2_26_12.WEB: The overhanging part that remains of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.CSB17: As for the flap that remains from the tent curtains, the leftover half curtain is to hang over the back of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.AMP: The overlapping part that is left over from the tent curtains, the half curtain that is left over, shall lap over the back of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.MSG: "Hang half of the overlap of the tapestry panels over the rear of The Dwelling.
2_26_12.NIV: As for the additional length of the tent curtains, the half curtain that is left over is to hang down at the rear of the tabernacle.
2_26_12.NRSVCE: The part that remains of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.

2_26_13.NKJV: And a cubit on one side and a cubit on the other side, of what remains of the length of the curtains of the tent, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle, on this side and on that side, to cover it.
2_26_13.KJV: And a cubit on the one side, and a cubit on the other side of that which remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tent, it shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it. of that: Heb. in the remainder, or, surplus
2_26_13.ESV: And the extra that remains in the length of the curtains, the cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle, on this side and that side, to cover it.
2_26_13.NASB: 'The cubit on one side and the cubit on the other, of what is left over in the length of the curtains of the tent, shall lap over the sides of the tabernacle on one side and on the other, to cover it.
2_26_13.NLT: Allow 18 inches of remaining material to hang down over each side, so the Tabernacle is completely covered.
2_26_13.WEB: The cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side, of that which remains in the length of the curtains of the tent, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it.
2_26_13.CSB17: What remains along the length of the tent curtains — a half yard on one side and a half yard on the other side — should hang over the sides of the tabernacle on either side to cover it.
2_26_13.AMP: The cubit on one side and the cubit on the other, of what is left over in the length of the curtains of the tent shall lap over the sides of the tabernacle on one side and the other side, to cover it.
2_26_13.MSG: The eighteen inches of overlap on either side will cover the sides of the tent.
2_26_13.NIV: The tent curtains will be a cubit longer on both sides; what is left will hang over the sides of the tabernacle so as to cover it.
2_26_13.NRSVCE: The cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side, of what remains in the length of the curtains of the tent, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle, on this side and that side, to cover it.

2_26_14.NKJV: “You shall also make a covering of ram skins dyed red for the tent, and a covering of badger skins above that.
2_26_14.KJV: And thou shalt make a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red, and a covering above of badgers' skins.
2_26_14.ESV: And you shall make for the tent a covering of tanned rams' skins and a covering of goatskins on top.
2_26_14.NASB: 'You shall make a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red and a covering of porpoise skins above.
2_26_14.NLT: Complete the tent covering with a protective layer of tanned ram skins and a layer of fine goatskin leather.
2_26_14.WEB: You shall make a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering of sea cow hides above.
2_26_14.CSB17: Make a covering for the tent from ram skins dyed red and a covering of fine leather on top of that.
2_26_14.AMP: You shall make a third covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red, and a fourth covering above that of porpoise skins.
2_26_14.MSG: Finally, make a covering for the tapestries of tanned rams' skins dyed red and over that a covering of dolphin skins.
2_26_14.NIV: Make for the tent a covering of ram skins dyed red, and over that a covering of hides of sea cows.
2_26_14.NRSVCE: You shall make for the tent a covering of tanned rams’ skins and an outer covering of fine leather.

2_26_15.NKJV: “And for the tabernacle you shall make the boards of acacia wood, standing upright.
2_26_15.KJV: And thou shalt make boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood standing up.
2_26_15.ESV: “You shall make upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.
2_26_15.NASB: 'Then you shall make the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing upright.
2_26_15.NLT: “For the framework of the Tabernacle, construct frames of acacia wood.
2_26_15.WEB: “You shall make the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing up.
2_26_15.CSB17: “You are to make upright supports of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
2_26_15.AMP: "Then you shall make the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing upright [as a trellis-like frame].
2_26_15.MSG: "Frame The Dwelling with planks of acacia wood,
2_26_15.NIV: “Make upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
2_26_15.NRSVCE: You shall make upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.

2_26_16.NKJV: Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and a cubit and a half shall be the width of each board.
2_26_16.KJV: Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and a cubit and a half shall be the breadth of one board.
2_26_16.ESV: Ten cubits shall be the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the breadth of each frame.
2_26_16.NASB: 'Ten cubits [shall be] the length of each board and one and a half cubits the width of each board.
2_26_16.NLT: Each frame must be 15 feet high and 27 inches wide,
2_26_16.WEB: Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and one and a half cubits the width of each board.
2_26_16.CSB17: Each support is to be fifteen feet long and twenty-seven inches wide.
2_26_16.AMP: The length of each board shall be ten cubits and the width of each board shall be one and a half cubits.
2_26_16.MSG: each section of frame fifteen feet long and two and one-quarter feet wide,
2_26_16.NIV: Each frame is to be ten cubits long and a cubit and a half wide,
2_26_16.NRSVCE: Ten cubits shall be the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the width of each frame.

2_26_17.NKJV: Two tenons shall be in each board for binding one to another. Thus you shall make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_26_17.KJV: Two tenons shall there be in one board, set in order one against another: thus shalt thou make for all the boards of the tabernacle. tenons: Heb. hands
2_26_17.ESV: There shall be two tenons in each frame, for fitting together. So shall you do for all the frames of the tabernacle.
2_26_17.NASB: '[There] [shall be] two tenons for each board, fitted to one another; thus you shall do for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_26_17.NLT: with two pegs under each frame. Make all the frames identical.
2_26_17.WEB: There shall be two tenons in each board, joined to one another: thus you shall make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_26_17.CSB17: Each support will have two tenons for joining. Do the same for all the supports of the tabernacle.
2_26_17.AMP: Make two dovetails in each board for fitting [them] together; you shall do the same for all the tabernacle boards.
2_26_17.MSG: with two pegs for securing them. Make all the frames identical:
2_26_17.NIV: with two projections set parallel to each other. Make all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.
2_26_17.NRSVCE: There shall be two pegs in each frame to fit the frames together; you shall make these for all the frames of the tabernacle.

2_26_18.NKJV: And you shall make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards for the south side.
2_26_18.KJV: And thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards on the south side southward.
2_26_18.ESV: You shall make the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side;
2_26_18.NASB: 'You shall make the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side.
2_26_18.NLT: Make twenty of these frames to support the curtains on the south side of the Tabernacle.
2_26_18.WEB: You shall make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards for the south side southward.
2_26_18.CSB17: Make the supports for the tabernacle as follows: twenty supports for the south side,
2_26_18.AMP: You shall make the boards for the tabernacle [in the following quantities]: twenty boards for the south side.
2_26_18.MSG: twenty frames for the south side
2_26_18.NIV: Make twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle
2_26_18.NRSVCE: You shall make the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side;

2_26_19.NKJV: You shall make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards: two sockets under each of the boards for its two tenons.
2_26_19.KJV: And thou shalt make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.
2_26_19.ESV: and forty bases of silver you shall make under the twenty frames, two bases under one frame for its two tenons, and two bases under the next frame for its two tenons;
2_26_19.NASB: 'You shall make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards, two sockets under one board for its two tenons and two sockets under another board for its two tenons;
2_26_19.NLT: Also make forty silver bases — two bases under each frame, with the pegs fitting securely into the bases.
2_26_19.WEB: You shall make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for its two tenons, and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.
2_26_19.CSB17: and make forty silver bases under the twenty supports, two bases under the first support for its two tenons, and two bases under the next support for its two tenons;
2_26_19.AMP: You shall make forty silver sockets under the twenty boards, two sockets under each board for its two dovetails, and two sockets under another board for its two dovetails;
2_26_19.MSG: with forty silver sockets to receive the two pegs from each of the twenty frames;
2_26_19.NIV: and make forty silver bases to go under them—two bases for each frame, one under each projection.
2_26_19.NRSVCE: and you shall make forty bases of silver under the twenty frames, two bases under the first frame for its two pegs, and two bases under the next frame for its two pegs;

2_26_20.NKJV: And for the second side of the tabernacle, the north side, there shall be twenty boards
2_26_20.KJV: And for the second side of the tabernacle on the north side there shall be twenty boards:
2_26_20.ESV: and for the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side twenty frames,
2_26_20.NASB: and for the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, twenty boards,
2_26_20.NLT: For the north side of the Tabernacle, make another twenty frames,
2_26_20.WEB: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, twenty boards,
2_26_20.CSB17: twenty supports for the second side of the tabernacle, the north side,
2_26_20.AMP: for the north side of the tabernacle there shall be twenty boards,
2_26_20.MSG: the same construction on the north side of The Dwelling;
2_26_20.NIV: For the other side, the north side of the tabernacle, make twenty frames
2_26_20.NRSVCE: and for the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side twenty frames,

2_26_21.NKJV: and their forty sockets of silver: two sockets under each of the boards.
2_26_21.KJV: And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
