2_26_21.ESV: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under one frame, and two bases under the next frame.
2_26_21.NASB: and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
2_26_21.NLT: with their forty silver bases, two bases under each frame.
2_26_21.WEB: and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
2_26_21.CSB17: along with their forty silver bases, two bases under the first support and two bases under each support;
2_26_21.AMP: and their forty silver sockets, two sockets under each board.
2_26_21.NIV: and forty silver bases—two under each frame.
2_26_21.NRSVCE: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under the first frame, and two bases under the next frame;

2_26_22.NKJV: For the far side of the tabernacle, westward, you shall make six boards.
2_26_22.KJV: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward thou shalt make six boards.
2_26_22.ESV: And for the rear of the tabernacle westward you shall make six frames.
2_26_22.NASB: 'For the rear of the tabernacle, to the west, you shall make six boards.
2_26_22.NLT: Make six frames for the rear — the west side of the Tabernacle —
2_26_22.WEB: For the far part of the tabernacle westward you shall make six boards.
2_26_22.CSB17: and make six supports for the west side of the tabernacle.
2_26_22.AMP: For the back or west side of the tabernacle you shall make six boards.
2_26_22.MSG: for the rear of The Dwelling, which faces west, make six frames
2_26_22.NIV: Make six frames for the far end, that is, the west end of the tabernacle,
2_26_22.NRSVCE: and for the rear of the tabernacle westward you shall make six frames.

2_26_23.NKJV: And you shall also make two boards for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
2_26_23.KJV: And two boards shalt thou make for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.
2_26_23.ESV: And you shall make two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear;
2_26_23.NASB: 'You shall make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear.
2_26_23.NLT: along with two additional frames to reinforce the rear corners of the Tabernacle.
2_26_23.WEB: You shall make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle in the far part.
2_26_23.CSB17: Make two additional supports for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
2_26_23.AMP: Make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear [on both sides].
2_26_23.MSG: with two additional frames for the rear corners.
2_26_23.NIV: and make two frames for the corners at the far end.
2_26_23.NRSVCE: You shall make two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear;

2_26_24.NKJV: They shall be coupled together at the bottom and they shall be coupled together at the top by one ring. Thus it shall be for both of them. They shall be for the two corners.
2_26_24.KJV: And they shall be coupled together beneath, and they shall be coupled together above the head of it unto one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners. coupled: Heb. twinned
2_26_24.ESV: they shall be separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring. Thus shall it be with both of them; they shall form the two corners.
2_26_24.NASB: 'They shall be double beneath, and together they shall be complete to its top to the first ring; thus it shall be with both of them: they shall form the two corners.
2_26_24.NLT: These corner frames will be matched at the bottom and firmly attached at the top with a single ring, forming a single corner unit. Make both of these corner units the same way.
2_26_24.WEB: They shall be double beneath, and in the same way they shall be whole to its top to one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.
2_26_24.CSB17: They are to be paired at the bottom, and joined together at the top in a single ring. So it should be for both of them; they will serve as the two corners.
2_26_24.AMP: They shall be joined together underneath, and joined together on top with one ring. So shall it be for both of them; they shall form the two [rear] corners.
2_26_24.MSG: Both of the two corner frames need to be double in thickness from top to bottom and fit into a single ring—
2_26_24.NIV: At these two corners they must be double from the bottom all the way to the top, and fitted into a single ring; both shall be like that.
2_26_24.NRSVCE: they shall be separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring; it shall be the same with both of them; they shall form the two corners.

2_26_25.NKJV: So there shall be eight boards with their sockets of silver—sixteen sockets—two sockets under each of the boards.
2_26_25.KJV: And they shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
2_26_25.ESV: And there shall be eight frames, with their bases of silver, sixteen bases; two bases under one frame, and two bases under another frame.
2_26_25.NASB: 'There shall be eight boards with their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
2_26_25.NLT: So there will be eight frames at the rear of the Tabernacle, set in sixteen silver bases — two bases under each frame.
2_26_25.WEB: There shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
2_26_25.CSB17: There are to be eight supports with their silver bases: sixteen bases; two bases under the first support and two bases under each support.
2_26_25.AMP: There shall be eight boards and sixteen silver sockets; two sockets under each board.
2_26_25.MSG: eight frames altogether with sixteen sockets of silver, two under each frame.
2_26_25.NIV: So there will be eight frames and sixteen silver bases—two under each frame.
2_26_25.NRSVCE: And so there shall be eight frames, with their bases of silver, sixteen bases; two bases under the first frame, and two bases under the next frame.

2_26_26.NKJV: “And you shall make bars of acacia wood: five for the boards on one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.KJV: And thou shalt make bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.ESV: “You shall make bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.NASB: 'Then you shall make bars of acacia wood, five for the boards of one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.NLT: “Make crossbars of acacia wood to link the frames, five crossbars for the north side of the Tabernacle
2_26_26.WEB: “You shall make bars of acacia wood: five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.CSB17: “You are to make five crossbars of acacia wood for the supports on one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.AMP: "Then you shall make [fifteen] bars of acacia wood: five for the boards of one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.MSG: "Now make crossbars of acacia wood, five for the frames on one side of The Dwelling,
2_26_26.NIV: “Also make crossbars of acacia wood: five for the frames on one side of the tabernacle,
2_26_26.NRSVCE: You shall make bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,

2_26_27.NKJV: five bars for the boards on the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the far side westward.
2_26_27.KJV: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the two sides westward.
2_26_27.ESV: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the side of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
2_26_27.NASB: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle for the rear [side] to the west.
2_26_27.NLT: and five for the south side. Also make five crossbars for the rear of the Tabernacle, which will face west.
2_26_27.WEB: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the far part westward.
2_26_27.CSB17: five crossbars for the supports on the other side of the tabernacle, and five crossbars for the supports of the back side of the tabernacle on the west.
2_26_27.AMP: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the rear end of the tabernacle, for the back wall toward the west.
2_26_27.MSG: five for the other side, and five for the back side facing west.
2_26_27.NIV: five for those on the other side, and five for the frames on the west, at the far end of the tabernacle.
2_26_27.NRSVCE: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the side of the tabernacle at the rear westward.

2_26_28.NKJV: The middle bar shall pass through the midst of the boards from end to end.
2_26_28.KJV: And the middle bar in the midst of the boards shall reach from end to end.
2_26_28.ESV: The middle bar, halfway up the frames, shall run from end to end.
2_26_28.NASB: 'The middle bar in the center of the boards shall pass through from end to end.
2_26_28.NLT: The middle crossbar, attached halfway up the frames, will run all the way from one end of the Tabernacle to the other.
2_26_28.WEB: The middle bar in the middle of the boards shall pass through from end to end.
2_26_28.CSB17: The central crossbar is to run through the middle of the supports from one end to the other.
2_26_28.AMP: And the middle bar in the center of the boards shall pass through [horizontally] from end to end.
2_26_28.MSG: The center crossbar runs from end to end halfway up the frames.
2_26_28.NIV: The center crossbar is to extend from end to end at the middle of the frames.
2_26_28.NRSVCE: The middle bar, halfway up the frames, shall pass through from end to end.

2_26_29.NKJV: You shall overlay the boards with gold, make their rings of gold as holders for the bars, and overlay the bars with gold.
2_26_29.KJV: And thou shalt overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold for places for the bars: and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold.
2_26_29.ESV: You shall overlay the frames with gold and shall make their rings of gold for holders for the bars, and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
2_26_29.NASB: 'You shall overlay the boards with gold and make their rings of gold [as] holders for the bars; and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
2_26_29.NLT: Overlay the frames with gold, and make gold rings to hold the crossbars. Overlay the crossbars with gold as well.
2_26_29.WEB: You shall overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold for places for the bars: and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
2_26_29.CSB17: Then overlay the supports with gold, and make their rings of gold as the holders for the crossbars. Also overlay the crossbars with gold.
2_26_29.AMP: You shall overlay the boards with gold and make their rings of gold to hold the bars. You shall overlay the bars with gold.
2_26_29.MSG: Cover the frames with a veneer of gold and make gold rings to hold the crossbars. And cover the crossbars with a veneer of gold.
2_26_29.NIV: Overlay the frames with gold and make gold rings to hold the crossbars. Also overlay the crossbars with gold.
2_26_29.NRSVCE: You shall overlay the frames with gold, and shall make their rings of gold to hold the bars; and you shall overlay the bars with gold.

2_26_30.NKJV: And you shall raise up the tabernacle according to its pattern which you were shown on the mountain.
2_26_30.KJV: And thou shalt rear up the tabernacle according to the fashion thereof which was shewed thee in the mount.
2_26_30.ESV: Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to the plan for it that you were shown on the mountain.
2_26_30.NASB: 'Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to its plan which you have been shown in the mountain.
2_26_30.NLT: “Set up this Tabernacle according to the pattern you were shown on the mountain.
2_26_30.WEB: You shall set up the tabernacle according to the way that it was shown to you on the mountain.
2_26_30.CSB17: You are to set up the tabernacle according to the plan for it that you have been shown on the mountain.
2_26_30.AMP: You shall erect the tabernacle according to its plan [the direction corresponding to its meaning and purpose] which has been shown to you on the mountain.
2_26_30.MSG: Then put The Dwelling together, following the design you were shown on the mountain.
2_26_30.NIV: “Set up the tabernacle according to the plan shown you on the mountain.
2_26_30.NRSVCE: Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to the plan for it that you were shown on the mountain.

2_26_31.NKJV: “You shall make a veil woven of blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine woven linen. It shall be woven with an artistic design of cherubim.
2_26_31.KJV: And thou shalt make a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet , and fine twined linen of cunning work: with cherubims shall it be made:
2_26_31.ESV: “And you shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen. It shall be made with cherubim skillfully worked into it.
2_26_31.NASB: 'You shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet [material] and fine twisted linen; it shall be made with cherubim, the work of a skillful workman.
2_26_31.NLT: “For the inside of the Tabernacle, make a special curtain of finely woven linen. Decorate it with blue, purple, and scarlet thread and with skillfully embroidered cherubim.
2_26_31.WEB: “You shall make a veil of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, with cherubim. The work of the skillful workman shall it be made.
2_26_31.CSB17: “You are to make a curtain of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen with a design of cherubim worked into it.
2_26_31.AMP: "You shall make a veil [to divide the two rooms] of blue, purple, and scarlet fabric and fine twisted linen, skillfully worked with cherubim on it.
2_26_31.MSG: "Make a curtain of blue, purple, and scarlet material and fine twisted linen. Have a design of angel-cherubim woven into it by a skilled craftsman.
2_26_31.NIV: “Make a curtain of blue, purple and scarlet yarn and finely twisted linen, with cherubim worked into it by a skilled craftsman.
2_26_31.NRSVCE: You shall make a curtain of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen; it shall be made with cherubim skillfully worked into it.

2_26_32.NKJV: You shall hang it upon the four pillars of acacia wood overlaid with gold. Their hooks shall be gold, upon four sockets of silver.
2_26_32.KJV: And thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of shittim wood overlaid with gold: their hooks shall be of gold, upon the four sockets of silver.
2_26_32.ESV: And you shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, with hooks of gold, on four bases of silver.
2_26_32.NASB: 'You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, their hooks [also being of] gold, on four sockets of silver.
2_26_32.NLT: Hang this curtain on gold hooks attached to four posts of acacia wood. Overlay the posts with gold, and set them in four silver bases.
2_26_32.WEB: You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, on four sockets of silver.
2_26_32.CSB17: Hang it on four gold-plated pillars of acacia wood that have gold hooks and that stand on four silver bases.
2_26_32.AMP: You shall hang it on four pillars (support poles) of acacia wood overlaid with gold, with gold hooks, on four silver sockets.
2_26_32.MSG: Fasten it with gold hooks to four posts of acacia wood covered with a veneer of gold, set on four silver bases.
2_26_32.NIV: Hang it with gold hooks on four posts of acacia wood overlaid with gold and standing on four silver bases.
2_26_32.NRSVCE: You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, which have hooks of gold and rest on four bases of silver.

2_26_33.NKJV: And you shall hang the veil from the clasps. Then you shall bring the ark of the Testimony in there, behind the veil. The veil shall be a divider for you between the holy place and the Most Holy.
2_26_33.KJV: And thou shalt hang up the vail under the taches, that thou mayest bring in thither within the vail the ark of the testimony: and the vail shall divide unto you between the holy place and the most holy.
2_26_33.ESV: And you shall hang the veil from the clasps, and bring the ark of the testimony in there within the veil. And the veil shall separate for you the Holy Place from the Most Holy.
2_26_33.NASB: 'You shall hang up the veil under the clasps, and shall bring in the ark of the testimony there within the veil; and the veil shall serve for you as a partition between the holy place and the holy of holies.
2_26_33.NLT: Hang the inner curtain from clasps, and put the Ark of the Covenant in the room behind it. This curtain will separate the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.
2_26_33.WEB: You shall hang up the veil under the clasps, and shall bring the ark of the testimony in there within the veil: and the veil shall separate the holy place from the most holy for you.
2_26_33.CSB17: Hang the curtain under the clasps and bring the ark of the testimony there behind the curtain, so the curtain will make a separation for you between the holy place and the most holy place.
2_26_33.AMP: You shall hang the veil from the hooks [that connect the curtains together], and you shall bring the ark of the Testimony there within the veil. The veil shall separate for you the Holy Place and the Holy of Holies.
2_26_33.MSG: After hanging the curtain from the clasps, bring the Chest of The Testimony in behind the curtain. The curtain will separate the Holy Place from the Holy-of-Holies.
2_26_33.NIV: Hang the curtain from the clasps and place the ark of the Testimony behind the curtain. The curtain will separate the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.
2_26_33.NRSVCE: You shall hang the curtain under the clasps, and bring the ark of the covenant in there, within the curtain; and the curtain shall separate for you the holy place from the most holy.

2_26_34.NKJV: You shall put the mercy seat upon the ark of the Testimony in the Most Holy.
2_26_34.KJV: And thou shalt put the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
2_26_34.ESV: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the Most Holy Place.
2_26_34.NASB: 'You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the holy of holies.
2_26_34.NLT: “Then put the Ark’s cover — the place of atonement — on top of the Ark of the Covenant inside the Most Holy Place.
2_26_34.WEB: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
2_26_34.CSB17: Put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
2_26_34.AMP: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the Testimony in the Holy of Holies.
2_26_34.MSG: Now place the Atonement-Cover lid on the Chest of The Testimony in the Holy-of-Holies.
2_26_34.NIV: Put the atonement cover on the ark of the Testimony in the Most Holy Place.
2_26_34.NRSVCE: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the covenant in the most holy place.

2_26_35.NKJV: You shall set the table outside the veil, and the lampstand across from the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south; and you shall put the table on the north side.
2_26_35.KJV: And thou shalt set the table without the vail, and the candlestick over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and thou shalt put the table on the north side.
2_26_35.ESV: And you shall set the table outside the veil, and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle opposite the table, and you shall put the table on the north side.
2_26_35.NASB: 'You shall set the table outside the veil, and the lampstand opposite the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south; and you shall put the table on the north side.
2_26_35.NLT: Place the table outside the inner curtain on the north side of the Tabernacle, and place the lampstand across the room on the south side.
2_26_35.WEB: You shall set the table outside the veil, and the lamp stand over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and you shall put the table on the north side.
2_26_35.CSB17: Place the table outside the curtain and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle, opposite the table; put the table on the north side.
2_26_35.AMP: You shall set the table [for the bread] outside the veil [in the Holy Place] on the north side, and the lampstand opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle.
2_26_35.MSG: Place the Table and the Lampstand outside the curtain, the Lampstand on the south side of The Dwelling and the Table opposite it on the north side.
2_26_35.NIV: Place the table outside the curtain on the north side of the tabernacle and put the lampstand opposite it on the south side.
2_26_35.NRSVCE: You shall set the table outside the curtain, and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle opposite the table; and you shall put the table on the north side.

2_26_36.NKJV: “You shall make a screen for the door of the tabernacle, woven of blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine woven linen, made by a weaver.
2_26_36.KJV: And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tent, of blue, and purple, and scarlet , and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework.
2_26_36.ESV: “You shall make a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework.
2_26_36.NASB: 'You shall make a screen for the doorway of the tent of blue and purple and scarlet [material] and fine twisted linen, the work of a weaver.
2_26_36.NLT: “Make another curtain for the entrance to the sacred tent. Make it of finely woven linen and embroider it with exquisite designs, using blue, purple, and scarlet thread.
2_26_36.WEB: “You shall make a screen for the door of the Tent, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the embroiderer.
2_26_36.CSB17: “For the entrance to the tent you are to make a screen embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen.
2_26_36.AMP: "You shall make a screen [to provide a covering] for the doorway of the tent of blue, purple, and scarlet fabric and finely woven [embroidered] linen, the work of an embroiderer.
2_26_36.MSG: "Make a screen for the door of the tent. Weave it from blue, purple, and scarlet material and fine twisted linen.
2_26_36.NIV: “For the entrance to the tent make a curtain of blue, purple and scarlet yarn and finely twisted linen—the work of an embroiderer.
2_26_36.NRSVCE: You shall make a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework.

2_26_37.NKJV: And you shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be gold, and you shall cast five sockets of bronze for them.
2_26_37.KJV: And thou shalt make for the hanging five pillars of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, and their hooks shall be of gold: and thou shalt cast five sockets of brass for them.
2_26_37.ESV: And you shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold. Their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast five bases of bronze for them.
2_26_37.NASB: 'You shall make five pillars of acacia for the screen and overlay them with gold, their hooks [also being of] gold; and you shall cast five sockets of bronze for them.
2_26_37.NLT: Craft five posts from acacia wood. Overlay them with gold, and hang the curtain from them with gold hooks. Cast five bronze bases for the posts.
2_26_37.WEB: You shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold: their hooks shall be of gold: and you shall cast five sockets of brass for them.
2_26_37.CSB17: Make five pillars of acacia wood for the screen and overlay them with gold; their hooks are to be gold, and you are to cast five bronze bases for them.
2_26_37.AMP: You shall make five pillars (support poles) of acacia wood to support the hanging curtain and overlay them with gold. Their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast five [base] sockets of bronze for them.
2_26_37.MSG: Frame the weaving with five poles of acacia wood covered with a veneer of gold and make gold hooks to hang the weaving. Cast five bronze bases for the poles.
2_26_37.NIV: Make gold hooks for this curtain and five posts of acacia wood overlaid with gold. And cast five bronze bases for them.
2_26_37.NRSVCE: You shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast five bases of bronze for them.

2_27_1.NKJV: “You shall make an altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide—the altar shall be square—and its height shall be three cubits.
2_27_1.KJV: And thou shalt make an altar of shittim wood, five cubits long, and five cubits broad; the altar shall be foursquare: and the height thereof shall be three cubits.
2_27_1.ESV: “You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits broad. The altar shall be square, and its height shall be three cubits.
2_27_1.NASB: 'And you shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square, and its height shall be three cubits.
2_27_1.NLT: “Using acacia wood, construct a square altar 7 1⁄2 feet wide, 7 1⁄2 feet long, and 4 1⁄2 feet high.
2_27_1.WEB: “You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long, and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square: and its height shall be three cubits.
2_27_1.CSB17: “You are to construct the altar of acacia wood. The altar must be square, 7½ feet long, and 7½ feet wide; it must be 4½ feet high.
2_27_1.AMP: "And you shall make the altar [for burnt offerings] of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square, and its height shall be three cubits.
2_27_1.MSG: "Make an Altar of acacia wood. Make it seven and a half feet square and four and a half feet high.
2_27_1.NIV: The Altar of Burnt Offering“Build an altar of acacia wood, three cubits high; it is to be square, five cubits long and five cubits wide.
2_27_1.NRSVCE: You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square, and it shall be three cubits high.

2_27_2.NKJV: You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it. And you shall overlay it with bronze.
2_27_2.KJV: And thou shalt make the horns of it upon the four corners thereof: his horns shall be of the same: and thou shalt overlay it with brass.
2_27_2.ESV: And you shall make horns for it on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it, and you shall overlay it with bronze.
2_27_2.NASB: 'You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it, and you shall overlay it with bronze.
2_27_2.NLT: Make horns for each of its four corners so that the horns and altar are all one piece. Overlay the altar with bronze.
2_27_2.WEB: You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it; and you shall overlay it with brass.
2_27_2.CSB17: Make horns for it on its four corners; the horns are to be of one piece. Overlay it with bronze.
2_27_2.AMP: Make horns (horn-shaped projections) for it on its four corners; the horns shall be of one piece with it, and you shall overlay it with bronze.
2_27_2.MSG: Make horns at each of the four corners. The horns are to be of one piece with the Altar and covered with a veneer of bronze.
2_27_2.NIV: Make a horn at each of the four corners, so that the horns and the altar are of one piece, and overlay the altar with bronze.
2_27_2.NRSVCE: You shall make horns for it on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it, and you shall overlay it with bronze.

2_27_3.NKJV: Also you shall make its pans to receive its ashes, and its shovels and its basins and its forks and its firepans; you shall make all its utensils of bronze.
2_27_3.KJV: And thou shalt make his pans to receive his ashes, and his shovels, and his basons, and his fleshhooks, and his firepans: all the vessels thereof thou shalt make of brass.
2_27_3.ESV: You shall make pots for it to receive its ashes, and shovels and basins and forks and fire pans. You shall make all its utensils of bronze.
2_27_3.NASB: 'You shall make its pails for removing its ashes, and its shovels and its basins and its forks and its firepans; you shall make all its utensils of bronze.
2_27_3.NLT: Make ash buckets, shovels, basins, meat forks, and firepans, all of bronze.
2_27_3.WEB: You shall make its pots to take away its ashes, its shovels, its basins, its meat hooks, and its fire pans: all its vessels you shall make of brass.
2_27_3.CSB17: Make its pots for removing ashes, and its shovels, basins, meat forks, and firepans; make all its utensils of bronze.
2_27_3.AMP: You shall make pots to remove its ashes, and shovels, basins [to catch the blood of the sacrificed animal], meat-forks, and firepans [to store live coals]. You shall make all its utensils of bronze.
2_27_3.MSG: Make buckets for removing the ashes, along with shovels, basins, forks, and fire pans. Make all these utensils from bronze.
2_27_3.NIV: Make all its utensils of bronze—its pots to remove the ashes, and its shovels, sprinkling bowls, meat forks and firepans.
2_27_3.NRSVCE: You shall make pots for it to receive its ashes, and shovels and basins and forks and firepans; you shall make all its utensils of bronze.

2_27_4.NKJV: You shall make a grate for it, a network of bronze; and on the network you shall make four bronze rings at its four corners.
2_27_4.KJV: And thou shalt make for it a grate of network of brass; and upon the net shalt thou make four brasen rings in the four corners thereof.
2_27_4.ESV: You shall also make for it a grating, a network of bronze, and on the net you shall make four bronze rings at its four corners.
2_27_4.NASB: 'You shall make for it a grating of network of bronze, and on the net you shall make four bronze rings at its four corners.
2_27_4.NLT: Make a bronze grating for it, and attach four bronze rings at its four corners.
2_27_4.WEB: You shall make a grating for it of network of brass: and on the net you shall make four bronze rings in its four corners.
2_27_4.CSB17: Construct a grate for it of bronze mesh, and make four bronze rings on the mesh at its four corners.
2_27_4.AMP: Also make a grate for it, a network of bronze; and on the grid you are to make four bronze rings at its four corners.
2_27_4.MSG: Make a grate of bronze mesh and attach bronze rings at each of the four corners.
2_27_4.NIV: Make a grating for it, a bronze network, and make a bronze ring at each of the four corners of the network.
2_27_4.NRSVCE: You shall also make for it a grating, a network of bronze; and on the net you shall make four bronze rings at its four corners.

2_27_5.NKJV: You shall put it under the rim of the altar beneath, that the network may be midway up the altar.
2_27_5.KJV: And thou shalt put it under the compass of the altar beneath, that the net may be even to the midst of the altar.
2_27_5.ESV: And you shall set it under the ledge of the altar so that the net extends halfway down the altar.
2_27_5.NASB: 'You shall put it beneath, under the ledge of the altar, so that the net will reach halfway up the altar.
2_27_5.NLT: Install the grating halfway down the side of the altar, under the ledge.
2_27_5.WEB: You shall put it under the ledge around the altar beneath, that the net may reach halfway up the altar.
2_27_5.CSB17: Set it below, under the altar’s ledge, so that the mesh comes halfway up the altar.
2_27_5.AMP: And you shall put it under the ledge of the altar, so that the grid will extend halfway up the altar.
2_27_5.MSG: Put the grate under the ledge of the Altar at the halfway point of the Altar.
2_27_5.NIV: Put it under the ledge of the altar so that it is halfway up the altar.
2_27_5.NRSVCE: You shall set it under the ledge of the altar so that the net shall extend halfway down the altar.

2_27_6.NKJV: And you shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze.
2_27_6.KJV: And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with brass.
2_27_6.ESV: And you shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze.
2_27_6.NASB: 'You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze.
2_27_6.NLT: For carrying the altar, make poles from acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze.
2_27_6.WEB: You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with brass.
2_27_6.CSB17: Then make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze.
2_27_6.AMP: You shall make [carrying] poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, overlaid with bronze.
2_27_6.MSG: Make acacia wood poles for the Altar and cover them with a veneer of bronze.
2_27_6.NIV: Make poles of acacia wood for the altar and overlay them with bronze.
2_27_6.NRSVCE: You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze;

2_27_7.NKJV: The poles shall be put in the rings, and the poles shall be on the two sides of the altar to bear it.
2_27_7.KJV: And the staves shall be put into the rings, and the staves shall be upon the two sides of the altar, to bear it.
2_27_7.ESV: And the poles shall be put through the rings, so that the poles are on the two sides of the altar when it is carried.
2_27_7.NASB: 'Its poles shall be inserted into the rings, so that the poles shall be on the two sides of the altar when it is carried.
2_27_7.NLT: Insert the poles through the rings on the two sides of the altar.
2_27_7.WEB: Its poles shall be put into the rings, and the poles shall be on the two sides of the altar, when carrying it.
2_27_7.CSB17: The poles are to be inserted into the rings so that the poles are on two sides of the altar when it is carried.
2_27_7.AMP: The poles shall be inserted through the rings on the two sides of the altar so that it may be carried.
2_27_7.MSG: Insert the poles through the rings on the two sides of the Altar for carrying.
2_27_7.NIV: The poles are to be inserted into the rings so they will be on two sides of the altar when it is carried.

2_27_21.NKJV: In the tabernacle of meeting, outside the veil which is before the Testimony, Aaron and his sons shall tend it from evening until morning before the LORD. It shall be a statute forever to their generations on behalf of the children of Israel.

2_27_21.NLT: The lampstand will stand in the Tabernacle, in front of the inner curtain that shields the Ark of the Covenant. Aaron and his sons must keep the lamps burning in the LORD’s presence all night. This is a permanent law for the people of Israel, and it must be observed from generation to generation.

It shall be a perpetual statute [to be observed] throughout their generations on behalf of the Israelites.
2_27_21.MSG: In the Tent of Meeting, the area outside the curtain that veils The Testimony, Aaron and his sons will keep this light burning from evening until morning before God. This is to be a permanent practice down through the generations for Israelites.

2_27_21.NRSVCE: In the tent of meeting, outside the curtain that is before the covenant, Aaron and his sons shall tend it from evening to morning before the LORD. It shall be a perpetual ordinance to be observed throughout their generations by the Israelites.

2_28_3.CSB17: You are to instruct all the skilled artisans, whom I have filled with a spirit of wisdom, to make Aaron’s garments for consecrating him to serve me as priest.
2_28_3.AMP: Tell all the skilled and talented people whom I have endowed with a spirit of wisdom, that they are to make Aaron's garments to sanctify him and set him apart to serve as a priest for Me.
2_28_3.MSG: Consult with the skilled craftsmen, those whom I have gifted in this work, and arrange for them to make Aaron's vestments, to set him apart as holy, to act as priest for me.
2_28_3.NIV: Tell all the skilled men to whom I have given wisdom in such matters that they are to make garments for Aaron, for his consecration, so he may serve me as priest.
2_28_3.NRSVCE: And you shall speak to all who have ability, whom I have endowed with skill, that they make Aaron’s vestments to consecrate him for my priesthood.

2_28_4.NKJV: And these are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate, an ephod, a robe, a skillfully woven tunic, a turban, and a sash. So they shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother and his sons, that he may minister to Me as priest.
2_28_4.KJV: And these are the garments which they shall make; a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a broidered coat, a mitre, and a girdle: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, and his sons, that he may minister unto me in the priest's office.
2_28_4.ESV: These are the garments that they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a coat of checker work, a turban, and a sash. They shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother and his sons to serve me as priests.
2_28_4.NASB: 'These are the garments which they shall make: a breastpiece and an ephod and a robe and a tunic of checkered work, a turban and a sash, and they shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother and his sons, that he may minister as priest to Me.
2_28_4.NLT: These are the garments they are to make: a chestpiece, an ephod, a robe, a patterned tunic, a turban, and a sash. They are to make these sacred garments for your brother, Aaron, and his sons to wear when they serve me as priests.
2_28_4.WEB: These are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a coat of checker work, a turban, and a sash: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, and his sons, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office.
2_28_4.CSB17: These are the garments that they must make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a specially woven tunic, a turban, and a sash. They are to make holy garments for your brother Aaron and his sons so that they may serve me as priests.
2_28_4.AMP: These are the garments which they shall make: a breastpiece and an ephod [for the breastpiece] and a robe and a tunic of checkered work, a turban, and a sash. They shall make sacred garments for Aaron your brother and his sons, so that he may serve as a priest to Me.
2_28_4.MSG: These are the articles of clothing they are to make: Breastpiece, Ephod, robe, woven tunic, turban, sash. They are making holy vestments for your brother Aaron and his sons as they work as priests for me.
2_28_4.NIV: These are the garments they are to make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a woven tunic, a turban and a sash. They are to make these sacred garments for your brother Aaron and his sons, so they may serve me as priests.
2_28_4.NRSVCE: These are the vestments that they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a checkered tunic, a turban, and a sash. When they make these sacred vestments for your brother Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests,

2_28_5.NASB: 'They shall take the gold and the blue and the purple and the scarlet [material] and the fine linen.

2_28_6.KJV: And they shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and of purple, of scarlet , and fine twined linen, with cunning work.
2_28_6.ESV: “And they shall make the ephod of gold, of blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and of fine twined linen, skillfully worked.
2_28_6.NASB: 'They shall also make the ephod of gold, of blue and purple [and] scarlet [material] and fine twisted linen, the work of the skillful workman.
2_28_6.NLT: “The craftsmen must make the ephod of finely woven linen and skillfully embroider it with gold and with blue, purple, and scarlet thread.

2_28_7.MSG: Give it two shoulder pieces at two of the corners so it can be fastened.

2_28_8.NKJV: And the intricately woven band of the ephod, which is on it, shall be of the same workmanship, made of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine woven linen.
2_28_8.KJV: And the curious girdle of the ephod, which is upon it, shall be of the same, according to the work thereof; even of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet , and fine twined linen. curious: or, embroidered

2_28_8.NRSVCE: The decorated band on it shall be of the same workmanship and materials, of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.

2_28_9.NRSVCE: You shall take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel,

2_28_11.NKJV: With the work of an engraver in stone, like the engravings of a signet, you shall engrave the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel. You shall set them in settings of gold.

2_28_11.NASB: 'As a jeweler engraves a signet, you shall engrave the two stones according to the names of the sons of Israel; you shall set them in filigree [settings] of gold.
2_28_11.NLT: Engrave these names on the two stones in the same way a jeweler engraves a seal. Then mount the stones in settings of gold filigree.

2_28_11.NRSVCE: As a gem-cutter engraves signets, so you shall engrave the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel; you shall mount them in settings of gold filigree.

2_28_12.NKJV: And you shall put the two stones on the shoulders of the ephod as memorial stones for the sons of Israel. So Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD on his two shoulders as a memorial.
2_28_12.KJV: And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulders of the ephod for stones of memorial unto the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD upon his two shoulders for a memorial.
2_28_12.ESV: And you shall set the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod, as stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel. And Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD on his two shoulders for remembrance.
2_28_12.NASB: 'You shall put the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod, [as] stones of memorial for the sons of Israel, and Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD on his two shoulders for a memorial.
2_28_12.NLT: Fasten the two stones on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod as a reminder that Aaron represents the people of Israel. Aaron will carry these names on his shoulders as a constant reminder whenever he goes before the LORD.
2_28_12.WEB: You shall put the two stones on the shoulder straps of the ephod, to be stones of memorial for the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before Yahweh on his two shoulders for a memorial.

2_28_12.NIV: and fasten them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the sons of Israel. Aaron is to bear the names on his shoulders as a memorial before the Lord.
2_28_12.NRSVCE: You shall set the two stones on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, as stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel; and Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD on his two shoulders for remembrance.

2_28_21.AMP: The [engraved] stones shall be twelve, according to the names of [the twelve tribes of] the sons of Israel; they shall be like the engravings of a signet, each with its name for the twelve tribes.

2_28_29.NKJV: “So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel on the breastplate of judgment over his heart, when he goes into the holy place, as a memorial before the LORD continually.

2_28_29.NASB: 'Aaron shall carry the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment over his heart when he enters the holy place, for a memorial before the LORD continually.
2_28_29.NLT: “In this way, Aaron will carry the names of the tribes of Israel on the sacred chestpiece over his heart when he goes into the Holy Place. This will be a continual reminder that he represents the people when he comes before the LORD.

2_28_29.AMP: So Aaron shall carry the names of the sons of Israel (Jacob) in the breastpiece of judgment over his heart when he enters the Holy Place, to bring them in continual remembrance before the LORD.
2_28_29.MSG: "Aaron will regularly carry the names of the sons of Israel on the Breastpiece of Judgment over his heart as he enters the Sanctuary into the presence of God for remembrance.
2_28_29.NIV: “Whenever Aaron enters the Holy Place, he will bear the names of the sons of Israel over his heart on the breastpiece of decision as a continuing memorial before the Lord.
2_28_29.NRSVCE: So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart when he goes into the holy place, for a continual remembrance before the LORD.

2_28_30.NKJV: And you shall put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be over Aaron’s heart when he goes in before the LORD. So Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel over his heart before the LORD continually.
2_28_30.KJV: And thou shalt put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be upon Aaron's heart, when he goeth in before the LORD: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before the LORD continually.
2_28_30.ESV: And in the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be on Aaron's heart, when he goes in before the LORD. Thus Aaron shall bear the judgment of the people of Israel on his heart before the LORD regularly.
2_28_30.NASB: 'You shall put in the breastpiece of judgment the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be over Aaron's heart when he goes in before the LORD; and Aaron shall carry the judgment of the sons of Israel over his heart before the LORD continually.
2_28_30.NLT: Insert the Urim and Thummim into the sacred chestpiece so they will be carried over Aaron’s heart when he goes into the LORD’s presence. In this way, Aaron will always carry over his heart the objects used to determine the LORD’s will for his people whenever he goes in before the LORD.
2_28_30.WEB: You shall put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be on Aaron’s heart, when he goes in before Yahweh: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel on his heart before Yahweh continually.
2_28_30.CSB17: Place the Urim and Thummim in the breastpiece for decisions, so that they will also be over Aaron’s heart whenever he comes before the LORD. Aaron will continually carry the means of decisions for the Israelites over his heart before the LORD.
2_28_30.AMP: In the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim (Lights) and the Thummim (Perfections) [to be used for determining God's will in a matter]. They shall be over Aaron's heart whenever he goes before the LORD, and Aaron shall always carry the judgment (verdict, judicial decisions) of the sons of Israel over his heart before the LORD.
2_28_30.MSG: Place the Urim and Thummim in the Breastpiece of Judgment. They will be over Aaron's heart when he enters the presence of God. In this way Aaron will regularly carry the Breastpiece of Judgment into the presence of God.
2_28_30.NIV: Also put the Urim and the Thummim in the breastpiece, so they may be over Aaron’s heart whenever he enters the presence of the Lord. Thus Aaron will always bear the means of making decisions for the Israelites over his heart before the Lord.
2_28_30.NRSVCE: In the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be on Aaron’s heart when he goes in before the LORD; thus Aaron shall bear the judgment of the Israelites on his heart before the LORD continually.

2_28_38.NKJV: So it shall be on Aaron’s forehead, that Aaron may bear the iniquity of the holy things which the children of Israel hallow in all their holy gifts; and it shall always be on his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.
2_28_38.KJV: And it shall be upon Aaron's forehead, that Aaron may bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always upon his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.
2_28_38.ESV: It shall be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall bear any guilt from the holy things that the people of Israel consecrate as their holy gifts. It shall regularly be on his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.
2_28_38.NASB: 'It shall be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall take away the iniquity of the holy things which the sons of Israel consecrate, with regard to all their holy gifts; and it shall always be on his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.
2_28_38.NLT: Aaron must wear it on his forehead so he may take on himself any guilt of the people of Israel when they consecrate their sacred offerings. He must always wear it on his forehead so the LORD will accept the people.
2_28_38.WEB: It shall be on Aaron’s forehead, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall make holy in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always on his forehead, that they may be accepted before Yahweh.
2_28_38.CSB17: It will be on Aaron’s forehead so that Aaron may bear the guilt connected with the holy offerings that the Israelites consecrate as all their holy gifts. It is always to be on his forehead, so that they may find acceptance with the LORD.
2_28_38.AMP: It shall be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall take away the guilt from the holy things which the sons of Israel dedicate, with regard to all their holy gifts. It shall always be on his forehead, so that they may be accepted before the LORD.
2_28_38.MSG: It is to rest there on Aaron's forehead. He'll take on any guilt involved in the sacred offerings that the Israelites consecrate, no matter what they bring. It will always be on Aaron's forehead so that the offerings will be acceptable before God.
2_28_38.NIV: It will be on Aaron’s forehead, and he will bear the guilt involved in the sacred gifts the Israelites consecrate, whatever their gifts may be. It will be on Aaron’s forehead continually so that they will be acceptable to the Lord.
2_28_38.NRSVCE: It shall be on Aaron’s forehead, and Aaron shall take on himself any guilt incurred in the holy offering that the Israelites consecrate as their sacred donations; it shall always be on his forehead, in order that they may find favor before the LORD.

2_29_27.KJV: And thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the shoulder of the heave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of the consecration, even of that which is for Aaron, and of that which is for his sons:
2_29_27.ESV: And you shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering that is waved and the thigh of the priests' portion that is contributed from the ram of ordination, from what was Aaron's and his sons'.
2_29_27.NASB: 'You shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering which was waved and which was offered from the ram of ordination, from the one which was for Aaron and from the one which was for his sons.
2_29_27.NLT: “Set aside the portions of the ordination ram that belong to Aaron and his sons. This includes the breast and the thigh that were lifted up before the LORD as a special offering.
2_29_27.WEB: You shall sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the thigh of the wave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of consecration, even of that which is for Aaron, and of that which is for his sons:
2_29_27.CSB17: Consecrate for Aaron and his sons the breast of the presentation offering that is presented and the thigh of the contribution that is lifted up from the ram of ordination.
2_29_27.AMP: You shall consecrate the waved breast offering [of the ram] used in the ordination and the waved thigh offering of the priests' portion, since it is [a contribution] for Aaron and for his sons.
2_29_27.MSG: "Consecrate the Wave-Offering breast and the thigh that was held up. These are the parts of the ordination ram that are for Aaron and his sons.
2_29_27.NIV: “Consecrate those parts of the ordination ram that belong to Aaron and his sons: the breast that was waved and the thigh that was presented.
2_29_27.NRSVCE: You shall consecrate the breast that was raised as an elevation offering and the thigh that was raised as an elevation offering from the ram of ordination, from that which belonged to Aaron and his sons.

2_29_28.NKJV: It shall be from the children of Israel for Aaron and his sons by a statute forever. For it is a heave offering; it shall be a heave offering from the children of Israel from the sacrifices of their peace offerings, that is, their heave offering to the LORD.
2_29_28.KJV: And it shall be Aaron's and his sons' by a statute for ever from the children of Israel: for it is an heave offering: and it shall be an heave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifice of their peace offerings, even their heave offering unto the LORD.
2_29_28.ESV: It shall be for Aaron and his sons as a perpetual due from the people of Israel, for it is a contribution. It shall be a contribution from the people of Israel from their peace offerings, their contribution to the LORD.
2_29_28.NASB: 'It shall be for Aaron and his sons as [their] portion forever from the sons of Israel, for it is a heave offering; and it shall be a heave offering from the sons of Israel from the sacrifices of their peace offerings, [even] their heave offering to the LORD.
2_29_28.NLT: In the future, whenever the people of Israel lift up a peace offering, a portion of it must be set aside for Aaron and his descendants. This is their permanent right, and it is a sacred offering from the Israelites to the LORD.
2_29_28.WEB: and it shall be for Aaron and his sons as their portion forever from the children of Israel; for it is a wave offering: and it shall be a wave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifices of their peace offerings, even their wave offering to Yahweh.
2_29_28.CSB17: This will belong to Aaron and his sons as a regular portion from the Israelites, for it is a contribution. It will be the Israelites’ contribution from their fellowship sacrifices, their contribution to the LORD.
2_29_28.AMP: It shall be for Aaron and his sons as their due portion from the Israelites forever, for it is a heave offering. It shall be a heave offering to the LORD from the Israelites from the sacrifices of their peace offerings.
2_29_28.MSG: Aaron and his sons are always to get this offering from the Israelites; the Israelites are to make this offering regularly from their Peace-Offerings.
2_29_28.NIV: This is always to be the regular share from the Israelites for Aaron and his sons. It is the contribution the Israelites are to make to the Lord from their fellowship offerings.
2_29_28.NRSVCE: These things shall be a perpetual ordinance for Aaron and his sons from the Israelites, for this is an offering; and it shall be an offering by the Israelites from their sacrifice of offerings of well-being, their offering to the LORD.

2_29_29.NKJV: “And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons’ after him, to be anointed in them and to be consecrated in them.
2_29_29.KJV: And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him, to be anointed therein, and to be consecrated in them.
2_29_29.ESV: “The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him; they shall be anointed in them and ordained in them.
2_29_29.NASB: 'The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him, that in them they may be anointed and ordained.
2_29_29.NLT: “Aaron’s sacred garments must be preserved for his descendants who succeed him, and they will wear them when they are anointed and ordained.
2_29_29.WEB: “The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him, to be anointed in them, and to be consecrated in them.
2_29_29.CSB17: “The holy garments that belong to Aaron are to belong to his sons after him, so that they can be anointed and ordained in them.
2_29_29.AMP: "The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him, to be anointed and ordained in them.
2_29_29.MSG: "Aaron's sacred garments are to be handed down to his descendants so they can be anointed and ordained in them.
2_29_29.NIV: “Aaron’s sacred garments will belong to his descendants so that they can be anointed and ordained in them.
2_29_29.NRSVCE: The sacred vestments of Aaron shall be passed on to his sons after him; they shall be anointed in them and ordained in them.

2_29_30.NKJV: That son who becomes priest in his place shall put them on for seven days, when he enters the tabernacle of meeting to minister in the holy place.
2_29_30.KJV: And that son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days, when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister in the holy place. that son: Heb. he of his sons
2_29_30.ESV: The son who succeeds him as priest, who comes into the tent of meeting to minister in the Holy Place, shall wear them seven days.
2_29_30.NASB: 'For seven days the one of his sons who is priest in his stead shall put them on when he enters the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
2_29_30.NLT: The descendant who succeeds him as high priest will wear these clothes for seven days as he ministers in the Tabernacle and the Holy Place.
2_29_30.WEB: Seven days shall the son who is priest in his place put them on, when he comes into the Tent of Meeting to minister in the holy place.
2_29_30.CSB17: Any priest who is one of his sons and who succeeds him and enters the tent of meeting to minister in the sanctuary must wear them for seven days.
2_29_30.AMP: That son who is [high] priest in his place shall put them on [each day for] seven days when he comes into the Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy Place.
2_29_30.MSG: The son who succeeds him as priest is to wear them for seven days and enter the Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy Place.
2_29_30.NIV: The son who succeeds him as priest and comes to the Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy Place is to wear them seven days.
2_29_30.NRSVCE: The son who is priest in his place shall wear them seven days, when he comes into the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.

2_29_31.NKJV: “And you shall take the ram of the consecration and boil its flesh in the holy place.
2_29_31.KJV: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and seethe his flesh in the holy place.
2_29_31.ESV: “You shall take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
2_29_31.NASB: 'You shall take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
2_29_31.NLT: “Take the ram used in the ordination ceremony, and boil its meat in a sacred place.
2_29_31.WEB: “You shall take the ram of consecration, and boil its meat in a holy place.
2_29_31.CSB17: “You are to take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
2_29_31.AMP: "You shall take the ram of the ordination and boil its meat in a holy place.
2_29_31.MSG: "Take the ordination ram and boil the meat in the Holy Place.
2_29_31.NIV: “Take the ram for the ordination and cook the meat in a sacred place.
2_29_31.NRSVCE: You shall take the ram of ordination, and boil its flesh in a holy place;

2_29_32.NKJV: Then Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, by the door of the tabernacle of meeting.
2_29_32.KJV: And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
2_29_32.ESV: And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket in the entrance of the tent of meeting.
2_29_32.NASB: 'Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket, at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
2_29_32.NLT: Then Aaron and his sons will eat this meat, along with the bread in the basket, at the Tabernacle entrance.
2_29_32.WEB: Aaron and his sons shall eat the meat of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
2_29_32.CSB17: Aaron and his sons are to eat the meat of the ram and the bread that is in the basket at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
2_29_32.AMP: Aaron and his sons shall eat the meat of the ram and the bread in the basket, at the doorway of the Tent of Meeting.
2_29_32.MSG: At the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, Aaron and his sons will eat the boiled ram and the bread that is in the basket.
2_29_32.NIV: At the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, Aaron and his sons are to eat the meat of the ram and the bread that is in the basket.
2_29_32.NRSVCE: and Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

2_29_33.NKJV: They shall eat those things with which the atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them; but an outsider shall not eat them, because they are holy.
2_29_33.KJV: And they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy.
2_29_33.ESV: They shall eat those things with which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration, but an outsider shall not eat of them, because they are holy.
2_29_33.NASB: 'Thus they shall eat those things by which atonement was made at their ordination [and] consecration; but a layman shall not eat [them], because they are holy.
2_29_33.NLT: They alone may eat the meat and bread used for their purification in the ordination ceremony. No one else may eat them, for these things are set apart and holy.
2_29_33.WEB: They shall eat those things with which atonement was made, to consecrate and sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat of it, because they are holy.
2_29_33.CSB17: They must eat those things by which atonement was made at the time of their ordination and consecration. An unauthorized person must not eat them, for these things are holy.
2_29_33.AMP: They shall eat those things by which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration; but a layman shall not eat them, because they are holy [that is, set apart to the worship of God].
2_29_33.MSG: Atoned by these offerings, ordained and consecrated by them, they are the only ones who are to eat them. No outsiders are to eat them; they're holy.
2_29_33.NIV: They are to eat these offerings by which atonement was made for their ordination and consecration. But no one else may eat them, because they are sacred.
2_29_33.NRSVCE: They themselves shall eat the food by which atonement is made, to ordain and consecrate them, but no one else shall eat of them, because they are holy.

2_29_34.NKJV: And if any of the flesh of the consecration offerings, or of the bread, remains until the morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire. It shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
2_29_34.KJV: And if ought of the flesh of the consecrations, or of the bread, remain unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
2_29_34.ESV: And if any of the flesh for the ordination or of the bread remain until the morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire. It shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
2_29_34.NASB: 'If any of the flesh of ordination or any of the bread remains until morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire; it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
2_29_34.NLT: If any of the ordination meat or bread remains until the morning, it must be burned. It may not be eaten, for it is holy.
2_29_34.WEB: If anything of the meat of the consecration, or of the bread, remains to the morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
2_29_34.CSB17: If any of the meat of ordination or any of the bread is left until morning, burn what is left over. It must not be eaten because it is holy.
2_29_34.AMP: And if any of the meat of ordination or the bread remains until morning, you shall burn it in the fire; it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
2_29_34.MSG: Anything from the ordination ram or from the bread that is left over until morning you are to burn up. Don't eat it; it's holy.
2_29_34.NIV: And if any of the meat of the ordination ram or any bread is left over till morning, burn it up. It must not be eaten, because it is sacred.
2_29_34.NRSVCE: If any of the flesh for the ordination, or of the bread, remains until the morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire; it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.

2_29_35.NKJV: “Thus you shall do to Aaron and his sons, according to all that I have commanded you. Seven days you shall consecrate them.
2_29_35.KJV: And thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons, according to all things which I have commanded thee: seven days shalt thou consecrate them.
2_29_35.ESV: “Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded you. Through seven days shall you ordain them,
2_29_35.NASB: 'Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded you; you shall ordain them through seven days.
2_29_35.NLT: “This is how you will ordain Aaron and his sons to their offices, just as I have commanded you. The ordination ceremony will go on for seven days.
2_29_35.WEB: “You shall do so to Aaron, and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded you. You shall consecrate them seven days.
2_29_35.CSB17: “This is what you are to do for Aaron and his sons based on all I have commanded you. Take seven days to ordain them.
2_29_35.AMP: "So you shall do to Aaron and to his sons in accordance with all I have commanded you; during seven days you are to ordain them.
2_29_35.MSG: "Do everything for the ordination of Aaron and his sons exactly as I've commanded you throughout the seven days.
2_29_35.NIV: “Do for Aaron and his sons everything I have commanded you, taking seven days to ordain them.
2_29_35.NRSVCE: Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons, just as I have commanded you; through seven days you shall ordain them.

2_29_36.NKJV: And you shall offer a bull every day as a sin offering for atonement. You shall cleanse the altar when you make atonement for it, and you shall anoint it to sanctify it.
2_29_36.KJV: And thou shalt offer every day a bullock for a sin offering for atonement: and thou shalt cleanse the altar, when thou hast made an atonement for it, and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.
2_29_36.ESV: and every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Also you shall purify the altar, when you make atonement for it, and shall anoint it to consecrate it.
2_29_36.NASB: 'Each day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement, and you shall purify the altar when you make atonement for it, and you shall anoint it to consecrate it.
2_29_36.NLT: Each day you must sacrifice a young bull as a sin offering to purify them, making them right with the LORD. Afterward, cleanse the altar by purifying it ; make it holy by anointing it with oil.
2_29_36.WEB: Every day you shall offer the bull of sin offering for atonement: and you shall cleanse the altar, when you make atonement for it; and you shall anoint it, to sanctify it.
2_29_36.CSB17: Sacrifice a bull as a sin offering each day for atonement. Purify the altar when you make atonement for it, and anoint it in order to consecrate it.
2_29_36.AMP: You shall offer a bull every day as a sin offering for atonement. You shall cleanse the altar from sin when you make atonement for it, and you shall anoint it to consecrate it [for God's sacred purpose].
2_29_36.MSG: Offer a bull as an Absolution-Offering for atonement each day. Offer it on the Altar when you make atonement for it: Anoint and consecrate it.
2_29_36.NIV: Sacrifice a bull each day as a sin offering to make atonement. Purify the altar by making atonement for it, and anoint it to consecrate it.
2_29_36.NRSVCE: Also every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Also you shall offer a sin offering for the altar, when you make atonement for it, and shall anoint it, to consecrate it.

2_29_37.NKJV: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and sanctify it. And the altar shall be most holy. Whatever touches the altar must be holy.
2_29_37.KJV: Seven days thou shalt make an atonement for the altar, and sanctify it; and it shall be an altar most holy : whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy.
2_29_37.ESV: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it, and the altar shall be most holy. Whatever touches the altar shall become holy.
2_29_37.NASB: 'For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it; then the altar shall be most holy, [and] whatever touches the altar shall be holy.
2_29_37.NLT: Purify the altar, and consecrate it every day for seven days. After that, the altar will be absolutely holy, and whatever touches it will become holy.
2_29_37.WEB: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar, and sanctify it: and the altar shall be most holy; whatever touches the altar shall be holy.
2_29_37.CSB17: For seven days you must make atonement for the altar and consecrate it. The altar will be especially holy. Whatever touches the altar will be consecrated.
2_29_37.AMP: For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar [of burnt offering] and consecrate it; then the altar shall be most holy. Whatever touches the altar must be holy (set apart for God's service).
2_29_37.MSG: Make atonement for the Altar and consecrate it for seven days; the Altar will become soaked in holiness—anyone who so much as touches the Altar will become holy.
2_29_37.NIV: For seven days make atonement for the altar and consecrate it. Then the altar will be most holy, and whatever touches it will be holy.
2_29_37.NRSVCE: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar, and consecrate it, and the altar shall be most holy; whatever touches the altar shall become holy.

2_29_38.NKJV: “Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs of the first year, day by day continually.
2_29_38.KJV: Now this is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar; two lambs of the first year day by day continually.
2_29_38.ESV: “Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old day by day regularly.
2_29_38.NASB: 'Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two one year old lambs each day, continuously.
2_29_38.NLT: “These are the sacrifices you are to offer regularly on the altar. Each day, offer two lambs that are a year old,
2_29_38.WEB: “Now this is that which you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old day by day continually.
2_29_38.CSB17: “This is what you are to offer regularly on the altar every day: two year-old lambs.
2_29_38.AMP: "Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two one year old lambs shall be offered each day, continuously.
2_29_38.MSG: "This is what you are to offer on the Altar: two year-old lambs each and every day,
2_29_38.NIV: “This is what you are to offer on the altar regularly each day: two lambs a year old.
2_29_38.NRSVCE: Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old regularly each day.

2_29_39.NKJV: One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer at twilight.
2_29_39.KJV: The one lamb thou shalt offer in the morning; and the other lamb thou shalt offer at even:
2_29_39.ESV: One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer at twilight.
2_29_39.NASB: 'The one lamb you shall offer in the morning and the other lamb you shall offer at twilight;
2_29_39.NLT: one in the morning and the other in the evening.
2_29_39.WEB: The one lamb you shall offer in the morning; and the other lamb you shall offer at evening:
2_29_39.CSB17: In the morning offer one lamb, and at twilight offer the other lamb.
2_29_39.AMP: One lamb you shall offer in the morning and the other lamb at twilight;
2_29_39.MSG: one lamb in the morning and the second lamb at evening.
2_29_39.NIV: Offer one in the morning and the other at twilight.
2_29_39.NRSVCE: One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer in the evening;

2_29_40.NKJV: With the one lamb shall be one-tenth of an ephah of flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of pressed oil, and one-fourth of a hin of wine as a drink offering.
2_29_40.KJV: And with the one lamb a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of an hin of wine for a drink offering.
2_29_40.ESV: And with the first lamb a tenth measure of fine flour mingled with a fourth of a hin of beaten oil, and a fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering.
2_29_40.NASB: and there [shall be] one-tenth [of an ephah] of fine flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil, and one-fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering with one lamb.
2_29_40.NLT: With one of them, offer two quarts of choice flour mixed with one quart of pure oil of pressed olives; also, offer one quart of wine as a liquid offering.
2_29_40.WEB: and with the one lamb a tenth part of an ephah of fine flour mixed with the fourth part of a hin of beaten oil, and the fourth part of a hin of wine for a drink offering.
2_29_40.CSB17: With the first lamb offer two quarts of fine flour mixed with one quart of oil from crushed olives, and a drink offering of one quart of wine.
2_29_40.AMP: and with the one lamb there shall be one-tenth of a measure of fine flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten [olive] oil, and one-fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering [to be poured out].
2_29_40.MSG: With the sacrifice of the first lamb offer two quarts of fine flour with a quart of virgin olive oil, plus a quart of wine for a Drink-Offering.
2_29_40.NIV: With the first lamb offer a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a quarter of a hin of oil from pressed olives, and a quarter of a hin of wine as a drink offering.
2_29_40.NRSVCE: and with the first lamb one-tenth of a measure of choice flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil, and one-fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering.

2_29_41.NKJV: And the other lamb you shall offer at twilight; and you shall offer with it the grain offering and the drink offering, as in the morning, for a sweet aroma, an offering made by fire to the LORD.
2_29_41.KJV: And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering of the morning, and according to the drink offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
2_29_41.ESV: The other lamb you shall offer at twilight, and shall offer with it a grain offering and its drink offering, as in the morning, for a pleasing aroma, a food offering to the LORD.
2_29_41.NASB: 'The other lamb you shall offer at twilight, and shall offer with it the same grain offering and the same drink offering as in the morning, for a soothing aroma, an offering by fire to the LORD.
2_29_41.NLT: Offer the other lamb in the evening, along with the same offerings of flour and wine as in the morning. It will be a pleasing aroma, a special gift presented to the LORD.
2_29_41.WEB: The other lamb you shall offer at evening, and shall do to it according to the meal offering of the morning, and according to its drink offering, for a pleasant aroma, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.
2_29_41.CSB17: You are to offer the second lamb at twilight. Offer a grain offering and a drink offering with it, like the one in the morning, as a pleasing aroma, a fire offering to the LORD.
2_29_41.AMP: And the other lamb you shall offer at twilight, and do with it as with the grain offering of the morning and with the drink offering, for a sweet and soothing aroma [to appease God], an offering by fire to the LORD.
2_29_41.MSG: The sacrifice of the second lamb, the one at evening, is also to be accompanied by the same Grain-Offering and Drink-Offering of the morning sacrifice to give a pleasing fragrance, a gift to God.
2_29_41.NIV: Sacrifice the other lamb at twilight with the same grain offering and its drink offering as in the morning—a pleasing aroma, an offering made to the Lord by fire.
2_29_41.NRSVCE: And the other lamb you shall offer in the evening, and shall offer with it a grain offering and its drink offering, as in the morning, for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the LORD.

2_29_42.NKJV: This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tabernacle of meeting before the LORD, where I will meet you to speak with you.
2_29_42.KJV: This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD: where I will meet you, to speak there unto thee.
2_29_42.ESV: It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.
2_29_42.NASB: 'It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the doorway of the tent of meeting before the LORD, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.
2_29_42.NLT: “These burnt offerings are to be made each day from generation to generation. Offer them in the LORD’s presence at the Tabernacle entrance; there I will meet with you and speak with you.
2_29_42.WEB: It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the Tent of Meeting before Yahweh, where I will meet with you, to speak there to you.
2_29_42.CSB17: This will be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance to the tent of meeting before the LORD, where I will meet you to speak with you.
2_29_42.AMP: This will be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the doorway of the Tent of Meeting before the LORD, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.
2_29_42.MSG: "This is to be your regular, daily Whole-Burnt-Offering before God, generation after generation, sacrificed at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. That's where I'll meet you; that's where I'll speak with you;
2_29_42.NIV: “For the generations to come this burnt offering is to be made regularly at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting before the Lord. There I will meet you and speak to you;
2_29_42.NRSVCE: It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.

2_29_43.NKJV: And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and the tabernacle shall be sanctified by My glory.
2_29_43.KJV: And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and the tabernacle shall be sanctified by my glory. the tabernacle: or, Israel
2_29_43.ESV: There I will meet with the people of Israel, and it shall be sanctified by my glory.
2_29_43.NASB: 'I will meet there with the sons of Israel, and it shall be consecrated by My glory.
2_29_43.NLT: I will meet the people of Israel there, in the place made holy by my glorious presence.
2_29_43.WEB: There I will meet with the children of Israel; and the place shall be sanctified by my glory.
2_29_43.CSB17: I will also meet with the Israelites there, and that place will be consecrated by my glory.

2_29_43.NIV: there also I will meet with the Israelites, and the place will be consecrated by my glory.
2_29_43.NRSVCE: I will meet with the Israelites there, and it shall be sanctified by my glory;

2_29_44.NKJV: So I will consecrate the tabernacle of meeting and the altar. I will also consecrate both Aaron and his sons to minister to Me as priests.
