2_29_44.NASB: 'I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; I will also consecrate Aaron and his sons to minister as priests to Me.
2_29_44.NLT: Yes, I will consecrate the Tabernacle and the altar, and I will consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests.
2_29_44.WEB: I will sanctify the Tent of Meeting and the altar: Aaron also and his sons I will sanctify, to minister to me in the priest’s office.
2_29_44.CSB17: I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; I will also consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests.
2_29_44.AMP: I will sanctify the Tent of Meeting and the altar [of burnt offering]; also I will sanctify Aaron and his sons to serve as priests to Me.
2_29_44.MSG: I'll make the Tent of Meeting and the Altar holy. I'll make Aaron and his sons holy in order to serve me as priests.
2_29_44.NIV: “So I will consecrate the Tent of Meeting and the altar and will consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests.
2_29_44.NRSVCE: I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; Aaron also and his sons I will consecrate, to serve me as priests.

2_29_45.NKJV: I will dwell among the children of Israel and will be their God.
2_29_45.KJV: And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.
2_29_45.ESV: I will dwell among the people of Israel and will be their God.
2_29_45.NASB: 'I will dwell among the sons of Israel and will be their God.
2_29_45.NLT: Then I will live among the people of Israel and be their God,
2_29_45.WEB: I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.
2_29_45.CSB17: I will dwell among the Israelites and be their God.
2_29_45.AMP: I will dwell among the sons of Israel and be their God.
2_29_45.MSG: I'll move in and live with the Israelites. I'll be their God.
2_29_45.NIV: Then I will dwell among the Israelites and be their God.
2_29_45.NRSVCE: I will dwell among the Israelites, and I will be their God.

2_29_46.NKJV: And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them up out of the land of Egypt, that I may dwell among them. I am the LORD their God.
2_29_46.KJV: And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, that brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, that I may dwell among them: I am the LORD their God.
2_29_46.ESV: And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt that I might dwell among them. I am the LORD their God.
2_29_46.NASB: 'They shall know that I am the LORD their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt, that I might dwell among them; I am the LORD their God.
2_29_46.NLT: and they will know that I am the LORD their God. I am the one who brought them out of the land of Egypt so that I could live among them. I am the LORD their God.
2_29_46.WEB: They shall know that I am Yahweh their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, that I might dwell among them: I am Yahweh their God.
2_29_46.CSB17: And they will know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, so that I might dwell among them. I am the LORD their God.
2_29_46.AMP: They shall know [from personal experience] and acknowledge that I am the LORD their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt so that I might dwell among them; I am the LORD their God.
2_29_46.MSG: They'll realize that I am their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt so that I could live with them. I am God, your God.
2_29_46.NIV: They will know that I am the Lord their God, who brought them out of Egypt so that I might dwell among them. I am the Lord their God.
2_29_46.NRSVCE: And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt that I might dwell among them; I am the LORD their God.

2_30_1.NKJV: “You shall make an altar to burn incense on; you shall make it of acacia wood.
2_30_1.KJV: And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: of shittim wood shalt thou make it.
2_30_1.ESV: “You shall make an altar on which to burn incense; you shall make it of acacia wood.
2_30_1.NASB: 'Moreover, you shall make an altar as a place for burning incense; you shall make it of acacia wood.
2_30_1.NLT: “Then make another altar of acacia wood for burning incense.
2_30_1.WEB: “You shall make an altar to burn incense on. You shall make it of acacia wood.
2_30_1.CSB17: “You are to make an altar for the burning of incense; make it of acacia wood.
2_30_1.AMP: "You shall make an altar upon which to burn incense; you shall make it of acacia wood.
2_30_1.MSG: "Make an Altar for burning incense. Construct it from acacia wood,
2_30_1.NIV: The Altar of Incense“Make an altar of acacia wood for burning incense.
2_30_1.NRSVCE: You shall make an altar on which to offer incense; you shall make it of acacia wood.

2_30_2.NKJV: A cubit shall be its length and a cubit its width—it shall be square—and two cubits shall be its height. Its horns shall be of one piece with it.
2_30_2.KJV: A cubit shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits shall be the height thereof: the horns thereof shall be of the same.
2_30_2.ESV: A cubit shall be its length, and a cubit its breadth. It shall be square, and two cubits shall be its height. Its horns shall be of one piece with it.
2_30_2.NASB: 'Its length [shall be] a cubit, and its width a cubit, it shall be square, and its height [shall be] two cubits; its horns [shall be] of one piece with it.
2_30_2.NLT: Make it 18 inches square and 36 inches high, with horns at the corners carved from the same piece of wood as the altar itself.
2_30_2.WEB: Its length shall be a cubit, and its width a cubit. It shall be square, and its height shall be two cubits. Its horns shall be of one piece with it.
2_30_2.CSB17: It must be square, eighteen inches long and eighteen inches wide; it must be thirty-six inches high. Its horns must be of one piece with it.
2_30_2.AMP: It shall be a cubit long and a cubit wide. It shall be square and it shall be two cubits high. Its horns of one piece with it.
2_30_2.MSG: one and one-half feet square and three feet high with its horns of one piece with it.
2_30_2.NIV: It is to be square, a cubit long and a cubit wide, and two cubits high—its horns of one piece with it.
2_30_2.NRSVCE: It shall be one cubit long, and one cubit wide; it shall be square, and shall be two cubits high; its horns shall be of one piece with it.

2_30_3.NKJV: And you shall overlay its top, its sides all around, and its horns with pure gold; and you shall make for it a molding of gold all around.
2_30_3.KJV: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about. top: Heb. roof sides: Heb. walls
2_30_3.ESV: You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top and around its sides and its horns. And you shall make a molding of gold around it.
2_30_3.NASB: 'You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top and its sides all around, and its horns; and you shall make a gold molding all around for it.
2_30_3.NLT: Overlay the top, sides, and horns of the altar with pure gold, and run a gold molding around the entire altar.
2_30_3.WEB: You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top, its sides around it, and its horns; and you shall make a gold molding around it.
2_30_3.CSB17: Overlay its top, all around its sides, and its horns with pure gold; make a gold molding all around it.
2_30_3.AMP: You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top and its sides all around, and its horns; and you shall make a gold molding all around it.
2_30_3.MSG: Cover it with a veneer of pure gold, its top, sides, and horns, and make a gold molding around it
2_30_3.NIV: Overlay the top and all the sides and the horns with pure gold, and make a gold molding around it.
2_30_3.NRSVCE: You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top, and its sides all around and its horns; and you shall make for it a molding of gold all around.

2_30_4.NKJV: Two gold rings you shall make for it, under the molding on both its sides. You shall place them on its two sides, and they will be holders for the poles with which to bear it.
2_30_4.KJV: And two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it, by the two corners thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make it; and they shall be for places for the staves to bear it withal. corners: Heb. ribs
2_30_4.ESV: And you shall make two golden rings for it. Under its molding on two opposite sides of it you shall make them, and they shall be holders for poles with which to carry it.
2_30_4.NASB: 'You shall make two gold rings for it under its molding; you shall make [them] on its two side walls-- on opposite sides-- and they shall be holders for poles with which to carry it.
2_30_4.NLT: Make two gold rings, and attach them on opposite sides of the altar below the gold molding to hold the carrying poles.
2_30_4.WEB: You shall make two golden rings for it under its molding; on its two ribs, on its two sides you shall make them; and they shall be for places for poles with which to bear it.
2_30_4.CSB17: Make two gold rings for it under the molding on two of its sides; put these on opposite sides of it to be holders for the poles to carry it with.
2_30_4.AMP: You shall make two gold rings under its molding, make them on the two side walls—on opposite sides—they shall be holders for the poles with which to carry it.
2_30_4.MSG: with two rings of gold beneath the molding. Place the rings on the two opposing sides to serve as holders for poles by which it will be carried.
2_30_4.NIV: Make two gold rings for the altar below the molding—two on opposite sides—to hold the poles used to carry it.
2_30_4.NRSVCE: And you shall make two golden rings for it; under its molding on two opposite sides of it you shall make them, and they shall hold the poles with which to carry it.

2_30_5.NKJV: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.

2_30_5.ESV: You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
2_30_5.NASB: 'You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
2_30_5.NLT: Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
2_30_5.WEB: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.
2_30_5.CSB17: Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
2_30_5.AMP: You shall make the poles of acacia wood overlaid with gold.
2_30_5.MSG: Make the poles of acacia wood and cover them with a veneer of gold.
2_30_5.NIV: Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
2_30_5.NRSVCE: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.

2_30_6.NKJV: And you shall put it before the veil that is before the ark of the Testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the Testimony, where I will meet with you.
2_30_6.KJV: And thou shalt put it before the vail that is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.

2_30_6.NASB: 'You shall put this altar in front of the veil that is near the ark of the testimony, in front of the mercy seat that is over [the ark of] the testimony, where I will meet with you.
2_30_6.NLT: Place the incense altar just outside the inner curtain that shields the Ark of the Covenant, in front of the Ark’s cover — the place of atonement — that covers the tablets inscribed with the terms of the covenant. I will meet with you there.

2_30_7.NKJV: “Aaron shall burn on it sweet incense every morning; when he tends the lamps, he shall burn incense on it.

2_30_7.ESV: And Aaron shall burn fragrant incense on it. Every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall burn it,

2_30_8.NKJV: And when Aaron lights the lamps at twilight, he shall burn incense on it, a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations.
2_30_8.KJV: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations. lighteth: or setteth up: Heb. causeth to ascend at even: Heb. between the two evenings

2_30_8.NRSVCE: and when Aaron sets up the lamps in the evening, he shall offer it, a regular incense offering before the LORD throughout your generations.

2_30_9.NKJV: You shall not offer strange incense on it, or a burnt offering, or a grain offering; nor shall you pour a drink offering on it.
2_30_9.KJV: Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; neither shall ye pour drink offering thereon.

2_30_10.NKJV: And Aaron shall make atonement upon its horns once a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonement; once a year he shall make atonement upon it throughout your generations. It is most holy to the LORD.”
2_30_10.KJV: And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements: once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it is most holy unto the LORD.

2_30_10.MSG: Once a year Aaron is to purify the Altar horns. Using the blood of the Absolution-Offering of atonement, he is to make this atonement every year down through the generations. It is most holy to God."
2_30_10.NIV: Once a year Aaron shall make atonement on its horns. This annual atonement must be made with the blood of the atoning sin offering for the generations to come. It is most holy to the Lord.”
2_30_10.NRSVCE: Once a year Aaron shall perform the rite of atonement on its horns. Throughout your generations he shall perform the atonement for it once a year with the blood of the atoning sin offering. It is most holy to the LORD.

2_30_12.NKJV: “When you take the census of the children of Israel for their number, then every man shall give a ransom for himself to the LORD, when you number them, that there may be no plague among them when you number them.
2_30_12.KJV: When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the LORD, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when thou numberest them. their number: Heb. them that are to be numbered
2_30_12.ESV: “When you take the census of the people of Israel, then each shall give a ransom for his life to the LORD when you number them, that there be no plague among them when you number them.
2_30_12.NASB: 'When you take a census of the sons of Israel to number them, then each one of them shall give a ransom for himself to the LORD, when you number them, so that there will be no plague among them when you number them.
2_30_12.NLT: “Whenever you take a census of the people of Israel, each man who is counted must pay a ransom for himself to the LORD. Then no plague will strike the people as you count them.
2_30_12.WEB: “When you take a census of the children of Israel, according to those who are counted among them, then each man shall give a ransom for his soul to Yahweh, when you count them; that there be no plague among them when you count them.
2_30_12.CSB17: “When you take a census of the Israelites to register them, each of the men must pay a ransom for his life to the LORD as they are registered. Then no plague will come on them as they are registered.
2_30_12.AMP: "When you take the census of the Israelites, each one shall give a ransom for himself to the LORD when you count them, so that no plague will come on them when you number them.
2_30_12.MSG: "When you take a head count of the Israelites to keep track of them, all must pay an atonement-tax to God for their life at the time of being registered so that nothing bad will happen because of the registration.
2_30_12.NIV: “When you take a census of the Israelites to count them, each one must pay the Lord a ransom for his life at the time he is counted. Then no plague will come on them when you number them.
2_30_12.NRSVCE: When you take a census of the Israelites to register them, at registration all of them shall give a ransom for their lives to the LORD, so that no plague may come upon them for being registered.

2_30_13.NKJV: This is what everyone among those who are numbered shall give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (a shekel is twenty gerahs). The half-shekel shall be an offering to the LORD.
2_30_13.KJV: This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel is twenty gerahs:) an half shekel shall be the offering of the LORD.
2_30_13.ESV: Each one who is numbered in the census shall give this: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs), half a shekel as an offering to the LORD.
2_30_13.NASB: 'This is what everyone who is numbered shall give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs), half a shekel as a contribution to the LORD.
2_30_13.NLT: Each person who is counted must give a small piece of silver as a sacred offering to the LORD. (This payment is half a shekel, based on the sanctuary shekel, which equals twenty gerahs.)
2_30_13.WEB: They shall give this, everyone who passes over to those who are counted, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs ); half a shekel for an offering to Yahweh.
2_30_13.CSB17: Everyone who is registered must pay half a shekel according to the sanctuary shekel (twenty gerahs to the shekel). This half shekel is a contribution to the LORD.
2_30_13.AMP: This is what everyone who is counted shall give [as he joins those already counted]: a half shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel (the shekel is twenty gerahs); a half shekel as a contribution to the LORD.
2_30_13.MSG: Everyone who gets counted is to give a half-shekel (using the standard Sanctuary shekel of a fifth of an ounce to the shekel)—a half-shekel offering to God.
2_30_13.NIV: Each one who crosses over to those already counted is to give a half shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, which weighs twenty gerahs. This half shekel is an offering to the Lord.
2_30_13.NRSVCE: This is what each one who is registered shall give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs), half a shekel as an offering to the LORD.

2_30_14.NKJV: Everyone included among those who are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering to the LORD.
2_30_14.KJV: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the LORD.
2_30_14.ESV: Everyone who is numbered in the census, from twenty years old and upward, shall give the LORD's offering.
2_30_14.NASB: 'Everyone who is numbered, from twenty years old and over, shall give the contribution to the LORD.
2_30_14.NLT: All who have reached their twentieth birthday must give this sacred offering to the LORD.
2_30_14.WEB: Everyone who passes over to those who are counted, from twenty years old and upward, shall give the offering to Yahweh.
2_30_14.CSB17: Each man who is registered, twenty years old or more, must give this contribution to the LORD.
2_30_14.AMP: Everyone who is counted, from twenty years old and over, [as he joins those already counted], shall give this contribution to the LORD.
2_30_14.MSG: Everyone counted, age twenty and up, is to make the offering to God.
2_30_14.NIV: All who cross over, those twenty years old or more, are to give an offering to the Lord.
2_30_14.NRSVCE: Each one who is registered, from twenty years old and upward, shall give the LORD’s offering.

2_30_15.NKJV: The rich shall not give more and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when you give an offering to the LORD, to make atonement for yourselves.
2_30_15.KJV: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when they give an offering unto the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls. give more: Heb. multiply give less: Heb. diminish
2_30_15.ESV: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less, than the half shekel, when you give the LORD's offering to make atonement for your lives.

2_30_34.CSB17: The LORD said to Moses: “Take fragrant spices: stacte, onycha, and galbanum; the spices and pure frankincense are to be in equal measures.
2_30_34.AMP: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Take sweet and fragrant spices—stacte, onycha, and galbanum, sweet and fragrant spices with pure frankincense; there shall be an equal amount of each—
2_30_34.MSG: God spoke to Moses: "Take fragrant spices—gum resin, onycha, galbanum—and add pure frankincense. Mix the spices in equal proportions
2_30_34.NIV: IncenseThen the Lord said to Moses, “Take fragrant spices—gum resin, onycha and galbanum—and pure frankincense, all in equal amounts,
2_30_34.NRSVCE: The LORD said to Moses: Take sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum, sweet spices with pure frankincense (an equal part of each),

2_30_35.NKJV: You shall make of these an incense, a compound according to the art of the perfumer, salted, pure, and holy.
2_30_35.KJV: And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure and holy: tempered: Heb. salted
2_30_35.ESV: and make an incense blended as by the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy.
2_30_35.NASB: 'With it you shall make incense, a perfume, the work of a perfumer, salted, pure, [and] holy.
2_30_35.NLT: Using the usual techniques of the incense maker, blend the spices together and sprinkle them with salt to produce a pure and holy incense.
2_30_35.WEB: and you shall make incense of it, a perfume after the art of the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy:
2_30_35.CSB17: Prepare expertly blended incense from these; it is to be seasoned with salt, pure and holy.
2_30_35.AMP: and make incense with it, a perfume, the work of a perfumer, salted, pure and sacred.
2_30_35.MSG: to make an aromatic incense, the art of a perfumer, salted and pure—holy.
2_30_35.NIV: and make a fragrant blend of incense, the work of a perfumer. It is to be salted and pure and sacred.
2_30_35.NRSVCE: and make an incense blended as by the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy;

2_30_36.NKJV: And you shall beat some of it very fine, and put some of it before the Testimony in the tabernacle of meeting where I will meet with you. It shall be most holy to you.
2_30_36.KJV: And thou shalt beat some of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.
2_30_36.ESV: You shall beat some of it very small, and put part of it before the testimony in the tent of meeting where I shall meet with you. It shall be most holy for you.
2_30_36.NASB: 'You shall beat some of it very fine, and put part of it before the testimony in the tent of meeting where I will meet with you; it shall be most holy to you.
2_30_36.NLT: Grind some of the mixture into a very fine powder and put it in front of the Ark of the Covenant, where I will meet with you in the Tabernacle. You must treat this incense as most holy.
2_30_36.WEB: and you shall beat some of it very small, and put some of it before the testimony in the Tent of Meeting, where I will meet with you. It shall be to you most holy.
2_30_36.CSB17: Grind some of it into a fine powder and put some in front of the testimony in the tent of meeting, where I will meet with you. It must be especially holy to you.
2_30_36.AMP: You shall crush some of it [into a] very fine [powder], and put some of it before the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting, where I will meet with you; it shall be most holy to you.
2_30_36.MSG: Now crush some of it into powder and place some of it before The Testimony in the Tent of Meeting where I will meet with you; it will be for you the holiest of holy places.
2_30_36.NIV: Grind some of it to powder and place it in front of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting, where I will meet with you. It shall be most holy to you.
2_30_36.NRSVCE: and you shall beat some of it into powder, and put part of it before the covenant in the tent of meeting where I shall meet with you; it shall be for you most holy.

2_30_37.NKJV: But as for the incense which you shall make, you shall not make any for yourselves, according to its composition. It shall be to you holy for the LORD.
2_30_37.KJV: And as for the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD.
2_30_37.ESV: And the incense that you shall make according to its composition, you shall not make for yourselves. It shall be for you holy to the LORD.
2_30_37.NASB: 'The incense which you shall make, you shall not make in the same proportions for yourselves; it shall be holy to you for the LORD.
2_30_37.NLT: Never use this formula to make this incense for yourselves. It is reserved for the LORD, and you must treat it as holy.
2_30_37.WEB: The incense which you shall make, according to its composition you shall not make for yourselves: it shall be to you holy for Yahweh.
2_30_37.CSB17: As for the incense you are making, you must not make any for yourselves using its formula. It is to be regarded by you as holy — belonging to the LORD.
2_30_37.AMP: The incense which you shall make, you shall not make in the same proportions for yourselves; it shall be holy to you for the LORD.
2_30_37.MSG: When you make this incense, you are not to copy the mixture for your own use. It's holy to God; keep it that way.
2_30_37.NIV: Do not make any incense with this formula for yourselves; consider it holy to the Lord.
2_30_37.NRSVCE: When you make incense according to this composition, you shall not make it for yourselves; it shall be regarded by you as holy to the LORD.

2_30_38.NKJV: Whoever makes any like it, to smell it, he shall be cut off from his people.”

2_31_13.NKJV: “Speak also to the children of Israel, saying: ‘Surely My Sabbaths you shall keep, for it is a sign between Me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I am the LORD who sanctifies you.
2_31_13.KJV: Speak thou also unto the children of Israel, saying, Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep: for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that ye may know that I am the LORD that doth sanctify you.
2_31_13.ESV: “You are to speak to the people of Israel and say, ‘Above all you shall keep my Sabbaths, for this is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I, the LORD, sanctify you.
2_31_13.NASB: 'But as for you, speak to the sons of Israel, saying, 'You shall surely observe My sabbaths; for [this] is a sign between Me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I am the LORD who sanctifies you.
2_31_13.NLT: “Tell the people of Israel: ‘Be careful to keep my Sabbath day, for the Sabbath is a sign of the covenant between me and you from generation to generation. It is given so you may know that I am the LORD, who makes you holy.
2_31_13.WEB: “Speak also to the children of Israel, saying, ‘Most certainly you shall keep my Sabbaths: for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that you may know that I am Yahweh who sanctifies you.
2_31_13.CSB17: “Tell the Israelites: You must observe my Sabbaths, for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, so that you will know that I am the LORD who consecrates you.
2_31_13.AMP: "But as for you, say to the Israelites, 'You shall most certainly observe My Sabbaths, for it is a sign between Me and you throughout your generations, so that you may know [without any doubt] and acknowledge that I am the LORD who sanctifies you and sets you apart [for Myself].
2_31_13.MSG: "Tell the Israelites, 'Above all, keep my Sabbaths, the sign between me and you, generation after generation, to keep the knowledge alive that I am the God who makes you holy.
2_31_13.NIV: “Say to the Israelites, ‘You must observe my Sabbaths. This will be a sign between me and you for the generations to come, so you may know that I am the Lord, who makes you holy.
2_31_13.NRSVCE: You yourself are to speak to the Israelites: “You shall keep my sabbaths, for this is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, given in order that you may know that I, the LORD, sanctify you.

2_31_14.NKJV: You shall keep the Sabbath, therefore, for it is holy to you. Everyone who profanes it shall surely be put to death; for whoever does any work on it, that person shall be cut off from among his people.
2_31_14.KJV: Ye shall keep the sabbath therefore; for it is holy unto you: every one that defileth it shall surely be put to death: for whosoever doeth any work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

2_31_14.NASB: 'Therefore you are to observe the sabbath, for it is holy to you. Everyone who profanes it shall surely be put to death; for whoever does any work on it, that person shall be cut off from among his people.
2_31_14.NLT: You must keep the Sabbath day, for it is a holy day for you. Anyone who desecrates it must be put to death; anyone who works on that day will be cut off from the community.
2_31_14.WEB: You shall keep the Sabbath therefore; for it is holy to you. Everyone who profanes it shall surely be put to death; for whoever does any work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
2_31_14.CSB17: Observe the Sabbath, for it is holy to you. Whoever profanes it must be put to death. If anyone does work on it, that person must be cut off from his people.
2_31_14.AMP: Therefore, you shall keep the Sabbath, for it is holy to you. Everyone who profanes it must be put to death; for whoever does work on the Sabbath, that person (soul) shall be cut off from among his people [excluding him from the atonement made for them].
2_31_14.MSG: Keep the Sabbath; it's holy to you. Whoever profanes it will most certainly be put to death. Whoever works on it will be excommunicated from the people.
2_31_14.NIV: “‘Observe the Sabbath, because it is holy to you. Anyone who desecrates it must be put to death; whoever does any work on that day must be cut off from his people.
2_31_14.NRSVCE: You shall keep the sabbath, because it is holy for you; everyone who profanes it shall be put to death; whoever does any work on it shall be cut off from among the people.

2_31_15.NKJV: Work shall be done for six days, but the seventh is the Sabbath of rest, holy to the LORD. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day, he shall surely be put to death.
2_31_15.KJV: Six days may work be done; but in the seventh is the sabbath of rest, holy to the LORD: whosoever doeth any work in the sabbath day, he shall surely be put to death. holy: Heb. holiness
2_31_15.ESV: Six days shall work be done, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, holy to the LORD. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day shall be put to death.
2_31_15.NASB: 'For six days work may be done, but on the seventh day there is a sabbath of complete rest, holy to the LORD; whoever does any work on the sabbath day shall surely be put to death.
2_31_15.NLT: You have six days each week for your ordinary work, but the seventh day must be a Sabbath day of complete rest, a holy day dedicated to the LORD. Anyone who works on the Sabbath must be put to death.
2_31_15.WEB: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, holy to Yahweh. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day shall surely be put to death.
2_31_15.CSB17: Work may be done for six days, but on the seventh day there must be a Sabbath of complete rest, holy to the LORD. Anyone who does work on the Sabbath day must be put to death.
2_31_15.AMP: For six days work may be done, but the seventh is the Sabbath of complete rest, sacred to the LORD; whoever does work on the Sabbath day must be put to death.
2_31_15.MSG: There are six days for work but the seventh day is Sabbath, pure rest, holy to God. Anyone who works on the Sabbath will most certainly be put to death.
2_31_15.NIV: For six days, work is to be done, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of rest, holy to the Lord. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day must be put to death.
2_31_15.NRSVCE: Six days shall work be done, but the seventh day is a sabbath of solemn rest, holy to the LORD; whoever does any work on the sabbath day shall be put to death.

2_31_16.NKJV: Therefore the children of Israel shall keep the Sabbath, to observe the Sabbath throughout their generations as a perpetual covenant.

2_32_1.NKJV: Now when the people saw that Moses delayed coming down from the mountain, the people gathered together to Aaron, and said to him, “Come, make us gods that shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.”

2_32_12.ESV: Why should the Egyptians say, ‘With evil intent did he bring them out, to kill them in the mountains and to consume them from the face of the earth’? Turn from your burning anger and relent from this disaster against your people.
2_32_12.NASB: 'Why should the Egyptians speak, saying, 'With evil [intent] He brought them out to kill them in the mountains and to destroy them from the face of the earth '? Turn from Your burning anger and change Your mind about [doing] harm to Your people.
2_32_12.NLT: Why let the Egyptians say, ‘Their God rescued them with the evil intention of slaughtering them in the mountains and wiping them from the face of the earth’? Turn away from your fierce anger. Change your mind about this terrible disaster you have threatened against your people!
2_32_12.WEB: Why should the Egyptians speak, saying, ‘He brought them out for evil, to kill them in the mountains, and to consume them from the surface of the earth?’ Turn from your fierce wrath, and repent of this evil against your people.
2_32_12.CSB17: Why should the Egyptians say, ‘He brought them out with an evil intent to kill them in the mountains and eliminate them from the face of the earth’? Turn from your fierce anger and relent concerning this disaster planned for your people.
2_32_12.AMP: Why should the Egyptians say, 'With evil [intent] their God brought them out to kill them in the mountains and destroy them from the face of the earth'? Turn away from Your burning anger and change Your mind about harming Your people.
2_32_12.MSG: Why let the Egyptians say, 'He had it in for them—he brought them out so he could kill them in the mountains, wipe them right off the face of the Earth.' Stop your anger. Think twice about bringing evil against your people!
2_32_12.NIV: Why should the Egyptians say, ‘It was with evil intent that he brought them out, to kill them in the mountains and to wipe them off the face of the earth’? Turn from your fierce anger; relent and do not bring disaster on your people.

For Moses: or, And Moses said, Consecrate yourselves to day to the LORD, because every man hath been against his brother, etc Consecrate: Heb. Fill your hands

2_33_5.NKJV: For the LORD had said to Moses, “Say to the children of Israel, ‘You are a stiff-necked people. I could come up into your midst in one moment and consume you. Now therefore, take off your ornaments, that I may know what to do to you.’ ”
2_33_5.KJV: For the LORD had said unto Moses, Say unto the children of Israel, Ye are a stiffnecked people: I will come up into the midst of thee in a moment, and consume thee: therefore now put off thy ornaments from thee, that I may know what to do unto thee.

2_33_5.NASB: For the LORD had said to Moses, 'Say to the sons of Israel, 'You are an obstinate people; should I go up in your midst for one moment, I would destroy you. Now therefore, put off your ornaments from you, that I may know what I shall do with you.''

2_33_5.NIV: For the Lord had said to Moses, “Tell the Israelites, ‘You are a stiff-necked people. If I were to go with you even for a moment, I might destroy you. Now take off your ornaments and I will decide what to do with you.’”

2_33_7.NKJV: Moses took his tent and pitched it outside the camp, far from the camp, and called it the tabernacle of meeting. And it came to pass that everyone who sought the LORD went out to the tabernacle of meeting which was outside the camp.

2_33_7.ESV: Now Moses used to take the tent and pitch it outside the camp, far off from the camp, and he called it the tent of meeting. And everyone who sought the LORD would go out to the tent of meeting, which was outside the camp.

2_33_7.MSG: Moses used to take the Tent and set it up outside the camp, some distance away. He called it the Tent of Meeting. Anyone who sought God would go to the Tent of Meeting outside the camp.
2_33_7.NIV: The Tent of MeetingNow Moses used to take a tent and pitch it outside the camp some distance away, calling it the “tent of meeting.” Anyone inquiring of the Lord would go to the tent of meeting outside the camp.
2_33_7.NRSVCE: Now Moses used to take the tent and pitch it outside the camp, far off from the camp; he called it the tent of meeting. And everyone who sought the LORD would go out to the tent of meeting, which was outside the camp.

2_33_8.NKJV: So it was, whenever Moses went out to the tabernacle, that all the people rose, and each man stood at his tent door and watched Moses until he had gone into the tabernacle.
2_33_8.KJV: And it came to pass, when Moses went out unto the tabernacle, that all the people rose up, and stood every man at his tent door, and looked after Moses, until he was gone into the tabernacle.
2_33_8.ESV: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would rise up, and each would stand at his tent door, and watch Moses until he had gone into the tent.
2_33_8.NASB: And it came about, whenever Moses went out to the tent, that all the people would arise and stand, each at the entrance of his tent, and gaze after Moses until he entered the tent.
2_33_8.NLT: Whenever Moses went out to the Tent of Meeting, all the people would get up and stand in the entrances of their own tents. They would all watch Moses until he disappeared inside.
2_33_8.WEB: When Moses went out to the Tent, that all the people rose up, and stood, everyone at their tent door, and watched Moses, until he had gone into the Tent.
2_33_8.CSB17: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would stand up, each one at the door of his tent, and they would watch Moses until he entered the tent.
2_33_8.AMP: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would rise and stand, each at his tent door, and look at Moses until he entered the tent.
2_33_8.MSG: It went like this: When Moses would go to the Tent, all the people would stand at attention; each man would take his position at the entrance to his tent with his eyes on Moses until he entered the Tent;
2_33_8.NIV: And whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people rose and stood at the entrances to their tents, watching Moses until he entered the tent.
2_33_8.NRSVCE: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would rise and stand, each of them, at the entrance of their tents and watch Moses until he had gone into the tent.

2_33_9.NKJV: And it came to pass, when Moses entered the tabernacle, that the pillar of cloud descended and stood at the door of the tabernacle, and the LORD talked with Moses.

2_33_9.ESV: When Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent, and the LORD would speak with Moses.
2_33_9.NASB: Whenever Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent; and the LORD would speak with Moses.
2_33_9.NLT: As he went into the tent, the pillar of cloud would come down and hover at its entrance while the LORD spoke with Moses.

2_33_9.CSB17: When Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would come down and remain at the entrance to the tent, and the LORD would speak with Moses.
2_33_9.AMP: Whenever Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the doorway of the tent; and the LORD would speak with Moses.
2_33_9.MSG: whenever Moses entered the Tent, the Pillar of Cloud descended to the entrance to the Tent and God spoke with Moses.
2_33_9.NIV: As Moses went into the tent, the pillar of cloud would come down and stay at the entrance, while the Lord spoke with Moses.
2_33_9.NRSVCE: When Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent, and the LORD would speak with Moses.

2_33_10.NKJV: All the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the tabernacle door, and all the people rose and worshiped, each man in his tent door.
2_33_10.KJV: And all the people saw the cloudy pillar stand at the tabernacle door: and all the people rose up and worshipped, every man in his tent door.

2_33_10.CSB17: As all the people saw the pillar of cloud remaining at the entrance to the tent, they would stand up, then bow in worship, each one at the door of his tent.

2_33_10.NRSVCE: When all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tent, all the people would rise and bow down, all of them, at the entrance of their tent.

2_33_11.NKJV: So the LORD spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. And he would return to the camp, but his servant Joshua the son of Nun, a young man, did not depart from the tabernacle.

2_33_11.WEB: Yahweh spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. He turned again into the camp, but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, didn’t depart from the Tent.
2_33_11.CSB17: The LORD would speak with Moses face to face, just as a man speaks with his friend, then Moses would return to the camp. His assistant, the young man Joshua son of Nun, would not leave the inside of the tent.

2_33_11.NIV: The Lord would speak to Moses face to face, as a man speaks with his friend. Then Moses would return to the camp, but his young aide Joshua son of Nun did not leave the tent.
2_33_11.NRSVCE: Thus the LORD used to speak to Moses face to face, as one speaks to a friend. Then he would return to the camp; but his young assistant, Joshua son of Nun, would not leave the tent.

2_33_12.NKJV: Then Moses said to the LORD, “See, You say to me, ‘Bring up this people.’ But You have not let me know whom You will send with me. Yet You have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found grace in My sight.’
2_33_12.KJV: And Moses said unto the LORD, See, thou sayest unto me, Bring up this people: and thou hast not let me know whom thou wilt send with me. Yet thou hast said, I know thee by name, and thou hast also found grace in my sight.
2_33_12.ESV: Moses said to the LORD, “See, you say to me, ‘Bring up this people,’ but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found favor in my sight.’
2_33_12.NASB: Then Moses said to the LORD, 'See, You say to me, 'Bring up this people!' But You Yourself have not let me know whom You will send with me. Moreover, You have said, 'I have known you by name, and you have also found favor in My sight.'
2_33_12.NLT: One day Moses said to the LORD, “You have been telling me, ‘Take these people up to the Promised Land.’ But you haven’t told me whom you will send with me. You have told me, ‘I know you by name, and I look favorably on you.’
2_33_12.WEB: Moses said to Yahweh, “Behold, you tell me, ‘Bring up this people:’ and you haven’t let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found favor in my sight.’
2_33_12.CSB17: Moses said to the LORD, “Look, you have told me, ‘Lead this people up,’ but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. You said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found favor with me.’

2_33_12.NRSVCE: Moses said to the LORD, “See, you have said to me, ‘Bring up this people’; but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found favor in my sight.’

2_33_13.NKJV: Now therefore, I pray, if I have found grace in Your sight, show me now Your way, that I may know You and that I may find grace in Your sight. And consider that this nation is Your people.”
2_33_13.KJV: Now therefore, I pray thee, if I have found grace in thy sight, shew me now thy way, that I may know thee, that I may find grace in thy sight: and consider that this nation is thy people.
2_33_13.ESV: Now therefore, if I have found favor in your sight, please show me now your ways, that I may know you in order to find favor in your sight. Consider too that this nation is your people.”
2_33_13.NASB: 'Now therefore, I pray You, if I have found favor in Your sight, let me know Your ways that I may know You, so that I may find favor in Your sight. Consider too, that this nation is Your people.'
2_33_13.NLT: If it is true that you look favorably on me, let me know your ways so I may understand you more fully and continue to enjoy your favor. And remember that this nation is your very own people.”
2_33_13.WEB: Now therefore, if I have found favor in your sight, please show me now your way, that I may know you, so that I may find favor in your sight: and consider that this nation is your people.”
2_33_13.CSB17: Now if I have indeed found favor with you, please teach me your ways, and I will know you, so that I may find favor with you. Now consider that this nation is your people.”

And consider also, that this nation is Your people."
2_33_13.MSG: If I am so special to you, let me in on your plans. That way, I will continue being special to you. Don't forget, this is your people, your responsibility."
2_33_13.NIV: If you are pleased with me, teach me your ways so I may know you and continue to find favor with you. Remember that this nation is your people.”
2_33_13.NRSVCE: Now if I have found favor in your sight, show me your ways, so that I may know you and find favor in your sight. Consider too that this nation is your people.”

2_33_14.NKJV: And He said, “My Presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.”

2_33_16.NKJV: For how then will it be known that Your people and I have found grace in Your sight, except You go with us? So we shall be separate, Your people and I, from all the people who are upon the face of the earth.”
2_33_16.KJV: For wherein shall it be known here that I and thy people have found grace in thy sight? is it not in that thou goest with us? so shall we be separated, I and thy people, from all the people that are upon the face of the earth.

2_33_16.NASB: 'For how then can it be known that I have found favor in Your sight, I and Your people? Is it not by Your going with us, so that we, I and Your people, may be distinguished from all the [other] people who are upon the face of the earth?'
2_33_16.NLT: How will anyone know that you look favorably on me — on me and on your people — if you don’t go with us? For your presence among us sets your people and me apart from all other people on the earth.”

2_33_16.NIV: How will anyone know that you are pleased with me and with your people unless you go with us? What else will distinguish me and your people from all the other people on the face of the earth?”

2_34_10.NKJV: And He said: “Behold, I make a covenant. Before all your people I will do marvels such as have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation; and all the people among whom you are shall see the work of the LORD. For it is an awesome thing that I will do with you.
2_34_10.KJV: And he said, Behold, I make a covenant: before all thy people I will do marvels, such as have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation: and all the people among which thou art shall see the work of the LORD: for it is a terrible thing that I will do with thee.
2_34_10.ESV: And he said, “Behold, I am making a covenant. Before all your people I will do marvels, such as have not been created in all the earth or in any nation. And all the people among whom you are shall see the work of the LORD, for it is an awesome thing that I will do with you.
2_34_10.NASB: Then God said, 'Behold, I am going to make a covenant. Before all your people I will perform miracles which have not been produced in all the earth nor among any of the nations; and all the people among whom you live will see the working of the LORD, for it is a fearful thing that I am going to perform with you.
2_34_10.NLT: The LORD replied, “Listen, I am making a covenant with you in the presence of all your people. I will perform miracles that have never been performed anywhere in all the earth or in any nation. And all the people around you will see the power of the LORD — the awesome power I will display for you.
2_34_10.WEB: He said, “Behold, I make a covenant: before all your people I will do marvels, such as have not been worked in all the earth, nor in any nation; and all the people among which you are shall see the work of Yahweh; for it is an awesome thing that I do with you.
2_34_10.CSB17: And the LORD responded: “Look, I am making a covenant. I will perform wonders in the presence of all your people that have never been done in the whole earth or in any nation. All the people you live among will see the LORD’s work, for what I am doing with you is awe-inspiring.
2_34_10.AMP: Then God said, "Behold, I am going to make a covenant. Before all your people I will do wondrous works (miracles) such as have not been created or produced in all the earth nor among any of the nations; and all the people among whom you live shall see the working of the LORD, for it is a fearful and awesome thing that I am going to do with you.
2_34_10.MSG: And God said, "As of right now, I'm making a covenant with you: In full sight of your people I will work wonders that have never been created in all the Earth, in any nation. Then all the people with whom you're living will see how tremendous God's work is, the work I'll do for you.
2_34_10.NIV: Then the Lord said: “I am making a covenant with you. Before all your people I will do wonders never before done in any nation in all the world. The people you live among will see how awesome is the work that I, the Lord, will do for you.

2_34_16.NKJV: and you take of his daughters for your sons, and his daughters play the harlot with their gods and make your sons play the harlot with their gods.
2_34_16.KJV: And thou take of their daughters unto thy sons, and their daughters go a whoring after their gods, and make thy sons go a whoring after their gods.
2_34_16.ESV: and you take of their daughters for your sons, and their daughters whore after their gods and make your sons whore after their gods.
2_34_16.NASB: and you might take some of his daughters for your sons, and his daughters might play the harlot with their gods and cause your sons [also] to play the harlot with their gods.
2_34_16.NLT: Then you will accept their daughters, who sacrifice to other gods, as wives for your sons. And they will seduce your sons to commit adultery against me by worshiping other gods.
2_34_16.WEB: and you take of their daughters to your sons, and their daughters play the prostitute after their gods, and make your sons play the prostitute after their gods.
2_34_16.CSB17: Then you will take some of their daughters as brides for your sons. Their daughters will prostitute themselves with their gods and cause your sons to prostitute themselves with their gods.
2_34_16.AMP: and you might take some of his daughters for your sons, and his daughters would play the prostitute with their gods and cause your sons also to play the prostitute (commit apostasy) with their gods [that is, abandon the true God for man-made idols].
2_34_16.MSG: marry your sons to their women, women who take up with any convenient god or goddess and will get your sons to do the same thing.
2_34_16.NIV: And when you choose some of their daughters as wives for your sons and those daughters prostitute themselves to their gods, they will lead your sons to do the same.
2_34_16.NRSVCE: And you will take wives from among their daughters for your sons, and their daughters who prostitute themselves to their gods will make your sons also prostitute themselves to their gods.

2_34_17.NKJV: “You shall make no molded gods for yourselves.
2_34_17.KJV: Thou shalt make thee no molten gods.
2_34_17.ESV: “You shall not make for yourself any gods of cast metal.
2_34_17.NASB: 'You shall make for yourself no molten gods.
2_34_17.NLT: You must not make any gods of molten metal for yourselves.
2_34_17.WEB: “You shall make no cast idols for yourselves.
2_34_17.CSB17: “Do not make cast images of gods for yourselves.
2_34_17.AMP: You shall make for yourselves no molten gods.
2_34_17.NRSVCE: You shall not make cast idols.

2_34_18.NKJV: “The Feast of Unleavened Bread you shall keep. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, in the appointed time of the month of Abib; for in the month of Abib you came out from Egypt.
2_34_18.KJV: The feast of unleavened bread shalt thou keep. Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee, in the time of the month Abib: for in the month Abib thou camest out from Egypt.
2_34_18.ESV: “You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month Abib, for in the month Abib you came out from Egypt.
2_34_18.NASB: 'You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you are to eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in the month of Abib you came out of Egypt.
2_34_18.NLT: “You must celebrate the Festival of Unleavened Bread. For seven days the bread you eat must be made without yeast, just as I commanded you. Celebrate this festival annually at the appointed time in early spring, in the month of Abib, for that is the anniversary of your departure from Egypt.
2_34_18.WEB: “You shall keep the feast of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month Abib; for in the month Abib you came out of Egypt.
2_34_18.CSB17: “Observe the Festival of Unleavened Bread. You are to eat unleavened bread for seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib, as I commanded you, for you came out of Egypt in the month of Abib.
2_34_18.AMP: "You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread (Passover). For seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I have commanded you, at the appointed time in the month of Abib; for in the month of Abib you came out of Egypt.
2_34_18.MSG: "Keep the Feast of Unraised Bread. Eat only unraised bread for seven days in the month of Abib—it was in the month of Abib that you came out of Egypt.
2_34_18.NIV: “Celebrate the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days eat bread made without yeast, as I commanded you. Do this at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in that month you came out of Egypt.
2_34_18.NRSVCE: You shall keep the festival of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month of Abib; for in the month of Abib you came out from Egypt.

2_34_19.NKJV: “All that open the womb are Mine, and every male firstborn among your livestock, whether ox or sheep.
2_34_19.KJV: All that openeth the matrix is mine; and every firstling among thy cattle, whether ox or sheep, that is male. sheep: or, kid
2_34_19.ESV: All that open the womb are mine, all your male livestock, the firstborn of cow and sheep.
2_34_19.NASB: 'The first offspring from every womb belongs to Me, and all your male livestock, the first offspring from cattle and sheep.
2_34_19.NLT: “The firstborn of every animal belongs to me, including the firstborn males from your herds of cattle and your flocks of sheep and goats.
2_34_19.WEB: “All that opens the womb is mine; and all your livestock that is male, the firstborn of cow and sheep.
2_34_19.CSB17: “The firstborn male from every womb belongs to me, including all your male livestock, the firstborn of cattle or sheep.
2_34_19.AMP: "All the firstborn males among your livestock belong to Me, whether cattle or sheep.
2_34_19.MSG: "Every firstborn from the womb is mine, all the males of your herds, your firstborn oxen and sheep.
2_34_19.NIV: “The first offspring of every womb belongs to me, including all the firstborn males of your livestock, whether from herd or flock.
2_34_19.NRSVCE: All that first opens the womb is mine, all your male livestock, the firstborn of cow and sheep.

2_34_20.NKJV: But the firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb. And if you will not redeem him, then you shall break his neck. All the firstborn of your sons you shall redeem. “And none shall appear before Me empty-handed.
2_34_20.KJV: But the firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb: and if thou redeem him not, then shalt thou break his neck. All the firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem. And none shall appear before me empty. lamb: or, kid
2_34_20.ESV: The firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, or if you will not redeem it you shall break its neck. All the firstborn of your sons you shall redeem. And none shall appear before me empty-handed.
2_34_20.NASB: 'You shall redeem with a lamb the first offspring from a donkey; and if you do not redeem [it], then you shall break its neck. You shall redeem all the firstborn of your sons. None shall appear before Me empty-handed.
2_34_20.NLT: A firstborn donkey may be bought back from the LORD by presenting a lamb or young goat in its place. But if you do not buy it back, you must break its neck. However, you must buy back every firstborn son. “No one may appear before me without an offering.
2_34_20.WEB: You shall redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a lamb. If you will not redeem it, then you shall break its neck. You shall redeem all the firstborn of your sons. No one shall appear before me empty.
2_34_20.CSB17: You may redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a sheep, but if you do not redeem it, break its neck. You must redeem all the firstborn of your sons. No one is to appear before me empty-handed.
2_34_20.AMP: You shall redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a lamb; but if you do not redeem it, then you shall break its neck. You shall redeem all the firstborn of your sons. None of you are to appear before Me empty-handed.
2_34_20.MSG: "Redeem your firstborn donkey with a lamb. If you don't redeem it you must break its neck."Redeem each of your firstborn sons."No one is to show up in my presence empty-handed.
2_34_20.NIV: Redeem the firstborn donkey with a lamb, but if you do not redeem it, break its neck. Redeem all your firstborn sons.“No one is to appear before me empty-handed.
2_34_20.NRSVCE: The firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, or if you will not redeem it you shall break its neck. All the firstborn of your sons you shall redeem. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.

2_34_21.NKJV: “Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest; in plowing time and in harvest you shall rest.
2_34_21.KJV: Six days thou shalt work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest: in earing time and in harvest thou shalt rest.
2_34_21.ESV: “Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest. In plowing time and in harvest you shall rest.
2_34_21.NASB: 'You shall work six days, but on the seventh day you shall rest; [even] during plowing time and harvest you shall rest.
2_34_21.NLT: “You have six days each week for your ordinary work, but on the seventh day you must stop working, even during the seasons of plowing and harvest.
2_34_21.WEB: “Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest: in plowing time and in harvest you shall rest.
2_34_21.CSB17: “You are to labor six days but you must rest on the seventh day; you must even rest during plowing and harvesting times.
2_34_21.AMP: "You shall work for six days, but on the seventh day you shall rest; [even] in plowing time and in harvest you shall rest [on the Sabbath].
2_34_21.MSG: "Work six days and rest the seventh. Stop working even during plowing and harvesting.
2_34_21.NIV: “Six days you shall labor, but on the seventh day you shall rest; even during the plowing season and harvest you must rest.
2_34_21.NRSVCE: Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest; even in plowing time and in harvest time you shall rest.

2_34_22.NKJV: “And you shall observe the Feast of Weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the year’s end.
2_34_22.KJV: And thou shalt observe the feast of weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the year's end. year's end: Heb. revolution of the year
2_34_22.ESV: You shall observe the Feast of Weeks, the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the year's end.
2_34_22.NASB: 'You shall celebrate the Feast of Weeks, [that is], the first fruits of the wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the turn of the year.
2_34_22.NLT: “You must celebrate the Festival of Harvest with the first crop of the wheat harvest, and celebrate the Festival of the Final Harvest at the end of the harvest season.
2_34_22.WEB: “You shall observe the feast of weeks with the first fruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of harvest at the year’s end.
2_34_22.CSB17: “Observe the Festival of Weeks with the firstfruits of the wheat harvest, and the Festival of Ingathering at the turn of the agricultural year.
2_34_22.AMP: You shall observe and celebrate the Feast of Weeks (Harvest, First Fruits, or Pentecost), the first fruits of the wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering (Booths or Tabernacles) at the year's end.
2_34_22.MSG: "Keep the Feast of Weeks with the first cutting of the wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the turn of the year.
2_34_22.NIV: “Celebrate the Feast of Weeks with the firstfruits of the wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the turn of the year.
2_34_22.NRSVCE: You shall observe the festival of weeks, the first fruits of wheat harvest, and the festival of ingathering at the turn of the year.

2_34_23.NKJV: “Three times in the year all your men shall appear before the Lord, the LORD God of Israel.
2_34_23.KJV: Thrice in the year shall all your men children appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.ESV: Three times in the year shall all your males appear before the LORD God, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.NASB: 'Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.NLT: Three times each year every man in Israel must appear before the Sovereign, the LORD, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.WEB: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord Yahweh, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.CSB17: Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.AMP: Three times a year all your males shall appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.MSG: "All your men are to appear before the Master, the God of Israel, three times a year.
2_34_23.NIV: Three times a year all your men are to appear before the Sovereign Lord, the God of Israel.
2_34_23.NRSVCE: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the LORD God, the God of Israel.

2_34_24.NKJV: For I will cast out the nations before you and enlarge your borders; neither will any man covet your land when you go up to appear before the LORD your God three times in the year.
2_34_24.KJV: For I will cast out the nations before thee, and enlarge thy borders: neither shall any man desire thy land, when thou shalt go up to appear before the LORD thy God thrice in the year.
2_34_24.ESV: For I will cast out nations before you and enlarge your borders; no one shall covet your land, when you go up to appear before the LORD your God three times in the year.
2_34_24.NASB: 'For I will drive out nations before you and enlarge your borders, and no man shall covet your land when you go up three times a year to appear before the LORD your God.
2_34_24.NLT: I will drive out the other nations ahead of you and expand your territory, so no one will covet and conquer your land while you appear before the LORD your God three times each year.
2_34_24.WEB: For I will drive out nations before you and enlarge your borders; neither shall any man desire your land when you go up to appear before Yahweh, your God, three times in the year.
2_34_24.CSB17: For I will drive out nations before you and enlarge your territory. No one will covet your land when you go up three times a year to appear before the LORD your God.
2_34_24.AMP: For I will drive out and dispossess nations before you and enlarge your borders; nor shall any man covet (actively seek for himself) your land when you go up to appear before the LORD your God three times a year.
2_34_24.MSG: You won't have to worry about your land when you appear before your God three times each year, for I will drive out the nations before you and give you plenty of land. Nobody's going to be hanging around plotting ways to get it from you.
2_34_24.NIV: I will drive out nations before you and enlarge your territory, and no one will covet your land when you go up three times each year to appear before the Lord your God.
2_34_24.NRSVCE: For I will cast out nations before you, and enlarge your borders; no one shall covet your land when you go up to appear before the LORD your God three times in the year.

2_34_25.NKJV: “You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leaven, nor shall the sacrifice of the Feast of the Passover be left until morning.
2_34_25.KJV: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven; neither shall the sacrifice of the feast of the passover be left unto the morning.
2_34_25.ESV: “You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened, or let the sacrifice of the Feast of the Passover remain until the morning.
2_34_25.NASB: 'You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leavened bread, nor is the sacrifice of the Feast of the Passover to be left over until morning.
2_34_25.NLT: “You must not offer the blood of my sacrificial offerings together with any baked goods containing yeast. And none of the meat of the Passover sacrifice may be kept over until the next morning.
2_34_25.WEB: “You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread. The sacrifice of the feast of the Passover shall not be left to the morning.
2_34_25.CSB17: “Do not present the blood for my sacrifice with anything leavened. The sacrifice of the Passover Festival must not remain until morning.
2_34_25.AMP: "You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leavened bread, nor shall the sacrifice of the Feast of the Passover (Unleavened Bread) be left over until morning.
2_34_25.MSG: "Don't mix the blood of my sacrifices with anything fermented."Don't leave leftovers from the Passover Feast until morning.
2_34_25.NIV: “Do not offer the blood of a sacrifice to me along with anything containing yeast, and do not let any of the sacrifice from the Passover Feast remain until morning.
2_34_25.NRSVCE: You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven, and the sacrifice of the festival of the passover shall not be left until the morning.

2_34_26.NKJV: “The first of the firstfruits of your land you shall bring to the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
2_34_26.KJV: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring unto the house of the LORD thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.
2_34_26.ESV: The best of the firstfruits of your ground you shall bring to the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.”
2_34_26.NASB: 'You shall bring the very first of the first fruits of your soil into the house of the LORD your God. 'You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.'
2_34_26.NLT: “As you harvest your crops, bring the very best of the first harvest to the house of the LORD your God. “You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
2_34_26.WEB: “You shall bring the first of the first fruits of your ground to the house of Yahweh your God. “You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
2_34_26.CSB17: “Bring the best firstfruits of your land to the house of the LORD your God. “You must not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
2_34_26.AMP: "You shall bring the very first of the first fruits of your ground to the house of the LORD your God. "You shall not boil a young goat in his mother's milk [as some pagans do]."
2_34_26.MSG: "Bring the finest of the firstfruits of your produce to the house of your God."Don't boil a kid in its mother's milk."
2_34_26.NIV: “Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the Lord your God.“Do not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
2_34_26.NRSVCE: The best of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring to the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother’s milk.

2_34_27.NKJV: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Write these words, for according to the tenor of these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.”
2_34_27.KJV: And the LORD said unto Moses, Write thou these words: for after the tenor of these words I have made a covenant with thee and with Israel.
2_34_27.ESV: And the LORD said to Moses, “Write these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.”
2_34_27.NASB: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Write down these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.'
2_34_27.NLT: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Write down all these instructions, for they represent the terms of the covenant I am making with you and with Israel.”
2_34_27.WEB: Yahweh said to Moses, “Write you these words: for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.”
2_34_27.CSB17: The LORD also said to Moses, “Write down these words, for I have made a covenant with you and with Israel based on these words.”
2_34_27.AMP: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Write these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel."
2_34_27.MSG: God said to Moses: "Now write down these words, for by these words I've made a covenant with you and Israel."
2_34_27.NIV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Write down these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.”
2_34_27.NRSVCE: The LORD said to Moses: Write these words; in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.

2_34_28.NKJV: So he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread nor drank water. And He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments.
2_34_28.KJV: And he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did neither eat bread, nor drink water. And he wrote upon the tables the words of the covenant, the ten commandments. commandments: Heb. words
2_34_28.ESV: So he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights. He neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments.
2_34_28.NASB: So he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did not eat bread or drink water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments.
2_34_28.NLT: Moses remained there on the mountain with the LORD forty days and forty nights. In all that time he ate no bread and drank no water. And the LORD wrote the terms of the covenant — the Ten Commandments — on the stone tablets.
2_34_28.WEB: He was there with Yahweh forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread, nor drank water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.
2_34_28.CSB17: Moses was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did not eat food or drink water. He wrote the Ten Commandments, the words of the covenant, on the tablets.
2_34_28.AMP: Moses was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he ate no bread and drank no water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments.
2_34_28.MSG: Moses was there with God forty days and forty nights. He didn't eat any food; he didn't drink any water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Words.
2_34_28.NIV: Moses was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights without eating bread or drinking water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant—the Ten Commandments.
2_34_28.NRSVCE: He was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.

2_34_29.NKJV: Now it was so, when Moses came down from Mount Sinai (and the two tablets of the Testimony were in Moses’ hand when he came down from the mountain), that Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone while he talked with Him.
2_34_29.KJV: And it came to pass, when Moses came down from mount Sinai with the two tables of testimony in Moses' hand, when he came down from the mount, that Moses wist not that the skin of his face shone while he talked with him.
2_34_29.ESV: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai, with the two tablets of the testimony in his hand as he came down from the mountain, Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone because he had been talking with God.
2_34_29.NASB: It came about when Moses was coming down from Mount Sinai (and the two tablets of the testimony [were] in Moses' hand as he was coming down from the mountain), that Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone because of his speaking with Him.
2_34_29.NLT: When Moses came down Mount Sinai carrying the two stone tablets inscribed with the terms of the covenant, he wasn’t aware that his face had become radiant because he had spoken to the LORD.
2_34_29.WEB: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the testimony in Moses’ hand, when he came down from the mountain, Moses didn’t know that the skin of his face shone by reason of his speaking with him.
2_34_29.CSB17: As Moses descended from Mount Sinai — with the two tablets of the testimony in his hands as he descended the mountain — he did not realize that the skin of his face shone as a result of his speaking with the LORD.
2_34_29.AMP: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hand, he did not know that the skin of his face was shining [with a unique radiance] because he had been speaking with God.
2_34_29.MSG: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai carrying the two Tablets of The Testimony, he didn't know that the skin of his face glowed because he had been speaking with God.
2_34_29.NIV: The Radiant Face of MosesWhen Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hands, he was not aware that his face was radiant because he had spoken with the Lord.
2_34_29.NRSVCE: Moses came down from Mount Sinai. As he came down from the mountain with the two tablets of the covenant in his hand, Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone because he had been talking with God.

2_34_30.NKJV: So when Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to come near him.
2_34_30.KJV: And when Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come nigh him.
2_34_30.ESV: Aaron and all the people of Israel saw Moses, and behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to come near him.
2_34_30.NASB: So when Aaron and all the sons of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to come near him.
2_34_30.NLT: So when Aaron and the people of Israel saw the radiance of Moses’ face, they were afraid to come near him.
2_34_30.WEB: When Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come near him.
2_34_30.CSB17: When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, the skin of his face shone! They were afraid to come near him.
2_34_30.AMP: When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to approach him.
2_34_30.MSG: Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, saw his radiant face, and held back, afraid to get close to him.
2_34_30.NIV: When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, his face was radiant, and they were afraid to come near him.
2_34_30.NRSVCE: When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, the skin of his face was shining, and they were afraid to come near him.

2_34_31.NKJV: Then Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned to him; and Moses talked with them.
2_34_31.KJV: And Moses called unto them; and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned unto him: and Moses talked with them.
2_34_31.ESV: But Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him, and Moses talked with them.
2_34_31.NASB: Then Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the rulers in the congregation returned to him; and Moses spoke to them.
2_34_31.NLT: But Moses called out to them and asked Aaron and all the leaders of the community to come over, and he talked with them.
2_34_31.WEB: Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned to him; and Moses spoke to them.
2_34_31.CSB17: But Moses called out to them, so Aaron and all the leaders of the community returned to him, and Moses spoke to them.
2_34_31.AMP: But Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him; and he spoke to them.
2_34_31.MSG: Moses called out to them. Aaron and the leaders in the community came back and Moses talked with them.
2_34_31.NIV: But Moses called to them; so Aaron and all the leaders of the community came back to him, and he spoke to them.
2_34_31.NRSVCE: But Moses called to them; and Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him, and Moses spoke with them.

2_34_32.NKJV: Afterward all the children of Israel came near, and he gave them as commandments all that the LORD had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.
2_34_32.KJV: And afterward all the children of Israel came nigh: and he gave them in commandment all that the LORD had spoken with him in mount Sinai.
2_34_32.ESV: Afterward all the people of Israel came near, and he commanded them all that the LORD had spoken with him in Mount Sinai.
2_34_32.NASB: Afterward all the sons of Israel came near, and he commanded them [to do] everything that the LORD had spoken to him on Mount Sinai.
2_34_32.NLT: Then all the people of Israel approached him, and Moses gave them all the instructions the LORD had given him on Mount Sinai.
2_34_32.WEB: Afterward all the children of Israel came near, and he gave them all the commandments that Yahweh had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.

2_35_22.NKJV: They came, both men and women, as many as had a willing heart, and brought earrings and nose rings, rings and necklaces, all jewelry of gold, that is, every man who made an offering of gold to the LORD.
