2_35_35.NASB: 'He has filled them with skill to perform every work of an engraver and of a designer and of an embroiderer, in blue and in purple [and] in scarlet [material], and in fine linen, and of a weaver, as performers of every work and makers of designs.
2_35_35.NLT: The LORD has given them special skills as engravers, designers, embroiderers in blue, purple, and scarlet thread on fine linen cloth, and weavers. They excel as craftsmen and as designers.
2_35_35.WEB: He has filled them with wisdom of heart, to work all kinds of workmanship, of the engraver, of the skillful workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen, and of the weaver, even of those who do any workmanship, and of those who make skillful works.
2_35_35.CSB17: He has filled them with skill to do all the work of a gem cutter; a designer; an embroiderer in blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen; and a weaver. They can do every kind of craft and design artistic designs.
2_35_35.AMP: He has filled them with skill to do the work of an engraver, of a designer, and of an embroiderer, in blue, purple, and scarlet fabric, and in fine linen, and of a weaver; makers of every work and embroiderers of [excellent] designs.
2_35_35.MSG: He's gifted them with the know-how needed for carving, designing, weaving, and embroidering in blue, purple, and scarlet fabrics, and in fine linen. They can make anything and design anything.
2_35_35.NIV: He has filled them with skill to do all kinds of work as craftsmen, designers, embroiderers in blue, purple and scarlet yarn and fine linen, and weavers—all of them master craftsmen and designers.
2_35_35.NRSVCE: He has filled them with skill to do every kind of work done by an artisan or by a designer or by an embroiderer in blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and in fine linen, or by a weaver — by any sort of artisan or skilled designer.

2_36_1.NKJV: “And Bezalel and Aholiab, and every gifted artisan in whom the LORD has put wisdom and understanding, to know how to do all manner of work for the service of the sanctuary, shall do according to all that the LORD has commanded.”
2_36_1.KJV: Then wrought Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whom the LORD put wisdom and understanding to know how to work all manner of work for the service of the sanctuary, according to all that the LORD had commanded.
2_36_1.ESV: “Bezalel and Oholiab and every craftsman in whom the LORD has put skill and intelligence to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that the LORD has commanded.”
2_36_1.NASB: 'Now Bezalel and Oholiab, and every skillful person in whom the LORD has put skill and understanding to know how to perform all the work in the construction of the sanctuary, shall perform in accordance with all that the LORD has commanded.'
2_36_1.NLT: “The LORD has gifted Bezalel, Oholiab, and the other skilled craftsmen with wisdom and ability to perform any task involved in building the sanctuary. Let them construct and furnish the Tabernacle, just as the LORD has commanded.”
2_36_1.WEB: “Bezalel and Oholiab shall work with every wise-hearted man, in whom Yahweh has put wisdom and understanding to know how to do all the work for the service of the sanctuary, according to all that Yahweh has commanded.”
2_36_1.CSB17: Bezalel, Oholiab, and all the skilled people are to work based on everything the LORD has commanded. The LORD has given them wisdom and understanding to know how to do all the work of constructing the sanctuary.”
2_36_1.AMP: "Bezalel and Oholiab, and every skilled person in whom the LORD has put ability and understanding to know how to do all the work in the construction of the sanctuary, shall work according to all that the LORD has commanded."
2_36_1.MSG: "Bezalel and Oholiab, along with everyone whom God has given the skill and know-how for making everything involved in the worship of the Sanctuary as commanded by God, are to start to work."
2_36_1.NIV: 36 So Bezalel, Oholiab and every skilled person to whom the Lord has given skill and ability to know how to carry out all the work of constructing the sanctuary are to do the work just as the Lord has commanded.”
2_36_1.NRSVCE: Bezalel and Oholiab and every skillful one to whom the LORD has given skill and understanding to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that the LORD has commanded.

2_36_2.NKJV: Then Moses called Bezalel and Aholiab, and every gifted artisan in whose heart the LORD had put wisdom, everyone whose heart was stirred, to come and do the work.
2_36_2.KJV: And Moses called Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whose heart the LORD had put wisdom, even every one whose heart stirred him up to come unto the work to do it:
2_36_2.ESV: And Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every craftsman in whose mind the LORD had put skill, everyone whose heart stirred him up to come to do the work.
2_36_2.NASB: Then Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every skillful person in whom the LORD had put skill, everyone whose heart stirred him, to come to the work to perform it.
2_36_2.NLT: So Moses summoned Bezalel and Oholiab and all the others who were specially gifted by the LORD and were eager to get to work.
2_36_2.WEB: Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab, and every wise-hearted man, in whose heart Yahweh had put wisdom, even everyone whose heart stirred him up to come to the work to do it:
2_36_2.CSB17: So Moses summoned Bezalel, Oholiab, and every skilled person in whose heart the LORD had placed wisdom, all whose hearts moved them, to come to the work and do it.
2_36_2.AMP: So Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every skilled person in whom the LORD had put ability, everyone whose heart stirred him, to come to do the work.
2_36_2.MSG: Moses summoned Bezalel and Oholiab along with all whom God had gifted with the ability to work skillfully with their hands. The men were eager to get started and engage in the work.
2_36_2.NIV: Then Moses summoned Bezalel and Oholiab and every skilled person to whom the Lord had given ability and who was willing to come and do the work.
2_36_2.NRSVCE: Moses then called Bezalel and Oholiab and every skillful one to whom the LORD had given skill, everyone whose heart was stirred to come to do the work;

2_36_3.NKJV: And they received from Moses all the offering which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of making the sanctuary. So they continued bringing to him freewill offerings every morning.
2_36_3.KJV: And they received of Moses all the offering, which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, to make it withal. And they brought yet unto him free offerings every morning.
2_36_3.ESV: And they received from Moses all the contribution that the people of Israel had brought for doing the work on the sanctuary. They still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning,
2_36_3.NASB: They received from Moses all the contributions which the sons of Israel had brought to perform the work in the construction of the sanctuary. And they still [continued] bringing to him freewill offerings every morning.
2_36_3.NLT: Moses gave them the materials donated by the people of Israel as sacred offerings for the completion of the sanctuary. But the people continued to bring additional gifts each morning.
2_36_3.WEB: and they received from Moses all the offering which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, with which to make it. They brought yet to him freewill offerings every morning.
2_36_3.CSB17: They took from Moses’s presence all the contributions that the Israelites had brought for the task of making the sanctuary. Meanwhile, the people continued to bring freewill offerings morning after morning.
2_36_3.AMP: They received from Moses all the offerings which the Israelites had brought for the construction of the sanctuary, to prepare it for service. And they continued to bring him freewill (voluntary) offerings every morning.
2_36_3.MSG: They took from Moses all the offerings that the Israelites had brought for the work of constructing the Sanctuary. The people kept on bringing in their freewill offerings, morning after morning.
2_36_3.NIV: They received from Moses all the offerings the Israelites had brought to carry out the work of constructing the sanctuary. And the people continued to bring freewill offerings morning after morning.
2_36_3.NRSVCE: and they received from Moses all the freewill offerings that the Israelites had brought for doing the work on the sanctuary. They still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning,

2_36_4.NKJV: Then all the craftsmen who were doing all the work of the sanctuary came, each from the work he was doing,
2_36_4.KJV: And all the wise men, that wrought all the work of the sanctuary, came every man from his work which they made;
2_36_4.ESV: so that all the craftsmen who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came, each from the task that he was doing,
2_36_4.NASB: And all the skillful men who were performing all the work of the sanctuary came, each from the work which he was performing,
2_36_4.NLT: Finally the craftsmen who were working on the sanctuary left their work.
2_36_4.WEB: All the wise men, who performed all the work of the sanctuary, each came from his work which they did.
2_36_4.CSB17: Then all the artisans who were doing all the work for the sanctuary came one by one from the work they were doing
2_36_4.AMP: And all the skilled men who were doing all the work on the sanctuary came, each one from the work which he was doing,
2_36_4.MSG: All the artisans who were at work making everything involved in constructing the Sanctuary came, one after another,
2_36_4.NIV: So all the skilled craftsmen who were doing all the work on the sanctuary left their work
2_36_4.NRSVCE: so that all the artisans who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came, each from the task being performed,

2_36_5.NKJV: and they spoke to Moses, saying, “The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work which the LORD commanded us to do.”
2_36_5.KJV: And they spake unto Moses, saying, The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work, which the LORD commanded to make.
2_36_5.ESV: and said to Moses, “The people bring much more than enough for doing the work that the LORD has commanded us to do.”
2_36_5.NASB: and they said to Moses, 'The people are bringing much more than enough for the construction work which the LORD commanded [us] to perform.'
2_36_5.NLT: They went to Moses and reported, “The people have given more than enough materials to complete the job the LORD has commanded us to do!”
2_36_5.WEB: They spoke to Moses, saying, “The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work which Yahweh commanded to make.”
2_36_5.CSB17: and said to Moses, “The people are bringing more than is needed for the construction of the work the LORD commanded to be done.”
2_36_5.AMP: and they said to Moses, "The people are bringing much more than enough for the construction work which the LORD commanded us to do."
2_36_5.MSG: to Moses, saying, "The people are bringing more than enough for doing this work that God has commanded us to do!"
2_36_5.NIV: and said to Moses, “The people are bringing more than enough for doing the work the Lord commanded to be done.”
2_36_5.NRSVCE: and said to Moses, “The people are bringing much more than enough for doing the work that the LORD has commanded us to do.”

2_36_6.NKJV: So Moses gave a commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, “Let neither man nor woman do any more work for the offering of the sanctuary.” And the people were restrained from bringing,
2_36_6.KJV: And Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, Let neither man nor woman make any more work for the offering of the sanctuary. So the people were restrained from bringing.
2_36_6.ESV: So Moses gave command, and word was proclaimed throughout the camp, “Let no man or woman do anything more for the contribution for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing,
2_36_6.NASB: So Moses issued a command, and a proclamation was circulated throughout the camp, saying, 'Let no man or woman any longer perform work for the contributions of the sanctuary.' Thus the people were restrained from bringing [any more].
2_36_6.NLT: So Moses gave the command, and this message was sent throughout the camp: “Men and women, don’t prepare any more gifts for the sanctuary. We have enough!” So the people stopped bringing their sacred offerings.
2_36_6.WEB: Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, “Let neither man nor woman make anything else for the offering for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing.
2_36_6.CSB17: After Moses gave an order, they sent a proclamation throughout the camp: “Let no man or woman make anything else as an offering for the sanctuary.” So the people stopped.
2_36_6.AMP: So Moses issued a command, and it was proclaimed throughout the camp, "Let neither man nor woman do any more work for the sanctuary offering." So the people were restrained from bringing anything more;
2_36_6.MSG: So Moses sent out orders through the camp: "Men! Women! No more offerings for the building of the Sanctuary!"The people were ordered to stop bringing offerings!
2_36_6.NIV: Then Moses gave an order and they sent this word throughout the camp: “No man or woman is to make anything else as an offering for the sanctuary.” And so the people were restrained from bringing more,
2_36_6.NRSVCE: So Moses gave command, and word was proclaimed throughout the camp: “No man or woman is to make anything else as an offering for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing;

2_36_7.NKJV: for the material they had was sufficient for all the work to be done—indeed too much.
2_36_7.KJV: For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much.
2_36_7.ESV: for the material they had was sufficient to do all the work, and more.
2_36_7.NASB: For the material they had was sufficient and more than enough for all the work, to perform it.
2_36_7.NLT: Their contributions were more than enough to complete the whole project.
2_36_7.WEB: For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much.
2_36_7.CSB17: The materials were sufficient for them to do all the work. There was more than enough.
2_36_7.AMP: for the material they had was sufficient and more than enough to do all the work.
2_36_7.MSG: There was plenty of material for all the work to be done. Enough and more than enough.
2_36_7.NIV: because what they already had was more than enough to do all the work.
2_36_7.NRSVCE: for what they had already brought was more than enough to do all the work.

2_36_8.NKJV: Then all the gifted artisans among them who worked on the tabernacle made ten curtains woven of fine linen, and of blue, purple, and scarlet thread; with artistic designs of cherubim they made them.
2_36_8.KJV: And every wise hearted man among them that wrought the work of the tabernacle made ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet : with cherubims of cunning work made he them.
2_36_8.ESV: And all the craftsmen among the workmen made the tabernacle with ten curtains. They were made of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns, with cherubim skillfully worked.
2_36_8.NASB: All the skillful men among those who were performing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet [material], with cherubim, the work of a skillful workman, Bezalel made them.
2_36_8.NLT: The skilled craftsmen made ten curtains of finely woven linen for the Tabernacle. Then Bezalel decorated the curtains with blue, purple, and scarlet thread and with skillfully embroidered cherubim.
2_36_8.WEB: All the wise-hearted men among those who did the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twined linen, blue, purple, and scarlet, with cherubim, the work of the skillful workman, they made them.
2_36_8.CSB17: All the skilled artisans among those doing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains. Bezalel made them of finely spun linen, as well as blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, with a design of cherubim worked into them.
2_36_8.AMP: All the skilled men among them who were doing the work on the tabernacle made ten curtains of fine twisted linen and blue, purple, and scarlet fabric, with cherubim [worked into them], the work of an embroiderer, Bezalel made them.
2_36_8.MSG: Then all the skilled artisans on The Dwelling made ten tapestries of fine twisted linen and blue, purple, and scarlet fabric with an angel-cherubim design worked into the material.
2_36_8.NIV: The TabernacleAll the skilled men among the workmen made the tabernacle with ten curtains of finely twisted linen and blue, purple and scarlet yarn, with cherubim worked into them by a skilled craftsman.
2_36_8.NRSVCE: All those with skill among the workers made the tabernacle with ten curtains; they were made of fine twisted linen, and blue, purple, and crimson yarns, with cherubim skillfully worked into them.

2_36_9.NRSVCE: The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains were of the same size.

2_36_17.WEB: He made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that was outermost in the coupling, and he made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which was outermost in the second coupling.
2_36_17.CSB17: He made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set and fifty loops on the edge of the corresponding curtain in the second set.
2_36_17.AMP: He made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set, and he made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the second set.
2_36_17.MSG: by making fifty loops along the edge of the end panel and fifty loops along the edge of the joining panel,
2_36_17.NIV: Then they made fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in one set and also along the edge of the end curtain in the other set.
2_36_17.NRSVCE: He made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the other connecting curtain.

2_36_18.NKJV: He also made fifty bronze clasps to couple the tent together, that it might be one.
2_36_18.KJV: And he made fifty taches of brass to couple the tent together, that it might be one.
2_36_18.ESV: And he made fifty clasps of bronze to couple the tent together that it might be a single whole.
2_36_18.NASB: He made fifty clasps of bronze to join the tent together so that it would be a unit.
2_36_18.NLT: He also made fifty bronze clasps to fasten the long curtains together. In this way, the tent covering was made of one continuous piece.
2_36_18.WEB: He made fifty clasps of brass to couple the tent together, that it might be a unit.
2_36_18.CSB17: He made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together as a single unit.
2_36_18.AMP: He made fifty bronze hooks to join the tent together into a unit.
2_36_18.MSG: then making fifty clasps of bronze, connecting the clasps to the loops, bringing the tent together.
2_36_18.NIV: They made fifty bronze clasps to fasten the tent together as a unit.
2_36_18.NRSVCE: He made fifty clasps of bronze to join the tent together so that it might be one whole.

2_36_19.NKJV: Then he made a covering for the tent of ram skins dyed red, and a covering of badger skins above that.
2_36_19.KJV: And he made a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red, and a covering of badgers' skins above that.
2_36_19.ESV: And he made for the tent a covering of tanned rams' skins and goatskins.
2_36_19.NASB: He made a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red, and a covering of porpoise skins above.
2_36_19.NLT: He completed the tent covering with a layer of tanned ram skins and a layer of fine goatskin leather.
2_36_19.WEB: He made a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering of sea cow hides above.
2_36_19.CSB17: He also made a covering for the tent from ram skins dyed red and a covering of fine leather on top of it.
2_36_19.AMP: He made a [third] covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red, and above it a [fourth] covering of porpoise skins.
2_36_19.MSG: They finished it off by covering the tapestries with tanned rams' skins dyed red, and covered that with dolphin skins.
2_36_19.NIV: Then they made for the tent a covering of ram skins dyed red, and over that a covering of hides of sea cows.
2_36_19.NRSVCE: And he made for the tent a covering of tanned rams’ skins and an outer covering of fine leather.

2_36_20.NKJV: For the tabernacle he made boards of acacia wood, standing upright.
2_36_20.KJV: And he made boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood, standing up.
2_36_20.ESV: Then he made the upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.
2_36_20.NASB: Then he made the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing upright.
2_36_20.NLT: For the framework of the Tabernacle, Bezalel constructed frames of acacia wood.
2_36_20.WEB: He made the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing up.
2_36_20.CSB17: He made upright supports of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
2_36_20.AMP: Bezalel made boards of acacia wood for the upright framework of the tabernacle.
2_36_20.MSG: They framed The Dwelling with vertical planks of acacia wood,
2_36_20.NIV: They made upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
2_36_20.NRSVCE: Then he made the upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.

2_36_21.NKJV: The length of each board was ten cubits, and the width of each board a cubit and a half.
2_36_21.KJV: The length of a board was ten cubits, and the breadth of a board one cubit and a half.
2_36_21.ESV: Ten cubits was the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the breadth of each frame.
2_36_21.NASB: Ten cubits [was] the length of each board and one and a half cubits the width of each board.
2_36_21.NLT: Each frame was 15 feet high and 27 inches wide,
2_36_21.WEB: Ten cubits was the length of a board, and a cubit and a half the width of each board.
2_36_21.CSB17: Each support was fifteen feet long and twenty-seven inches wide.
2_36_21.AMP: Each board was ten cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.
2_36_21.MSG: each section of frame fifteen feet long and two and a quarter feet wide,
2_36_21.NIV: Each frame was ten cubits long and a cubit and a half wide,
2_36_21.NRSVCE: Ten cubits was the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the width of each frame.

2_36_22.NKJV: Each board had two tenons for binding one to another. Thus he made for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_36_22.KJV: One board had two tenons, equally distant one from another: thus did he make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_36_22.ESV: Each frame had two tenons for fitting together. He did this for all the frames of the tabernacle.
2_36_22.NASB: [There were] two tenons for each board, fitted to one another; thus he did for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_36_22.NLT: with two pegs under each frame. All the frames were identical.
2_36_22.WEB: Each board had two tenons, joined to one another. He made all the boards of the tabernacle this way.
2_36_22.CSB17: Each support had two tenons for joining one to another. He did the same for all the supports of the tabernacle.
2_36_22.AMP: Each board had two tenons (dovetails), fitted to one another; he did this for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_36_22.MSG: with two pegs for securing them. They made all the frames identical:
2_36_22.NIV: with two projections set parallel to each other. They made all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.
2_36_22.NRSVCE: Each frame had two pegs for fitting together; he did this for all the frames of the tabernacle.

2_36_23.NKJV: And he made boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards for the south side.
2_36_23.KJV: And he made boards for the tabernacle; twenty boards for the south side southward:
2_36_23.ESV: The frames for the tabernacle he made thus: twenty frames for the south side.
2_36_23.NASB: He made the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side;
2_36_23.NLT: He made twenty of these frames to support the curtains on the south side of the Tabernacle.
2_36_23.WEB: He made the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side southward.
2_36_23.CSB17: He made supports for the tabernacle as follows: twenty for the south side,
2_36_23.AMP: And [this is how] he made the boards [for frames] for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side;
2_36_23.MSG: twenty frames for the south side,
2_36_23.NIV: They made twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle
2_36_23.NRSVCE: The frames for the tabernacle he made in this way: twenty frames for the south side;

2_36_24.NKJV: Forty sockets of silver he made to go under the twenty boards: two sockets under each of the boards for its two tenons.
2_36_24.KJV: And forty sockets of silver he made under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.
2_36_24.ESV: And he made forty bases of silver under the twenty frames, two bases under one frame for its two tenons, and two bases under the next frame for its two tenons.
2_36_24.NASB: and he made forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for its two tenons and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.
2_36_24.NLT: He also made forty silver bases — two bases under each frame, with the pegs fitting securely into the bases.
2_36_24.WEB: He made forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for its two tenons, and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.
2_36_24.CSB17: and he made forty silver bases to put under the twenty supports, two bases under the first support for its two tenons, and two bases under each of the following supports for their two tenons;
2_36_24.AMP: and he made under the twenty boards forty silver sockets; two sockets under one board for its two tenons (dovetails), and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.
2_36_24.MSG: with forty silver sockets to receive the two tenons from each of the twenty frames;
2_36_24.NIV: and made forty silver bases to go under them—two bases for each frame, one under each projection.
2_36_24.NRSVCE: and he made forty bases of silver under the twenty frames, two bases under the first frame for its two pegs, and two bases under the next frame for its two pegs.

2_36_25.NKJV: And for the other side of the tabernacle, the north side, he made twenty boards
2_36_25.KJV: And for the other side of the tabernacle, which is toward the north corner, he made twenty boards,
2_36_25.ESV: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty frames
2_36_25.NASB: Then for the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty boards,
2_36_25.NLT: For the north side of the Tabernacle, he made another twenty frames,
2_36_25.WEB: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty boards,
2_36_25.CSB17: for the second side of the tabernacle, the north side, he made twenty supports,
2_36_25.AMP: For the other side of the tabernacle, the north side, he made twenty boards,
2_36_25.MSG: they repeated that construction on the north side of The Dwelling.
2_36_25.NIV: For the other side, the north side of the tabernacle, they made twenty frames
2_36_25.NRSVCE: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty frames

2_36_26.NKJV: and their forty sockets of silver: two sockets under each of the boards.
2_36_26.KJV: And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
2_36_26.ESV: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under one frame and two bases under the next frame.
2_36_26.NASB: and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
2_36_26.NLT: with their forty silver bases, two bases under each frame.
2_36_26.WEB: and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
2_36_26.CSB17: with their forty silver bases, two bases under the first support and two bases under each of the following ones;
2_36_26.AMP: and their forty silver sockets; two sockets under [the end of] each board.
2_36_26.NIV: and forty silver bases—two under each frame.
2_36_26.NRSVCE: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under the first frame and two bases under the next frame.

2_36_27.NKJV: For the west side of the tabernacle he made six boards.
2_36_27.KJV: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward he made six boards.
2_36_27.ESV: For the rear of the tabernacle westward he made six frames.
2_36_27.NASB: For the rear of the tabernacle, to the west, he made six boards.
2_36_27.NLT: He made six frames for the rear — the west side of the Tabernacle —
2_36_27.WEB: For the far part of the tabernacle westward he made six boards.
2_36_27.CSB17: and for the back of the tabernacle, on the west side, he made six supports.
2_36_27.AMP: And for the rear of the tabernacle, to the west, he made six [frame] boards.
2_36_27.MSG: For the rear of The Dwelling facing west, they made six frames,
2_36_27.NIV: They made six frames for the far end, that is, the west end of the tabernacle,
2_36_27.NRSVCE: For the rear of the tabernacle westward he made six frames.

2_36_28.NKJV: He also made two boards for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
2_36_28.KJV: And two boards made he for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.
2_36_28.ESV: He made two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear.
2_36_28.NASB: He made two boards for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear.
2_36_28.NLT: along with two additional frames to reinforce the rear corners of the Tabernacle.
2_36_28.WEB: He made two boards for the corners of the tabernacle in the far part.
2_36_28.CSB17: He also made two additional supports for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
2_36_28.AMP: And he made two boards for each corner of the tabernacle in the rear.
2_36_28.MSG: with two additional frames for the rear corners.
2_36_28.NIV: and two frames were made for the corners of the tabernacle at the far end.
2_36_28.NRSVCE: He made two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear.

2_36_29.NKJV: And they were coupled at the bottom and coupled together at the top by one ring. Thus he made both of them for the two corners.
2_36_29.KJV: And they were coupled beneath, and coupled together at the head thereof, to one ring: thus he did to both of them in both the corners. coupled: Heb. twinned
2_36_29.ESV: And they were separate beneath but joined at the top, at the first ring. He made two of them this way for the two corners.
2_36_29.NASB: They were double beneath, and together they were complete to its top to the first ring; thus he did with both of them for the two corners.
2_36_29.NLT: These corner frames were matched at the bottom and firmly attached at the top with a single ring, forming a single corner unit. Both of these corner units were made the same way.
2_36_29.WEB: They were double beneath, and in the same way they were all the way to its top to one ring. He did this to both of them in the two corners.
2_36_29.CSB17: They were paired at the bottom and joined together at the top in a single ring. This is what he did with both of them for the two corners.
2_36_29.AMP: They were separate below, but linked together at the top with one ring; thus he made both of them in both corners.
2_36_29.MSG: Both of the two corner frames were double in thickness from top to bottom and fit into a single ring
2_36_29.NIV: At these two corners the frames were double from the bottom all the way to the top and fitted into a single ring; both were made alike.
2_36_29.NRSVCE: They were separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring; he made two of them in this way, for the two corners.

2_36_30.NKJV: So there were eight boards and their sockets—sixteen sockets of silver—two sockets under each of the boards.
2_36_30.KJV: And there were eight boards; and their sockets were sixteen sockets of silver, under every board two sockets . under: Heb. two sockets, two sockets under one board
2_36_30.ESV: There were eight frames with their bases of silver: sixteen bases, under every frame two bases.
2_36_30.NASB: There were eight boards with their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets, two under every board.
2_36_30.NLT: So there were eight frames at the rear of the Tabernacle, set in sixteen silver bases — two bases under each frame.
2_36_30.WEB: There were eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; under every board two sockets.
2_36_30.CSB17: So there were eight supports with their sixteen silver bases, two bases under each one.
2_36_30.AMP: There were eight boards with sixteen silver sockets, and under [the end of] each board two sockets.
2_36_30.MSG: —eight frames altogether with sixteen sockets of silver, two under each frame.
2_36_30.NIV: So there were eight frames and sixteen silver bases—two under each frame.
2_36_30.NRSVCE: There were eight frames with their bases of silver: sixteen bases, under every frame two bases.

2_36_31.NKJV: And he made bars of acacia wood: five for the boards on one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.KJV: And he made bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.ESV: He made bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.NASB: Then he made bars of acacia wood, five for the boards of one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.NLT: Then he made crossbars of acacia wood to link the frames, five crossbars for the north side of the Tabernacle
2_36_31.WEB: He made bars of acacia wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.CSB17: He made five crossbars of acacia wood for the supports on one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.AMP: Bezalel made bars of acacia wood, five for the [frame] boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.MSG: They made crossbars of acacia wood, five for the frames on one side of The Dwelling,
2_36_31.NIV: They also made crossbars of acacia wood: five for the frames on one side of the tabernacle,
2_36_31.NRSVCE: He made bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,

2_36_32.NKJV: five bars for the boards on the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle on the far side westward.
2_36_32.KJV: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the sides westward.
2_36_32.ESV: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
2_36_32.NASB: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the rear [side] to the west.
2_36_32.NLT: and five for the south side. He also made five crossbars for the rear of the Tabernacle, which faced west.
2_36_32.WEB: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the hinder part westward.
2_36_32.CSB17: five crossbars for the supports on the other side of the tabernacle, and five crossbars for those at the back of the tabernacle on the west.
2_36_32.AMP: and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle's other side, and five bars for the boards at the rear side to the west.
2_36_32.MSG: five for the other side, and five for the back side facing west.
2_36_32.NIV: five for those on the other side, and five for the frames on the west, at the far end of the tabernacle.
2_36_32.NRSVCE: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the tabernacle at the rear westward.

2_36_33.NKJV: And he made the middle bar to pass through the boards from one end to the other.
2_36_33.KJV: And he made the middle bar to shoot through the boards from the one end to the other.
2_36_33.ESV: And he made the middle bar to run from end to end halfway up the frames.
2_36_33.NASB: He made the middle bar to pass through in the center of the boards from end to end.
2_36_33.NLT: He made the middle crossbar to attach halfway up the frames; it ran all the way from one end of the Tabernacle to the other.
2_36_33.WEB: He made the middle bar to pass through in the middle of the boards from the one end to the other.
2_36_33.CSB17: He made the central crossbar run through the middle of the supports from one end to the other.
2_36_33.AMP: And he made the middle bar pass through [horizontally] halfway up the boards from one end to the other.
2_36_33.MSG: The center crossbar ran from end to end halfway up the frames.
2_36_33.NIV: They made the center crossbar so that it extended from end to end at the middle of the frames.
2_36_33.NRSVCE: He made the middle bar to pass through from end to end halfway up the frames.

2_36_34.NKJV: He overlaid the boards with gold, made their rings of gold to be holders for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
2_36_34.KJV: And he overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings of gold to be places for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
2_36_34.ESV: And he overlaid the frames with gold, and made their rings of gold for holders for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
2_36_34.NASB: He overlaid the boards with gold and made their rings of gold [as] holders for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
2_36_34.NLT: He overlaid the frames with gold and made gold rings to hold the crossbars. Then he overlaid the crossbars with gold as well.
2_36_34.WEB: He overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings of gold for places for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
2_36_34.CSB17: He overlaid them with gold and made their rings out of gold as holders for the crossbars. He also overlaid the crossbars with gold.
2_36_34.AMP: He overlaid the boards and the bars with gold and made their rings of gold as holders for the bars.
2_36_34.MSG: They covered the frames with a veneer of gold, made gold rings to hold the crossbars, and covered the crossbars with a veneer of gold.
2_36_34.NIV: They overlaid the frames with gold and made gold rings to hold the crossbars. They also overlaid the crossbars with gold.
2_36_34.NRSVCE: And he overlaid the frames with gold, and made rings of gold for them to hold the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.

2_36_35.NKJV: And he made a veil of blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine woven linen; it was worked with an artistic design of cherubim.
2_36_35.KJV: And he made a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet , and fine twined linen: with cherubims made he it of cunning work.
2_36_35.ESV: He made the veil of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen; with cherubim skillfully worked into it he made it.
2_36_35.NASB: Moreover, he made the veil of blue and purple and scarlet [material], and fine twisted linen; he made it with cherubim, the work of a skillful workman.
2_36_35.NLT: For the inside of the Tabernacle, Bezalel made a special curtain of finely woven linen. He decorated it with blue, purple, and scarlet thread and with skillfully embroidered cherubim.
2_36_35.WEB: He made the veil of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen: with cherubim. He made it the work of a skillful workman.
2_36_35.CSB17: Then he made the curtain with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen. He made it with a design of cherubim worked into it.
2_36_35.AMP: Further, Bezalel made the veil of blue and purple and scarlet fabric, and fine twisted linen; he made it with cherubim, the work of an embroiderer.
2_36_35.MSG: They made the curtain of blue, purple, and scarlet material and fine twisted linen. They wove a design of angel-cherubim into it.
2_36_35.NIV: They made the curtain of blue, purple and scarlet yarn and finely twisted linen, with cherubim worked into it by a skilled craftsman.
2_36_35.NRSVCE: He made the curtain of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine twisted linen, with cherubim skillfully worked into it.

2_36_36.NKJV: He made for it four pillars of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold, with their hooks of gold; and he cast four sockets of silver for them.
2_36_36.KJV: And he made thereunto four pillars of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold: their hooks were of gold; and he cast for them four sockets of silver.
2_36_36.ESV: And for it he made four pillars of acacia and overlaid them with gold. Their hooks were of gold, and he cast for them four bases of silver.
2_36_36.NASB: He made four pillars of acacia for it, and overlaid them with gold, with their hooks of gold; and he cast four sockets of silver for them.
2_36_36.NLT: For the curtain, he made four posts of acacia wood and four gold hooks. He overlaid the posts with gold and set them in four silver bases.
2_36_36.WEB: He made four pillars of acacia for it, and overlaid them with gold. Their hooks were of gold. He cast four sockets of silver for them.
2_36_36.CSB17: He made four pillars of acacia wood for it and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold. And he cast four silver bases for the pillars.
2_36_36.AMP: For the veil (partition curtain) he made four support poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were gold, and he cast for them four silver sockets.
2_36_36.MSG: They made four posts of acacia wood, covered them with a veneer of gold, and cast four silver bases for them.
2_36_36.NIV: They made four posts of acacia wood for it and overlaid them with gold. They made gold hooks for them and cast their four silver bases.
2_36_36.NRSVCE: For it he made four pillars of acacia, and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold, and he cast for them four bases of silver.

2_36_37.NKJV: He also made a screen for the tabernacle door, of blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine woven linen, made by a weaver,
2_36_37.KJV: And he made an hanging for the tabernacle door of blue, and purple, and scarlet , and fine twined linen, of needlework ; of needlework: Heb. the work of a needleworker or, embroiderer
2_36_37.ESV: He also made a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework,
2_36_37.NASB: He made a screen for the doorway of the tent, of blue and purple and scarlet [material], and fine twisted linen, the work of a weaver;
2_36_37.NLT: Then he made another curtain for the entrance to the sacred tent. He made it of finely woven linen and embroidered it with exquisite designs using blue, purple, and scarlet thread.
2_36_37.WEB: He made a screen for the door of the tent, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of an embroiderer;
2_36_37.CSB17: He made a screen embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen for the entrance to the tent,
2_36_37.AMP: He made a screen (curtain) for the doorway of the tent, of blue, purple, and scarlet fabric, and fine twisted linen, the work of an embroiderer;
2_36_37.MSG: They made a screen for the door of the tent, woven from blue, purple, and scarlet material and fine twisted linen with embroidery.
2_36_37.NIV: For the entrance to the tent they made a curtain of blue, purple and scarlet yarn and finely twisted linen—the work of an embroiderer;
2_36_37.NRSVCE: He also made a screen for the entrance to the tent, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework;

2_36_38.NKJV: and its five pillars with their hooks. And he overlaid their capitals and their rings with gold, but their five sockets were bronze.
2_36_38.KJV: And the five pillars of it with their hooks: and he overlaid their chapiters and their fillets with gold: but their five sockets were of brass.
2_36_38.ESV: and its five pillars with their hooks. He overlaid their capitals, and their fillets were of gold, but their five bases were of bronze.
2_36_38.NASB: and [he made] its five pillars with their hooks, and he overlaid their tops and their bands with gold; but their five sockets were of bronze.
2_36_38.NLT: This curtain was hung on gold hooks attached to five posts. The posts with their decorated tops and hooks were overlaid with gold, and the five bases were cast from bronze.

2_37_19.NASB: three cups shaped like almond [blossoms], a bulb and a flower in one branch, and three cups shaped like almond [blossoms], a bulb and a flower in the other branch-- so for the six branches going out of the lampstand.
2_37_19.NLT: Each of the six branches had three lamp cups shaped like almond blossoms, complete with buds and petals.

2_37_20.NKJV: And on the lampstand itself were four bowls made like almond blossoms, each with its ornamental knob and flower.

2_37_21.NLT: There was an almond bud beneath each pair of branches where the six branches extended from the center stem, all made of one piece.

2_37_22.ESV: Their calyxes and their branches were of one piece with it. The whole of it was a single piece of hammered work of pure gold.
2_37_22.NASB: Their bulbs and their branches were [of one piece] with it; the whole of it [was] a single hammered work of pure gold.
2_37_22.NLT: The almond buds and branches were all of one piece with the center stem, and they were hammered from pure gold.

2_37_22.NRSVCE: Their calyxes and their branches were of one piece with it, the whole of it one hammered piece of pure gold.

2_37_25.NKJV: He made the incense altar of acacia wood. Its length was a cubit and its width a cubit—it was square—and two cubits was its height. Its horns were of one piece with it.
2_37_25.KJV: And he made the incense altar of shittim wood: the length of it was a cubit, and the breadth of it a cubit; it was foursquare; and two cubits was the height of it; the horns thereof were of the same.

2_37_25.NASB: Then he made the altar of incense of acacia wood: a cubit long and a cubit wide, square, and two cubits high; its horns were [of one piece] with it.

2_38_1.NKJV: He made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood; five cubits was its length and five cubits its width—it was square—and its height was three cubits.

2_38_25.CSB17: The silver from those of the community who were registered was 7,544 pounds, according to the sanctuary shekel —
2_38_25.AMP: The silver from those of the congregation who were assembled and counted was 100 talents and 1,775 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary;
2_38_25.MSG: Silver. The silver from those in the community who were registered in the census came to 6,437 pounds according to the Sanctuary standard
2_38_25.NIV: The silver obtained from those of the community who were counted in the census was 100 talents and 1,775 shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel—
2_38_25.NRSVCE: The silver from those of the congregation who were counted was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels, measured by the sanctuary shekel;

2_38_26.NKJV: a bekah for each man (that is, half a shekel, according to the shekel of the sanctuary), for everyone included in the numbering from twenty years old and above, for six hundred and three thousand, five hundred and fifty men.
2_38_26.KJV: A bekah for every man, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men. every man: Heb. a poll
2_38_26.ESV: a beka a head (that is, half a shekel, by the shekel of the sanctuary), for everyone who was listed in the records, from twenty years old and upward, for 603,550 men.
2_38_26.NASB: a beka a head ([that is], half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary), for each one who passed over to those who were numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for 603,550 men.
2_38_26.NLT: This silver came from the tax collected from each man registered in the census. (The tax is one beka, which is half a shekel, based on the sanctuary shekel.) The tax was collected from 603,550 men who had reached their twentieth birthday.
2_38_26.WEB: a beka a head, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for everyone who passed over to those who were counted, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty men.
2_38_26.CSB17: two-fifths of an ounce per man, that is, half a shekel according to the sanctuary shekel, from everyone twenty years old or more who had crossed over to the registered group, 603,550 men.
2_38_26.AMP: a beka for each man (that is, half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary) for everyone who was counted, from twenty years old and upward, for 603,550 men.
2_38_26.MSG: —that amounted to a beka, or half-shekel, for every registered person aged twenty and over, a total of 603,550 men.
2_38_26.NIV: one beka per person, that is, half a shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, from everyone who had crossed over to those counted, twenty years old or more, a total of 603,550 men.
2_38_26.NRSVCE: a beka a head (that is, half a shekel, measured by the sanctuary shekel), for everyone who was counted in the census, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred three thousand, five hundred fifty men.

2_38_27.NKJV: And from the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil: one hundred sockets from the hundred talents, one talent for each socket.
2_38_27.KJV: And of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the vail; an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
2_38_27.ESV: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil; a hundred bases for the hundred talents, a talent a base.
2_38_27.NASB: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the sockets of the sanctuary and the sockets of the veil; one hundred sockets for the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
2_38_27.NLT: The hundred bases for the frames of the sanctuary walls and for the posts supporting the inner curtain required 7,500 pounds of silver, about 75 pounds for each base.
2_38_27.WEB: The one hundred talents of silver were for casting the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the veil; one hundred sockets for the one hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
2_38_27.CSB17: There were 7,500 pounds of silver used to cast the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the curtain — one hundred bases from 7,500 pounds, 75 pounds for each base.
2_38_27.AMP: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the sockets of the sanctuary and the sockets of the veil (partition curtain); a hundred sockets for the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
2_38_27.MSG: They used the three and one-quarter tons of silver to cast the bases for the Sanctuary and for the hangings, one hundred bases at sixty-four pounds each.
2_38_27.NIV: The 100 talents of silver were used to cast the bases for the sanctuary and for the curtain—100 bases from the 100 talents, one talent for each base.
2_38_27.NRSVCE: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary, and the bases of the curtain; one hundred bases for the hundred talents, a talent for a base.

2_38_28.NKJV: Then from the one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, overlaid their capitals, and made bands for them.
2_38_28.KJV: And of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them.
2_38_28.ESV: And of the 1,775 shekels he made hooks for the pillars and overlaid their capitals and made fillets for them.
2_38_28.NASB: Of the 1,775 [shekels], he made hooks for the pillars and overlaid their tops and made bands for them.
2_38_28.NLT: The remaining 45 pounds of silver was used to make the hooks and rings and to overlay the tops of the posts.
2_38_28.WEB: Of the one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, overlaid their capitals, and made fillets for them.
2_38_28.CSB17: With the remaining 44 pounds he made the hooks for the posts, overlaid their tops, and supplied bands for them.
2_38_28.AMP: Of the 1,775 shekels, he made hooks for the support poles and overlaid their tops and made connecting rings for them.
2_38_28.MSG: They used the remaining thirty-seven pounds to make the connecting hooks on the posts, and the caps and bands for the posts.
2_38_28.NIV: They used the 1,775 shekels to make the hooks for the posts, to overlay the tops of the posts, and to make their bands.
2_38_28.NRSVCE: Of the thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their capitals and made bands for them.

2_39_1.NKJV: Of the blue, purple, and scarlet thread they made garments of ministry, for ministering in the holy place, and made the holy garments for Aaron, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_39_1.KJV: And of the blue, and purple, and scarlet , they made cloths of service, to do service in the holy place, and made the holy garments for Aaron; as the LORD commanded Moses.
2_39_1.ESV: From the blue and purple and scarlet yarns they made finely woven garments, for ministering in the Holy Place. They made the holy garments for Aaron, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_39_1.NLT: The craftsmen made beautiful sacred garments of blue, purple, and scarlet cloth — clothing for Aaron to wear while ministering in the Holy Place, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_39_2.CSB17: Bezalel made the ephod of gold, of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and of finely spun linen.

2_39_2.NRSVCE: He made the ephod of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.

2_39_3.NKJV: And they beat the gold into thin sheets and cut it into threads, to work it in with the blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and the fine linen, into artistic designs.

2_39_3.NASB: Then they hammered out gold sheets and cut [them] into threads to be woven in [with] the blue and the purple and the scarlet [material], and the fine linen, the work of a skillful workman.

2_39_3.WEB: They beat the gold into thin plates, and cut it into wires, to work it in the blue, in the purple, in the scarlet, and in the fine linen, the work of the skillful workman.

2_39_3.AMP: Then Bezalel and Oholiab hammered the gold [into thin sheets] and cut it into threads to work into the blue, purple, and scarlet fabric, and into the fine linen, the work of a skilled craftsman.

2_39_3.NIV: They hammered out thin sheets of gold and cut strands to be worked into the blue, purple and scarlet yarn and fine linen—the work of a skilled craftsman.
2_39_3.NRSVCE: Gold leaf was hammered out and cut into threads to work into the blue, purple, and crimson yarns and into the fine twisted linen, in skilled design.

2_39_42.NKJV: According to all that the LORD had commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did all the work.

2_39_42.NLT: So the people of Israel followed all of the LORD’s instructions to Moses.
2_39_42.WEB: According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did all the work.
2_39_42.CSB17: The Israelites had done all the work according to everything the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_39_42.MSG: The Israelites completed all the work, just as God had commanded.
2_39_42.NIV: The Israelites had done all the work just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
2_39_42.NRSVCE: The Israelites had done all of the work just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_39_43.NKJV: Then Moses looked over all the work, and indeed they had done it; as the LORD had commanded, just so they had done it. And Moses blessed them.
2_39_43.KJV: And Moses did look upon all the work, and, behold, they had done it as the LORD had commanded, even so had they done it: and Moses blessed them.
2_39_43.ESV: And Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it; as the LORD had commanded, so had they done it. Then Moses blessed them.
2_39_43.NASB: And Moses examined all the work and behold, they had done it; just as the LORD had commanded, this they had done. So Moses blessed them.
2_39_43.NLT: Then Moses inspected all their work. When he found it had been done just as the LORD had commanded him, he blessed them.
2_39_43.WEB: Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it as Yahweh had commanded, even so had they done it: and Moses blessed them.
2_39_43.CSB17: Moses inspected all the work they had accomplished. They had done just as the LORD commanded. Then Moses blessed them.
2_39_43.AMP: And Moses [carefully] inspected all the work, and behold, they had done it; just as the LORD had commanded, so had they done it. So Moses blessed them.

2_39_43.NIV: Moses inspected the work and saw that they had done it just as the Lord had commanded. So Moses blessed them.
2_39_43.NRSVCE: When Moses saw that they had done all the work just as the LORD had commanded, he blessed them.

2_40_1.AMP: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,

2_40_2.NKJV: “On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

2_40_2.ESV: “On the first day of the first month you shall erect the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

2_40_2.NLT: “Set up the Tabernacle on the first day of the new year.

2_40_2.NIV: “Set up the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting, on the first day of the first month.

2_40_25.NIV: and set up the lamps before the Lord, as the Lord commanded him.
2_40_25.NRSVCE: and set up the lamps before the LORD; as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_40_26.NKJV: He put the gold altar in the tabernacle of meeting in front of the veil;
2_40_26.KJV: And he put the golden altar in the tent of the congregation before the vail:
2_40_26.ESV: He put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the veil,
2_40_26.NASB: Then he placed the gold altar in the tent of meeting in front of the veil;
2_40_26.NLT: He also placed the gold incense altar in the Tabernacle, in the Holy Place in front of the inner curtain.
2_40_26.WEB: He put the golden altar in the Tent of Meeting before the veil;
2_40_26.CSB17: Moses installed the gold altar in the tent of meeting, in front of the curtain,
2_40_26.AMP: He put the golden altar [of incense] in the Tent of Meeting in front of the veil;
2_40_26.MSG: Moses placed the Gold Altar in the Tent of Meeting in front of the curtain
2_40_26.NIV: Moses placed the gold altar in the Tent of Meeting in front of the curtain
2_40_26.NRSVCE: He put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the curtain,

2_40_27.NKJV: and he burned sweet incense on it, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_27.KJV: And he burnt sweet incense thereon; as the LORD commanded Moses.
2_40_27.ESV: and burned fragrant incense on it, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_27.NASB: and he burned fragrant incense on it, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_27.NLT: On it he burned the fragrant incense, just as the LORD had commanded him.
2_40_27.WEB: and he burned incense of sweet spices on it, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
2_40_27.CSB17: and burned fragrant incense on it, just as the LORD had commanded him.
2_40_27.AMP: he burned fragrant incense [as a symbol of prayer] on it, just as the LORD commanded him.
2_40_27.MSG: and burned fragrant incense on it, just as God had commanded him.
2_40_27.NIV: and burned fragrant incense on it, as the Lord commanded him.
2_40_27.NRSVCE: and offered fragrant incense on it; as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_40_28.NKJV: He hung up the screen at the door of the tabernacle.
2_40_28.KJV: And he set up the hanging at the door of the tabernacle.
2_40_28.ESV: He put in place the screen for the door of the tabernacle.
2_40_28.NASB: Then he set up the veil for the doorway of the tabernacle.
2_40_28.NLT: He hung the curtain at the entrance of the Tabernacle,
2_40_28.WEB: He put up the screen of the door to the tabernacle.
2_40_28.CSB17: He put up the screen at the entrance to the tabernacle.

2_40_28.MSG: He placed the screen at the entrance to The Dwelling.
2_40_28.NIV: Then he put up the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
2_40_28.NRSVCE: He also put in place the screen for the entrance of the tabernacle.

2_40_29.NKJV: And he put the altar of burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, and offered upon it the burnt offering and the grain offering, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_29.KJV: And he put the altar of burnt offering by the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation, and offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering; as the LORD commanded Moses.
2_40_29.ESV: And he set the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the grain offering, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_29.NASB: He set the altar of burnt offering [before] the doorway of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the meal offering, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_29.NLT: and he placed the altar of burnt offering near the Tabernacle entrance. On it he offered a burnt offering and a grain offering, just as the LORD had commanded him.
2_40_29.WEB: He set the altar of burnt offering at the door of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the meal offering, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
2_40_29.CSB17: He placed the altar of burnt offering at the entrance to the tabernacle, the tent of meeting, and offered the burnt offering and the grain offering on it, just as the LORD had commanded him.
2_40_29.AMP: He set the altar of burnt offering before the doorway of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the grain offering, just as the LORD commanded him.
2_40_29.MSG: He set the Altar of Whole-Burnt-Offering at the door of The Dwelling, the Tent of Meeting, and offered up the Whole-Burnt-Offerings and the Grain-Offerings, just as God had commanded Moses.
2_40_29.NIV: He set the altar of burnt offering near the entrance to the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting, and offered on it burnt offerings and grain offerings, as the Lord commanded him.
2_40_29.NRSVCE: He set the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the grain offering as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_40_30.NKJV: He set the laver between the tabernacle of meeting and the altar, and put water there for washing;
2_40_30.KJV: And he set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and put water there, to wash withal.
2_40_30.ESV: He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it for washing,
2_40_30.NASB: He placed the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
2_40_30.NLT: Next Moses placed the washbasin between the Tabernacle and the altar. He filled it with water so the priests could wash themselves.
2_40_30.WEB: He set the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and put water therein, with which to wash.
2_40_30.CSB17: He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
2_40_30.AMP: He placed the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar [of burnt offering], and put water in it for washing.
2_40_30.MSG: He placed the Washbasin between the Tent of Meeting and the Altar, and filled it with water for washing.
2_40_30.NIV: He placed the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing,
2_40_30.NRSVCE: He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it for washing,

2_40_31.NKJV: and Moses, Aaron, and his sons would wash their hands and their feet with water from it.
2_40_31.KJV: And Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet thereat:
2_40_31.ESV: with which Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
2_40_31.NASB: From it Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
2_40_31.NLT: Moses and Aaron and Aaron’s sons used water from it to wash their hands and feet.
2_40_31.WEB: Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and their feet there.
2_40_31.CSB17: Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and feet from it.
2_40_31.AMP: Then from it Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
2_40_31.MSG: Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and feet there.
2_40_31.NIV: and Moses and Aaron and his sons used it to wash their hands and feet.
2_40_31.NRSVCE: with which Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.

2_40_32.NKJV: Whenever they went into the tabernacle of meeting, and when they came near the altar, they washed, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_32.KJV: When they went into the tent of the congregation, and when they came near unto the altar, they washed; as the LORD commanded Moses.
2_40_32.ESV: When they went into the tent of meeting, and when they approached the altar, they washed, as the LORD commanded Moses.
2_40_32.NASB: When they entered the tent of meeting, and when they approached the altar, they washed, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_32.NLT: Whenever they approached the altar and entered the Tabernacle, they washed themselves, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_32.WEB: When they went into the Tent of Meeting, and when they came near to the altar, they washed, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
2_40_32.CSB17: They washed whenever they came to the tent of meeting and approached the altar, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_40_32.AMP: When they entered the Tent of Meeting, and when they approached the altar, they washed, just as the LORD commanded Moses.
2_40_32.MSG: When they entered the Tent of Meeting and when they served at the Altar, they washed, just as God had commanded Moses.
2_40_32.NIV: They washed whenever they entered the Tent of Meeting or approached the altar, as the Lord commanded Moses.
2_40_32.NRSVCE: When they went into the tent of meeting, and when they approached the altar, they washed; as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2_40_33.NKJV: And he raised up the court all around the tabernacle and the altar, and hung up the screen of the court gate. So Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.KJV: And he reared up the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the hanging of the court gate. So Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.ESV: And he erected the court around the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the screen of the gate of the court. So Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.NASB: He erected the court all around the tabernacle and the altar, and hung up the veil for the gateway of the court. Thus Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.NLT: Then he hung the curtains forming the courtyard around the Tabernacle and the altar. And he set up the curtain at the entrance of the courtyard. So at last Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.WEB: He raised up the court around the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the screen of the gate of the court. So Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.CSB17: Next Moses set up the surrounding courtyard for the tabernacle and the altar and hung a screen for the gate of the courtyard. So Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.AMP: And he erected the courtyard all around the tabernacle and the altar, and hung the screen (curtain) at the gateway of the courtyard. So Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.MSG: Finally, he erected the Courtyard all around The Dwelling and the Altar, and put up the screen for the Courtyard entrance.Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.NIV: Then Moses set up the courtyard around the tabernacle and altar and put up the curtain at the entrance to the courtyard. And so Moses finished the work.
2_40_33.NRSVCE: He set up the court around the tabernacle and the altar, and put up the screen at the gate of the court. So Moses finished the work.

2_40_34.NKJV: Then the cloud covered the tabernacle of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.KJV: Then a cloud covered the tent of the congregation, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.ESV: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.NASB: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.NLT: Then the cloud covered the Tabernacle, and the glory of the LORD filled the Tabernacle.
2_40_34.WEB: Then the cloud covered the Tent of Meeting, and Yahweh’s glory filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.CSB17: The cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.AMP: Then the cloud [the Shekinah, God's visible, dwelling presence] covered the Tent of Meeting, and the glory and brilliance of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.MSG: The Cloud covered the Tent of Meeting, and the Glory of God filled The Dwelling.
2_40_34.NIV: The Glory of the LordThen the cloud covered the Tent of Meeting, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
2_40_34.NRSVCE: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.

2_40_35.NKJV: And Moses was not able to enter the tabernacle of meeting, because the cloud rested above it, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.KJV: And Moses was not able to enter into the tent of the congregation, because the cloud abode thereon, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.ESV: And Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled on it, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.NASB: Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud had settled on it, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.NLT: Moses could no longer enter the Tabernacle because the cloud had settled down over it, and the glory of the LORD filled the Tabernacle.
2_40_35.WEB: Moses wasn’t able to enter into the Tent of Meeting, because the cloud stayed on it, and Yahweh’s glory filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.CSB17: Moses was unable to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud rested on it, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.AMP: Moses was not able to enter the Tent of Meeting because the cloud remained on it, and the glory and brilliance of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.MSG: Moses couldn't enter the Tent of Meeting because the Cloud was upon it, and the Glory of God filled The Dwelling.
2_40_35.NIV: Moses could not enter the Tent of Meeting because the cloud had settled upon it, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
2_40_35.NRSVCE: Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled upon it, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.

2_40_36.NKJV: Whenever the cloud was taken up from above the tabernacle, the children of Israel would go onward in all their journeys.
2_40_36.KJV: And when the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel went onward in all their journeys: went onward: Heb. journeyed
2_40_36.ESV: Throughout all their journeys, whenever the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the people of Israel would set out.
2_40_36.NASB: Throughout all their journeys whenever the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the sons of Israel would set out;
2_40_36.NLT: Now whenever the cloud lifted from the Tabernacle, the people of Israel would set out on their journey, following it.
2_40_36.WEB: When the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel went onward, throughout all their journeys;
2_40_36.CSB17: The Israelites set out whenever the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle throughout all the stages of their journey.
2_40_36.AMP: In all their journeys, whenever the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the Israelites would set out;
2_40_36.MSG: Whenever the Cloud lifted from The Dwelling, the People of Israel set out on their travels,
2_40_36.NIV: In all the travels of the Israelites, whenever the cloud lifted from above the tabernacle, they would set out;
2_40_36.NRSVCE: Whenever the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle, the Israelites would set out on each stage of their journey;

2_40_37.NKJV: But if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not journey till the day that it was taken up.
2_40_37.KJV: But if the cloud were not taken up, then they journeyed not till the day that it was taken up.
2_40_37.ESV: But if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not set out till the day that it was taken up.
2_40_37.NASB: but if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not set out until the day when it was taken up.
2_40_37.NLT: But if the cloud did not rise, they remained where they were until it lifted.
2_40_37.WEB: but if the cloud wasn’t taken up, then they didn’t travel until the day that it was taken up.
2_40_37.CSB17: If the cloud was not taken up, they did not set out until the day it was taken up.
2_40_37.AMP: but if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not journey on until the day when it was taken up.
2_40_37.MSG: but if the Cloud did not lift, they wouldn't set out until it did lift.
2_40_37.NIV: but if the cloud did not lift, they did not set out—until the day it lifted.
2_40_37.NRSVCE: but if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not set out until the day that it was taken up.

2_40_38.NKJV: For the cloud of the LORD was above the tabernacle by day, and fire was over it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.
2_40_38.KJV: For the cloud of the LORD was upon the tabernacle by day, and fire was on it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.
2_40_38.ESV: For the cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day, and fire was in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel throughout all their journeys.
2_40_38.NASB: For throughout all their journeys, the cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day, and there was fire in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel.
2_40_38.NLT: The cloud of the LORD hovered over the Tabernacle during the day, and at night fire glowed inside the cloud so the whole family of Israel could see it. This continued throughout all their journeys.
2_40_38.WEB: For the cloud of Yahweh was on the tabernacle by day, and there was fire in the cloud by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.
2_40_38.CSB17: For the cloud of the LORD was over the tabernacle by day, and there was a fire inside the cloud by night, visible to the entire house of Israel throughout all the stages of their journey.
2_40_38.AMP: For throughout all their journeys, the cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day, and there was fire in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel.
2_40_38.MSG: The Cloud of God was over The Dwelling during the day and the fire was in it at night, visible to all the Israelites in all their travels.
2_40_38.NIV: So the cloud of the Lord was over the tabernacle by day, and fire was in the cloud by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel during all their travels.
2_40_38.NRSVCE: For the cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day, and fire was in the cloud by night, before the eyes of all the house of Israel at each stage of their journey.
